Title: Introduction to Supabase
Summary: # Introduction to Supabase Supabase is a Postgres development platform that provides a set of features similar to Firebase using enterprise-grade open source tools. | ## Key Features * Hosted Postgres Database * Authentication and Authorization * Auto-generated APIs (REST and GraphQL) * Realtime subscriptions * Functions (Database and Edge) * File Storage * AI + Vector/Embeddings Toolkit * Dashboard | ## Getting Started To get started with Supabase, visit the [official documentation](https://supabase.com/docs) for detailed guides and tutorials. | ## Community and Support Supabase has a strong community and support system, including: * Community Forum: best for discussing database best practices and getting help with building applications * GitHub Issues: best for reporting bugs and errors * Email Support: best for problems with your database or infrastructure * Discord: best for sharing applications and hanging out with the community | ## How it Works Supabase is a combination of open source tools, including: * Postgres: an object-relational database system * Realtime: an Elixir server for listening to PostgreSQL changes * PostgREST: a web server that turns your PostgreSQL database into a RESTful API * GoTrue: a JWT-based authentication API * Storage: a RESTful API for managing files in S3 * pg_graphql: a PostgreSQL extension that exposes a GraphQL API * postgres-meta: a RESTful API for managing your Postgres database * Kong: a cloud-native API gateway | ## Client Libraries Supabase provides client libraries for various languages, including: ### Official Clients | Language | Client | | --- | --- | | JavaScript (TypeScript) | supabase-js | | Flutter | supabase-flutter | | Swift | supabase-swift | | Python | supabase-py | | ### Community Clients | Language | Client | | --- | --- | | C# | supabase-csharp | | Go | postgrest-go | | Java | gotrue-java | | Kotlin | supabase-kt | | Ruby | supabase-rb | | Rust | postgrest-rs | | Godot Engine (GDScript) | supabase-gdscript | | ## Translations Supabase is available in multiple languages, including: * Arabic * Albanian * Bangla * Bulgarian * Catalan * Croatian * Czech * Danish * Dutch * English * Estonian * Finnish * French * German * Greek * Gujarati * Hebrew * Hindi * Hungarian * Nepali * Indonesian * Italian * Japanese * Korean * Lithuanian * Latvian * Malay * Norwegian (Bokmål) * Persian * Polish * Portuguese * Portuguese (Brazilian) * Romanian * Russian * Serbian * Sinhala * Slovak * Slovenian * Spanish * Simplified Chinese * Swedish * Thai * Traditional Chinese * Turkish * Ukrainian * Vietnamese

Title: Claude-Flow: Enterprise-Grade AI Orchestration Platform

Summary: # Claude-Flow: Enterprise-Grade AI Orchestration Platform | ## Overview Claude-Flow is an advanced AI orchestration platform tailored for developers and teams integrating AI into their workflows, with a particular focus on Anthropic’s Claude models. It facilitates the coordination of multi-agent AI swarms to automate various development processes, such as code review, repository management, and more. The platform integrates seamlessly with GitHub and offers advanced memory and persistence features. | ## Core Features | - **Multi-Agent Swarm Coordination**: Orchestrates specialized AI agents (e.g., code review, security scanning, PR management) to perform collaborative, automated tasks. - **Hive-Mind Intelligence**: Leverages neural pattern recognition and swarm intelligence to enhance decision-making and workflow automation. - **87 MCP Tools**: A comprehensive suite for memory, orchestration, automation, and advanced programming tasks. - **GitHub Integration**: Automates repository management, code review, pull request enhancement, and workflow creation with 13 specialized agents and custom GitHub Actions. - **Persistence & Memory**: Maintains project context, tracks decisions, and learns from previous coordination patterns using a SQLite-based memory system with cross-session persistence. - **Templates & Configuration**: Provides configurable **CLAUDE.md** templates for various project types, methodologies, architectures, and team sizes. - **Benchmarking & Reporting**: Includes tools for performance testing, comparative analysis, and automated reporting for CI/CD pipelines. | ## Main Category AI Development and Orchestration Platform, specifically for AI-powered development workflows and automated code/project management. | ## Key Use Cases and Target Audience | ### Use Cases | - **Automated Code Review & CI/CD**: Automate and enhance code review, testing, and deployment pipelines for improved workflow. - **AI-Powered Development Workflows**: Integrate intelligent, swarm-based assistance for web, mobile, API, or AI/ML projects. - **Repository Management**: Automate pull requests, issues, and repository analysis for GitHub users. - **Enterprise AI Orchestration**: Facilitate scalable, fault-tolerant, and persistent AI coordination for large-scale software projects. | ### Target Audience | - **Software Teams**: Integrate AI into the development lifecycle for higher efficiency and automated quality control. - **Enterprises**: Aim for intelligent project management and seamless AI integration. | | Feature | Description | |----------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | Multi-Agent Swarm Coordination | Orchestrates AI agents for collaborative tasks like code review, security scanning. | | Hive-Mind Intelligence | Enhances decision-making with neural pattern and swarm intelligence. | | 87 MCP Tools | Suite for memory, orchestration, automation, and programming tasks. | | GitHub Integration | Automates repository management, enhances pull requests, and creates workflows. | | Persistence & Memory | Maintains project context and learns from coordination patterns. | | Templates & Configuration | Configurable templates for various project setups and team sizes. | | Benchmarking & Reporting | Performance testing, comparative analysis, and CI/CD reporting tools. |

### Introduction to Self-Hosted AI Starter Kit The Self-Hosted AI Starter Kit is an open-source Docker Compose template designed to initialize a comprehensive local AI and low-code development environment. It combines the self-hosted n8n platform with compatible AI products and components for building self-hosted AI workflows. | ### What's Included * **Self-hosted n8n**: Low-code platform with over 400 integrations and advanced AI components * **Ollama**: Cross-platform LLM platform for installing and running local LLMs * **Qdrant**: Open-source, high-performance vector store with a comprehensive API * **PostgreSQL**: Handles large amounts of data safely | ### What You Can Build * **AI Agents**: For scheduling appointments * **Summarize Company PDFs**: Securely without data leaks * **Smarter Slack Bots**: For enhanced company communications and IT operations * **Private Financial Document Analysis**: At minimal cost | ### Installation To install the Self-Hosted AI Starter Kit, follow these steps: 1. Clone the repository: `git clone https://github.com/n8n-io/self-hosted-ai-starter-kit.git` 2. Change directory: `cd self-hosted-ai-starter-kit` 3. Copy example environment file: `cp .env.example .env` 4. Update secrets and passwords inside the `.env` file 5. Run the kit using Docker Compose: 	+ For Nvidia GPU users: `docker compose --profile gpu-nvidia up` 	+ For AMD GPU users on Linux: `docker compose --profile gpu-amd up` 	+ For Mac / Apple Silicon users: `docker compose up` 	+ For everyone else: `docker compose --profile cpu up` | ### Quick Start and Usage 1. Open `http://localhost:5678/` in your browser to set up n8n 2. Open the included workflow: `http://localhost:5678/workflow/srOnR8PAY3u4RSwb` 3. Click the **Chat** button at the bottom of the canvas to start running the workflow 4. To open n8n at any time, visit `http://localhost:5678/` in your browser | ### Upgrading To upgrade the Self-Hosted AI Starter Kit, follow these steps: 1. For Nvidia GPU setups: `docker compose --profile gpu-nvidia pull` and `docker compose --profile gpu-nvidia up` 2. For Mac / Apple Silicon users: `docker compose pull` and `docker compose up` 3. For Non-GPU setups: `docker compose --profile cpu pull` and `docker compose --profile cpu up` | ### Recommended Reading * n8n documentation for getting started with AI concepts and nodes * Support resources for troubleshooting and community engagement | ### Local AI Templates Some examples of local AI templates include: * Tax Code Assistant * Breakdown Documents into Study Notes with MistralAI and Qdrant * Financial Documents Assistant using Qdrant and Mistral.ai * Recipe Recommendations with Qdrant and Mistral | ### Tips & Tricks * Accessing local files: The self-hosted AI starter kit creates a shared folder mounted to the n8n container, allowing n8n to access files on disk * Nodes that interact with the local filesystem: Read/Write Files from Disk, Local File Trigger, Execute Command | ### License and Support * The project is licensed under the Apache License 2.0 * Join the conversation in the n8n Forum for community support, sharing your work, asking questions, and proposing ideas.

Title: Self-Hosted AI Package
Summary: # Self-Hosted AI Package The Self-Hosted AI Package is an open, docker compose template that quickly bootstraps a fully featured Local AI and Low Code development environment. | ## Included Components * Self-hosted n8n: Low-code platform with over 400 integrations and advanced AI components * Supabase: Open source database as a service * Ollama: Cross-platform LLM platform to install and run the latest local LLMs * Open WebUI: ChatGPT-like interface to privately interact with your local models and N8N agents * Flowise: No/low code AI agent builder that pairs well with n8n * Qdrant: Open source, high performance vector store with a comprehensive API * Neo4j: Knowledge graph engine that powers tools like GraphRAG, LightRAG, and Graphiti * SearXNG: Open source, free internet metasearch engine * Caddy: Managed HTTPS/TLS for custom domains * Langfuse: Open source LLM engineering platform for agent observability | ## Prerequisites * Python * Git/GitHub Desktop * Docker/Docker Desktop | ## Installation 1. Clone the repository and navigate to the project directory: `git clone -b stable https://github.com/coleam00/local-ai-packaged.git` 2. Set up environment variables for Supabase 3. Make a copy of `.env.example` and rename it to `.env` in the root directory of the project 4. Set required environment variables: 	+ N8N Configuration: `N8N_ENCRYPTION_KEY`, `N8N_USER_MANAGEMENT_JWT_SECRET` 	+ Supabase Secrets: `POSTGRES_PASSWORD`, `JWT_SECRET`, `ANON_KEY`, `SERVICE_ROLE_KEY`, `DASHBOARD_USERNAME`, `DASHBOARD_PASSWORD`, `POOLER_TENANT_ID` 	+ Neo4j Secrets: `NEO4J_AUTH` 	+ Langfuse credentials: `CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD`, `MINIO_ROOT_PASSWORD`, `LANGFUSE_SALT`, `NEXTAUTH_SECRET`, `ENCRYPTION_KEY` | ## Running the Services * Use the `start_services.py` script to start the services * Specify the GPU configuration using the `--profile` flag: `python start_services.py --profile gpu-nvidia` * For Mac/Apple Silicon users, use the `--profile cpu` flag or run Ollama on your Mac and connect to it from the n8n instance | ## Deploying to the Cloud 1. Prerequisites: Linux machine with Nano, Git, and Docker installed 2. Run the commands to open up the necessary ports: `ufw enable`, `ufw allow 80 && ufw allow 443`, `ufw reload` 3. Run the `start-services.py` script with the `--environment public` flag: `python3 start_services.py --profile gpu-nvidia --environment public` 4. Set up A records for your DNS provider to point to the IP address of your cloud instance | ## Quick Start and Usage 1. Open `http://localhost:5678/` in your browser to set up n8n 2. Open the included workflow: `http://localhost:5678/workflow/vTN9y2dLXqTiDfPT` 3. Create credentials for every service 4. Select the "Test workflow" button to start running the workflow 5. Make sure to toggle the workflow as active and copy the "Production" webhook URL | ## Upgrading 1. Stop all services: `docker compose -p localai -f docker-compose.yml --profile <your-profile> down` 2. Pull latest versions of all containers: `docker compose -p localai -f docker-compose.yml --profile <your-profile> pull` 3. Start services again with your desired profile: `python start_services.py --profile <your-profile>` | ## Troubleshooting * Supabase Issues: 	+ Supabase Pooler Restarting: follow the instructions in the GitHub issue 	+ Supabase Analytics Startup Failure: delete the folder `supabase/docker/volumes/db/data` * SearXNG Restarting: run the command `chmod 755 searxng` within the local-ai-packaged folder * Files not Found in Supabase Folder: delete the supabase/ folder within the Local AI Package folder entirely and try again * GPU Support Issues: 	+ Windows GPU Support: enable WSL 2 backend in Docker Desktop settings 	+ Linux GPU Support: follow the Ollama Docker instructions | ## Recommended Reading * n8n documentation: full of useful content for getting started quickly with its AI concepts and nodes * AI agents for developers: from theory to practice with n8n * Tutorial: Build an AI workflow in n8n * Langchain Concepts in n8n * Demonstration of key differences between agents and chains * What are vector databases? | ## License This project is licensed under the Apache License 2.0 - see the LICENSE file for details.

Title: FastKoko
Summary: ### Introduction FastKoko is a Dockerized FastAPI wrapper for the Kokoro-82M text-to-speech model. It supports multiple languages, including English, Japanese, Chinese, and Vietnamese. | ### Features * OpenAI-compatible Speech endpoint * NVIDIA GPU accelerated or CPU inference with PyTorch * ONNX support (coming soon) * Debug endpoints for monitoring system stats * Integrated web UI on localhost:8880/web * Phoneme-based audio generation * Per-word timestamped caption generation * Voice mixing with weighted combinations | ### Getting Started #### Quickest Start (docker run) To quickly start using FastKoko, you can use the pre-built images available on GitHub. Run the following command: ```bash docker run -p 8880:8880 ghcr.io/remsky/kokoro-fastapi-cpu:latest ``` For NVIDIA GPU support, use: ```bash docker run --gpus all -p 8880:8880 ghcr.io/remsky/kokoro-fastapi-gpu:latest ``` #### Quick Start (docker compose) To start the service using Docker Compose, follow these steps: 1. Install Docker and clone the repository: ```bash git clone https://github.com/remsky/Kokoro-FastAPI.git cd Kokoro-FastAPI ``` 2. Navigate to the desired directory (CPU or GPU support): ```bash cd docker/cpu ``` or ```bash cd docker/gpu ``` 3. Start the service: ```bash docker compose up --build ``` ### Integration Guides #### Running Locally To run FastKoko locally as an OpenAI-compatible Speech endpoint, use the following Python code: ```python from openai import OpenAI client = OpenAI(base_url="http://localhost:8880/v1", api_key="not-needed") response = client.audio.speech.create(model="kokoro", voice="af_bella", input="Hello world!") response.stream_to_file("output.mp3") ``` Alternatively, you can use the `requests` library: ```python import requests response = requests.post("http://localhost:8880/v1/audio/speech", json={"model": "kokoro", "input": "Hello world!", "voice": "af_bella", "response_format": "mp3"}) with open("output.mp3", "wb") as f: f.write(response.content) ``` ### API Documentation The API documentation is available at `http://localhost:8880/docs`. The web interface can be accessed at `http://localhost:8880/web`. | ### Voice Combination FastKoko supports weighted voice combinations using ratios (e.g., "af_bella(2)+af_heart(1)" for 67%/33% mix). You can use the following Python code to combine voices and generate audio: ```python import requests response = requests.post("http://localhost:8880/v1/audio/speech", json={"input": "Hello world!", "voice": "af_bella+af_sky", "response_format": "mp3"}) ``` ### Multiple Output Audio Formats FastKoko supports the following output audio formats: * mp3 * wav * opus * flac * m4a * pcm | ### Streaming Support FastKoko supports OpenAI-compatible streaming. You can use the following Python code to stream audio: ```python from openai import OpenAI client = OpenAI(base_url="http://localhost:8880/v1", api_key="not-needed") with client.audio.speech.with_streaming_response.create(model="kokoro", voice="af_bella", input="Hello world!") as response: response.stream_to_file("output.mp3") ``` ### Timestamped Captions & Phonemes FastKoko supports generating audio with word-level timestamps without streaming. You can use the following Python code: ```python import requests response = requests.post("http://localhost:8880/dev/captioned_speech", json={"model": "kokoro", "input": "Hello world!", "voice": "af_bella", "speed": 1.0, "response_format": "mp3", "stream": False}) with open("output.mp3", "wb") as f: audio_json = json.loads(response.content) chunk_audio = base64.b64decode(audio_json["audio"].encode("utf-8")) f.write(chunk_audio) print(audio_json["timestamps"]) ``` ### Phoneme & Token Routes FastKoko supports converting text to phonemes and generating audio directly from phonemes. You can use the following Python code: ```python import requests def get_phonemes(text: str, language: str = "a"): response = requests.post("http://localhost:8880/dev/phonemize", json={"text": text, "language": language}) result = response.json() return result["phonemes"], result["tokens"] def generate_audio_from_phonemes(phonemes: str, voice: str = "af_bella"): response = requests.post("http://localhost:8880/dev/generate_from_phonemes", json={"phonemes": phonemes, "voice": voice}, headers={"Accept": "audio/wav"}) return response.content ``` ### Debug Endpoints FastKoko provides the following debug endpoints: * `/debug/threads`: Get thread information and stack traces * `/debug/storage`: Monitor temp file and output directory usage * `/debug/system`: Get system information (CPU, memory, GPU) * `/debug/session_pools`: View ONNX session and CUDA stream status | ### Known Issues & Troubleshooting * Missing words & Missing some timestamps: The API will automatically do text normalization on input text, which may incorrectly remove or change some phrases. This can be disabled by adding `"normalization_options":{"normalize": false}` to your request JSON. | ### Versioning & Development * Branching Strategy: The `release` branch contains the latest stable build, while the `master` branch is used for active development. * Note: This is a development-focused project, and you may need to roll back a version or build from source if you encounter issues. | ### Model and License * Model: FastKoko uses the Kokoro-82M model from HuggingFace. * License: The project is licensed under the Apache License 2.0. The Kokoro model weights are licensed under Apache 2.0, and the FastAPI wrapper code is licensed under Apache 2.0 to match. The inference code adapted from StyleTTS2 is MIT licensed.

Title: Xreal "Project Aura" (2026) Fact Sheet

Summary: # Xreal "Project Aura" (2026) Fact Sheet | ## Overview | Xreal’s “Project Aura” is a pair of lightweight, tethered augmented reality (AR) smart glasses. Announced as the second officially recognized Android XR device, it is a collaboration between Xreal, Google, and Qualcomm. Positioned as a “cinematic and AI-powered device,” Aura is designed to overlay digital information onto the real world, offering both optical see-through (OST) and virtual see-through (VST) experiences. It is scheduled for availability in 2026. | ## Key Features | - **Field of View (FoV):** 70+ degrees, providing an immersive and unconstrained peripheral vision. - **Dual-chip Design:** Combines Xreal’s proprietary X1S chipset with a Qualcomm Snapdragon XR chipset, optimized for spatial computing. - **Android XR Ecosystem:** Deeply integrated with Google’s Gemini AI, enabling features like on-the-fly translation, contextual search, and heads-up navigation. - **Tethered Experience:** Requires a wired “puck-like” device to power Android XR applications. - **Developer-friendly:** Facilitates the porting of existing Android XR apps, encouraging third-party innovation. - **Early Access:** Users can subscribe for updates and early access opportunities. | ## Main Categories | - Augmented Reality (AR) Smart Glasses - Extended Reality (XR) Wearable | ## Target Audience and Use Cases | ### Target Audience | - **Tech Enthusiasts and Early Adopters:** Interested in next-generation wearable computing and spatial experiences. - **Developers:** Encouraged to develop and port Android XR applications for the new AR glasses form factor. | ### Use Cases | - **Everyday Productivity and Entertainment:** Navigation, real-time translation, contextual information overlay, and cinematic viewing experiences. - **Enterprise and Spatial Computing:** Business and professional users exploring AR for workplace applications, although specific enterprise features are not yet detailed. | ## Availability | Project Aura is expected to launch in 2026, with early access opportunities in the United States and other markets for subscribers.

Title: Cloud Document Management Features
Summary: # Cloud Document Management Features | ## Overview Cloud document management and cloud document services enable users to store, edit, share, and collaborate on documents online. These services provide access from any internet-connected device and automatically sync changes, enhancing flexibility and collaboration. | ## Key Features | - **Accessibility and Syncing** - Access files from any device via the internet - Automatic sync and autosave | - **Version Control** - Version history support - Ability to restore previous versions of documents | - **Collaboration** - Real-time collaboration and sharing across users and teams | - **Organization** - Metadata tagging for efficient search and retrieval | - **Integration** - Integration with various applications for cross-application usage - Workflow automation and rights management | - **Security** - Secure storage solutions | - **Digitization** - Scanning and digitizing paper documents using OCR and eForms technologies | ## Main Categories | - Cloud-based document management and collaboration platforms - Broader cloud service ecosystems | ## Target Audience and Use Cases | | Audience | Use Cases | |----------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | Creative professionals and teams | Seamless cross-device access and collaboration on multimedia documents (e.g., Adobe Cloud Documents) | | Businesses and enterprises | Secure document storage, automated workflow solutions, compliance management, and efficient document retrieval | | IT and cloud architecture teams | Visualizing, managing, and documenting cloud infrastructure for operations and compliance | | Developers and organizations | Deploying applications with integrated document lifecycle management on cloud platforms | | These features support increased productivity, collaboration, governance, and operational efficiency in both creative and business contexts.

Title: Introduction to AgenticSeek
Summary: # Introduction to AgenticSeek AgenticSeek is a 100% local alternative to Manus AI, providing a voice-enabled AI assistant that autonomously browses the web, writes code, and plans tasks while keeping all data on your device. | ## Key Features * Fully Local & Private: Everything runs on your machine, ensuring complete privacy and zero cloud dependency. * Smart Web Browsing: AgenticSeek can browse the internet by itself, search, read, extract info, and fill web forms, all hands-free. * Autonomous Coding Assistant: It can write, debug, and run programs in Python, C, Go, Java, and more, all without supervision. * Smart Agent Selection: You ask, it figures out the best agent for the job automatically, like having a team of experts ready to help. * Plans & Executes Complex Tasks: From trip planning to complex projects, it can split big tasks into steps and get things done using multiple AI agents. * Voice-Enabled: Clean, fast, futuristic voice and speech to text, allowing you to talk to it like it's your personal AI from a sci-fi movie. | ## Prerequisites Before you begin, ensure you have the following software installed: * Git: For cloning the repository. * Python 3.10.x: We strongly recommend using Python version 3.10.x. * Docker Engine & Docker Compose: For running bundled services like SearxNG. | ## Setup ### Clone the Repository and Setup 1. Clone the repository: `git clone https://github.com/Fosowl/agenticSeek.git` 2. Change into the repository directory: `cd agenticSeek` 3. Copy the example environment file: `mv .env.example .env` 4. Update the `.env` file with your own values as needed. | ### Start Docker Make sure Docker is installed and running on your system. | ### List of Local Providers | Provider | Local? | Description | | --- | --- | --- | | ollama | Yes | Run LLMs locally with ease using ollama as a LLM provider | | lm-studio | Yes | Run LLM locally with LM studio | | openai | Yes | Use openai compatible API | | ### Setup to Run with an API 1. Choose an API Provider and Get an API Key. 2. Set Your API Key as an Environment Variable. 3. Update `config.ini`. | ### List of API Providers | Provider | provider_name | Local? | Description | API Key Link | | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | | OpenAI | openai | No | Use ChatGPT models via OpenAI's API | platform.openai.com/signup | | Google Gemini | google | No | Use Google Gemini models via Google AI Studio | aistudio.google.com/keys | | Deepseek | deepseek | No | Use Deepseek models via their API | platform.deepseek.com | | Hugging Face | huggingface | No | Use models from Hugging Face Inference API | huggingface.co/settings/tokens | | TogetherAI | togetherAI | No | Use various open-source models via TogetherAI API | api.together.ai/settings/api-keys | | ## Config The `config.ini` file contains the following settings: * `[MAIN]`: Main configuration section. 	+ `is_local`: True if using a local LLM provider, False if using a cloud-based API. 	+ `provider_name`: Specifies the LLM provider. 	+ `provider_model`: The specific model name or ID for the chosen provider. 	+ `provider_server_address`: The address of your LLM provider. 	+ `agent_name`: Name of the AI assistant. 	+ `recover_last_session`: True to attempt to restore the previous session's state, False to start fresh. 	+ `save_session`: True to save the current session's state for potential recovery, False otherwise. 	+ `speak`: True to enable text-to-speech voice output, False otherwise. 	+ `listen`: True to enable speech-to-text input, False otherwise. * `[BROWSER]`: Browser configuration section. 	+ `headless_browser`: True to use a headless browser, False otherwise. 	+ `stealth_mode`: True to use undetected selenium, False otherwise. | ## Speech to Text Warning: Speech to text only works in CLI mode at the moment. Please note that currently speech to text only works in English. | ## Start Services and Run AgenticSeek By default, AgenticSeek is run fully in Docker. To start services and run AgenticSeek, use the following command: `./start_services.sh full` (or `start start_services.cmd full` on Windows).

Title: Claude Code Hooks Mastery
Summary: # Claude Code Hooks Mastery ## Introduction Claude Code hooks provide deterministic control over Claude Code behavior without relying on LLM decisions. This mastery guide covers the usage of Claude Code hooks, including hook lifecycle events, payloads, and best practices for flow control. | ## Prerequisites * Astral UV: Fast Python package installer and resolver * Claude Code: Anthropic's CLI for Claude AI * Optional: 	+ ElevenLabs: Text-to-speech provider (with MCP server integration) 	+ ElevenLabs MCP Server: MCP server for ElevenLabs 	+ Firecrawl MCP Server: Web scraping and crawling MCP server 	+ OpenAI: Language model provider + Text-to-speech provider 	+ Anthropic: Language model provider 	+ Ollama: Local language model provider | ## Hook Lifecycle & Payloads The following table lists the 8 Claude Code hook lifecycle events with their JSON payloads: | | Hook Event | Fires | Payload | | --- | --- | --- | | UserPromptSubmit Hook | Immediately when user submits a prompt | prompt, text, session_id, timestamp | | PreToolUse Hook | Before any tool execution | tool_name, tool_input parameters | | PostToolUse Hook | After successful tool completion | tool_name, tool_input, tool_response with results | | Notification Hook | When Claude Code sends notifications | message content | | Stop Hook | When Claude Code finishes responding | stop_hook_active boolean flag | | SubagentStop Hook | When Claude Code subagents finish responding | stop_hook_active boolean flag | | PreCompact Hook | Before Claude Code performs a compaction operation | trigger, custom_instructions, session info | | SessionStart Hook | When Claude Code starts a new session or resumes an existing one | source, session info | | ## Key Features * Prompt validation and security filtering * Context injection for enhanced AI responses * Command logging and auditing * Automatic transcript conversion * Permission-based tool access control * Error handling in hook execution | ## Key Files * `.claude/settings.json`: Hook configuration with permissions * `.claude/hooks/`: Python scripts using UV for each hook type * `utils/`: Intelligent TTS and LLM utility scripts * `tts/`: Text-to-speech providers (ElevenLabs, OpenAI, pyttsx3) * `llm/`: Language model integrations (OpenAI, Anthropic, Ollama) * `logs/`: JSON logs of all hook executions | ## UV Single-File Scripts Architecture This project leverages UV single-file scripts to keep hook logic cleanly separated from the main codebase. Each hook script declares its own dependencies inline, ensuring isolation, portability, and fast execution. | ## Hook Error Codes & Flow Control Hooks communicate status and control flow through exit codes. The following table lists the exit code behavior: | | Exit Code | Behavior | Description | | --- | --- | --- | | 0 | Success | Hook executed successfully. | | 2 | Blocking Error | Critical error, stderr is fed back to Claude automatically. | | Other | Non-blocking Error | stderr is shown to the user, execution continues normally. | | ## Best Practices for Flow Control * Use UserPromptSubmit for early intervention * Use PreToolUse for prevention * Use PostToolUse for validation * Use Stop for completion * Handle errors gracefully * Avoid infinite loops * Test thoroughly | ## Claude Code Sub-Agents Claude Code supports specialized sub-agents that handle specific tasks with custom system prompts, tools, and separate context windows. Sub-agents are AI assistants that the primary Claude Code agent can delegate tasks to. | ### Understanding Sub-Agents * System prompts, not user prompts * Information flow: User → Primary Agent → Sub-Agent → Primary Agent → User | ### Creating and Using Sub-Agents Refer to the Claude Code Sub-Agents documentation for more details. Watch the YouTube video on creating and using Claude Code sub-agents effectively.

Title: ElevenLabs Text to Speech Server
Summary: ### Introduction to ElevenLabs Model Context Protocol (MCP) The ElevenLabs MCP server enables interaction with powerful Text to Speech and audio processing APIs. This server supports various MCP clients, including Claude Desktop, Cursor, Windsurf, and OpenAI Agents. | ### Key Features * Generate speech * Clone voices * Transcribe audio * Create soundscapes * Convert speech into text | ### Quickstart with Claude Desktop To get started with Claude Desktop: * Obtain an API key from ElevenLabs (free tier available with 10k credits per month) * Install the `uv` package manager using `curl -LsSf https://astral.sh/uv/install.sh | sh` * Configure Claude Desktop by editing the `claude_desktop_config.json` file to include the following: ```json { "mcpServers": { "ElevenLabs": { "command": "uvx", "args": ["elevenlabs-mcp"], "env": { "ELEVENLABS_API_KEY": "<insert-your-api-key-here>" } } } } ``` * Enable "Developer Mode" in Claude Desktop if using Windows | ### Other MCP Clients For other clients like Cursor and Windsurf: * Install the `elevenlabs-mcp` package using `pip install elevenlabs-mcp` * Run `python -m elevenlabs_mcp --api-key={{PUT_YOUR_API_KEY_HERE}} --print` to get the configuration * Paste the configuration into the appropriate directory specified by your MCP client | ### Example Usage * Create an AI agent that speaks like a film noir detective * Generate voice variations for a character * Convert a recording to sound like a medieval knight * Create a soundscape of a thunderstorm * Transcribe speech and convert it back using unique voices | ### Optional Features #### File Output Configuration Configure file output using environment variables in `claude_desktop_config.json`: * `ELEVENLABS_MCP_BASE_PATH`: specify the base path for file operations (default: `~/Desktop`) * `ELEVENLABS_MCP_OUTPUT_MODE`: control how generated files are returned (default: `files`) | #### Output Modes The `ELEVENLABS_MCP_OUTPUT_MODE` environment variable supports three modes: * `files`: save files to disk and return file paths * `resources`: return files as MCP resources (binary data is base64-encoded, text is returned as UTF-8 text) * `both`: save files to disk and return as MCP resources | ### Resource Mode Benefits * Files are returned directly in the MCP response as base64-encoded data * No disk I/O required, useful for containerized or serverless environments * MCP clients can access file content immediately without file system access | ### Use Cases * `files`: traditional file-based workflows, local development * `resources`: cloud environments, MCP clients without file system access * `both`: maximum flexibility, caching, and resource sharing scenarios | ### Data Residency Specify the data residency region using the `ELEVENLABS_API_RESIDENCY` environment variable (default: `us`). Note: data residency is an enterprise-only feature. | ### Contributing To contribute or run from source: * Clone the repository: `git clone https://github.com/elevenlabs/elevenlabs-mcp` * Create a virtual environment and install dependencies using `uv` * Copy `.env.example` to `.env` and add your ElevenLabs API key * Run the tests to ensure everything is working | ### Troubleshooting * Logs can be found at: 	+ Windows: `%APPDATA%\Claude\logs\mcp-server-elevenlabs.log` 	+ macOS: `~/Library/Logs/Claude/mcp-server-elevenlabs.log` * Timeouts may occur when using certain tools; this should not occur when using a client like Claude * If encountering the error "MCP ElevenLabs: spawn uvx ENOENT", confirm the absolute path of `uvx` and update the configuration accordingly

Title: Chrome Browser Automation Project
Tags: LLMs, Model Context Protocol, Chrome, Node.js, JavaScript
Summary: ### Introduction to Chrome MCP Server Chrome MCP Server is a Chrome extension-based Model Context Protocol (MCP) server that exposes your Chrome browser functionality to AI assistants, enabling complex browser automation, content analysis, and semantic search. | ### Core Features * **Chatbot/Model Agnostic**: Compatible with any LLM or chatbot client or agent * **Use Your Original Browser**: Integrates with your existing browser environment, configurations, and login states * **Fully Local**: Ensures user privacy with a pure local MCP server * **Streamable HTTP**: Supports streamable HTTP connection method * **Cross-Tab**: Enables cross-tab context * **Semantic Search**: Built-in vector database for intelligent browser tab content discovery * **Smart Content Analysis**: AI-powered text extraction and similarity matching * **20+ Tools**: Supports screenshots, network monitoring, interactive operations, bookmark management, browsing history, and more * **SIMD-Accelerated AI**: Custom WebAssembly SIMD optimization for 4-8x faster vector operations | ### Comparison with Similar Projects | Comparison Dimension | Playwright-based MCP Server | Chrome Extension-based MCP Server | | --- | --- | --- | | Resource Usage | Requires launching independent browser process | No need to launch independent browser process | | User Session Reuse | Requires re-login | Automatically uses existing login state | | Browser Environment | Clean environment lacks user settings | Fully preserves user environment | | API Access | Limited to Playwright API | Full access to Chrome native APIs | | Startup Speed | Requires launching browser process | Only needs to activate extension | | Response Speed | 50-200ms inter-process communication | Faster | | ### Quick Start #### Prerequisites * Node.js >= 18.19.0 and pnpm/npm * Chrome/Chromium browser #### Installation Steps 1. Download the latest Chrome extension from GitHub 2. Install mcp-chrome-bridge globally using npm or pnpm 3. Load Chrome Extension: 	* Open Chrome and go to chrome://extensions/ 	* Enable "Developer mode" 	* Click "Load unpacked" and select the extension folder 	* Click the extension icon to open the plugin, then click connect to see the MCP configuration | ### Available Tools The following tools are available: #### Browser Management (6 tools) * get_windows_and_tabs: List all browser windows and tabs * chrome_navigate: Navigate to URLs and control viewport * chrome_switch_tab: Switch the current active tab * chrome_close_tabs: Close specific tabs or windows * chrome_go_back_or_forward: Browser navigation control * chrome_inject_script: Inject content scripts into web pages #### Screenshots & Visual (1 tool) * chrome_screenshot: Advanced screenshot capture with element targeting, full-page support, and custom dimensions #### Network Monitoring (4 tools) * chrome_network_capture_start/stop: webRequest API network capture * chrome_network_debugger_start/stop: Debugger API with response bodies * chrome_network_request: Send custom HTTP requests #### Content Analysis (4 tools) * search_tabs_content: AI-powered semantic search across browser tabs * chrome_get_web_content: Extract HTML/text content from pages * chrome_get_interactive_elements: Find clickable elements * chrome_console: Capture and retrieve console output from browser tabs #### Interaction (3 tools) * chrome_click_element: Click elements using CSS selectors * chrome_fill_or_select: Fill forms and select options * chrome_keyboard: Simulate keyboard input and shortcuts #### Data Management (5 tools) * chrome_history: Search browser history with time filters * chrome_bookmark_search: Find bookmarks by keywords * chrome_bookmark_add: Add new bookmarks with folder support * chrome_bookmark_delete: Delete bookmarks | ### Usage Examples * AI helps summarize webpage content and automatically control Excalidraw for drawing * AI automatically controls Excalidraw to replicate an image * AI automatically injects scripts and modifies webpage styles * AI automatically captures network requests * AI helps analyze browsing history * AI translates and summarizes web pages * AI takes screenshots of web pages * AI manages bookmarks | ### Contributing We welcome contributions! Please see [CONTRIBUTING.md](CONTRIBUTING.md) for detailed guidelines. | ### Future Roadmap We have exciting plans for the future development of Chrome MCP Server: * Authentication * Recording and Playback * Workflow Automation * Enhanced Browser Support (Firefox Extension) | ### License This project is licensed under the MIT License - see the [LICENSE](LICENSE) file for details. | ### More Documentation * [Architecture Design](Architecture Design): Detailed technical architecture documentation * [TOOLS API](TOOLS API): Complete tool API documentation * [Troubleshooting](Troubleshooting): Common issue solutions

Title: Advanced Video Generation Models
Summary: ### Introduction to Wan2.2 Wan2.2 is a major upgrade to the foundational video models, incorporating innovations such as Effective MoE Architecture, Cinematic-level Aesthetics, Complex Motion Generation, and Efficient High-Definition Hybrid TI2V. | ### Key Features of Wan2.2 * **Effective MoE Architecture**: A Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) architecture is introduced into video diffusion models, enlarging the overall model capacity while maintaining the same computational cost. * **Cinematic-level Aesthetics**: Meticulously curated aesthetic data is incorporated, allowing for more precise and controllable cinematic style generation. * **Complex Motion Generation**: The model is trained on a significantly larger dataset, enhancing its generalization across multiple dimensions such as motions, semantics, and aesthetics. * **Efficient High-Definition Hybrid TI2V**: A 5B model is open-sourced, achieving a compression ratio of 16×16×4 and supporting both text-to-video and image-to-video generation at 720P resolution with 24fps. | ### Models and Download Links | Model | Description | Download Link | | --- | --- | --- | | T2V-A14B | Text-to-Video MoE model, supports 480P & 720P | [Huggingface](https://huggingface.co/Wan-AI/Wan2.2-T2V-A14B) | | I2V-A14B | Image-to-Video MoE model, supports 480P & 720P | [Huggingface](https://huggingface.co/Wan-AI/Wan2.2-I2V-A14B) | | TI2V-5B | High-compression VAE, T2V+I2V, supports 720P | [Huggingface](https://huggingface.co/Wan-AI/Wan2.2-TI2V-5B) | | S2V-14B | Speech-to-Video model, supports 480P & 720P | [Huggingface](https://huggingface.co/Wan-AI/Wan2.2-S2V-14B) | | Animate-14B | Character animation and replacement | [Huggingface](https://huggingface.co/Wan-AI/Wan2.2-Animate-14B) | | ### Running Wan2.2 #### Installation 1. Clone the repository: `git clone https://github.com/Wan-Video/Wan2.2.git` 2. Install dependencies: `pip install -r requirements.txt` | #### Running Text-to-Video Generation * Single-GPU inference: `python generate.py --task t2v-A14B --size 1280*720 --ckpt_dir ./Wan2.2-T2V-A14B --offload_model True --convert_model_dtype --prompt "Two anthropomorphic cats in comfy boxing gear and bright gloves fight intensely on a spotlighted stage."` * Multi-GPU inference using FSDP + DeepSpeed Ulysses: `torchrun --nproc_per_node=8 generate.py --task t2v-A14B --size 1280*720 --ckpt_dir ./Wan2.2-T2V-A14B --dit_fsdp --t5_fsdp --ulysses_size 8 --prompt "Two anthropomorphic cats in comfy boxing gear and bright gloves fight intensely on a spotlighted stage."` | #### Running Image-to-Video Generation * Single-GPU inference: `python generate.py --task i2v-A14B --size 1280*720 --ckpt_dir ./Wan2.2-I2V-A14B --offload_model True --convert_model_dtype --image examples/i2v_input.JPG --prompt "Summer beach vacation style, a white cat wearing sunglasses sits on a surfboard."` * Multi-GPU inference using FSDP + DeepSpeed Ulysses: `torchrun --nproc_per_node=8 generate.py --task i2v-A14B --size 1280*720 --ckpt_dir ./Wan2.2-I2V-A14B --dit_fsdp --t5_fsdp --ulysses_size 8 --image examples/i2v_input.JPG --prompt "Summer beach vacation style, a white cat wearing sunglasses sits on a surfboard."` | #### Running Text-Image-to-Video Generation * Single-GPU inference: `python generate.py --task ti2v-5B --size 1280*704 --ckpt_dir ./Wan2.2-TI2V-5B --offload_model True --convert_model_dtype --image examples/i2v_input.JPG --prompt "Summer beach vacation style, a white cat wearing sunglasses sits on a surfboard."` * Multi-GPU inference using FSDP + DeepSpeed Ulysses: `torchrun --nproc_per_node=8 generate.py --task ti2v-5B --size 1280*704 --ckpt_dir ./Wan2.2-TI2V-5B --dit_fsdp --t5_fsdp --ulysses_size 8 --image examples/i2v_input.JPG --prompt "Summer beach vacation style, a white cat wearing sunglasses sits on a surfboard."` | #### Running Speech-to-Video Generation * Single-GPU inference: `python generate.py --task s2v-14B --size 1024*704 --ckpt_dir ./Wan2.2-S2V-14B/ --offload_model True --convert_model_dtype --prompt "Summer beach vacation style, a white cat wearing sunglasses sits on a surfboard." --image "examples/i2v_input.JPG" --audio "examples/talk.wav"` * Multi-GPU inference using FSDP + DeepSpeed Ulysses: `torchrun --nproc_per_node=8 generate.py --task s2v-14B --size 1024*704 --ckpt_dir ./Wan2.2-S2V-14B/ --dit_fsdp --t5_fsdp --ulysses_size 8 --prompt "Summer beach vacation style, a white cat wearing sunglasses sits on a surfboard." --image "examples/i2v_input.JPG" --audio "examples/talk.wav"` | ### Community Works Several community projects have built upon Wan2.1 or Wan2.2, including: * HuMo: A unified, human-centric framework for producing high-quality, fine-grained, and controllable human videos from multimodal inputs. * FastVideo: Distilled Wan models with sparse attention for faster inference times. * Cache-dit: Fully Cache Acceleration support for Wan2.2 MoE with DBCache, TaylorSeer, and Cache CFG. * Kijai's ComfyUI WanVideoWrapper: An alternative implementation of Wan models for ComfyUI. * DiffSynth-Studio: Comprehensive support for Wan 2.2, including low-GPU-memory layer-by-layer offload, FP8 quantization, sequence parallelism, LoRA training, and full training. | ### Todo List The following tasks are planned for future development: * Wan2.2 Text-to-Video: Multi-GPU inference code, checkpoints, ComfyUI integration, and Diffusers integration. * Wan2.2 Image-to-Video: Multi-GPU inference code, checkpoints, ComfyUI integration, and Diffusers integration. * Wan2.2 Text-Image-to-Video: Multi-GPU inference code, checkpoints, ComfyUI integration, and Diffusers integration. * Wan2.2-S2V: Inference code, checkpoints, ComfyUI integration, and Diffusers integration. * Wan2.2-Animate: Inference code, checkpoints, ComfyUI integration, and Diffusers integration. | ### Run Wan-Animate Wan-Animate takes a video and a character image as input and generates a video in either "animation" or "replacement" mode. * Preprocessing: The input video should be preprocessed into several materials before being fed into the inference process.

Title: VibeVoice: A Frontier Long Conversational Text-to-Speech Model
Summary: # VibeVoice: A Frontier Long Conversational Text-to-Speech Model ## Overview VibeVoice is an open-source research framework designed to generate expressive, long-form, multi-speaker conversational audio from text. It addresses challenges in traditional Text-to-Speech (TTS) systems, including scalability, speaker consistency, and natural turn-taking. | ## Key Features * Novel framework for generating conversational audio, such as podcasts * Uses continuous speech tokenizers (Acoustic and Semantic) operating at 7.5 Hz * Employs a next-token diffusion framework with a Large Language Model (LLM) * Can synthesize speech up to 90 minutes long with up to 4 distinct speakers | ## Demo Examples The following demo examples are available: * Video Demo: produced with Wan2.2 * Audio Demos: + English: ES_._3.mp4 + Chinese: default.mp4 + Cross-Lingual: 1p_EN2CH.mp4 + Spontaneous Singing: 2p_see_u_again.mp4 + Long Conversation with 4 people: 4p_climate_45min.mp4 | ## Risks and Limitations The following risks and limitations apply: * Potential for unexpected, biased, or inaccurate outputs * Inheritance of biases, errors, or omissions from the base model (Qwen2.5 1.5b) * Risk of Deepfakes and Disinformation: high-quality synthetic speech can be misused | ## Usage Guidelines The following guidelines apply: * Use transcripts that are reliable and accurate * Avoid using generated content in misleading ways * Disclose the use of AI when sharing AI-generated content * Use the model in compliance with all applicable laws and regulations | ## Technical Limitations The following technical limitations apply: | Limitation | Description | | --- | --- | | Language Support | English and Chinese only | | Non-Speech Audio | Does not handle background noise, music, or other sound effects | | Overlapping Speech | Does not explicitly model or generate overlapping speech segments | | Commercial Use | Not recommended for commercial or real-world applications without further testing and development | | ## Important Notice VibeVoice is intended for research and development purposes only. Users are expected to use the generated content and deploy the models in a lawful manner, in full compliance with all applicable laws and regulations.

Title: ChatLLM by Abacus.AI
Summary: # ChatLLM by Abacus.AI | ## Overview | ChatLLM by Abacus.AI is an all-in-one AI assistant platform that provides access to multiple state-of-the-art large language models (LLMs), including GPT-5, Claude, and Gemini. It enhances productivity for professionals, small teams, and enterprises by integrating general-purpose AI agents, coding tools, and multimedia generation into a single platform. | ## Key Features and Capabilities | - **Access to Multiple LLMs** - GPT-5, GPT-4.1, Gemini, Sonnet, and more | - **AI Agents and Tools** - DeepAgent: Automates workflows, data analysis, and app development - Python code generation and execution - Custom chatbot and AI agent creation for specific business needs | - **Real-Time Interaction** - Interaction with web content and documents (e.g., chat with PDFs) | - **Multimedia Generation** - Image and video generation from text prompts using models like FLUX.1 and DALL-E 3 | - **Integration and Collaboration** - Integration with enterprise tools: Slack, Microsoft Teams, Google Drive, Confluence - Team collaboration features: chat sharing and subscription management | - **Security and Compliance** - Enterprise-level security with data encryption, SOC-2 Type-2, and HIPAA compliance | - **Mobile Support** - Mobile apps available for iOS and Android with voice mode | - **AI Engineer Tool** - Customization of roles, knowledge bases, and functions of AI agents | ## Main Category | AI productivity platform / AI assistant software focusing on large language models and workflow automation. | ## Target Audience and Use Cases | | Audience | Use Cases | |--------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | Professionals and Developers | Advanced AI assistance for coding, data analysis, and content creation | | Small Teams and Enterprises | Workflow automation, streamlined collaboration, and custom AI agent development | | Students and Individuals | Powerful AI chat and research capabilities | | Organizations | Secure, compliant AI integrations with internal systems and data sources | | By leveraging the capabilities of ChatLLM, users can enhance their productivity and efficiency through advanced AI tools and integrations.

Title: Cloud Infrastructure Deployment Made Easy
Summary: ### Introduction to Railway Railway is a platform designed to improve the developer experience by rebuilding every layer of the stack for speed. It offers instant deployments and effortless scale, making it a better way to deploy applications. | ### Key Features * Automated service discovery * Blazing fast networking * Support for any protocol * Intuitive vertical and horizontal scaling * Dynamic scaling of servers, storage, and networking to meet application demands * Environments, staged changes, and pull request deploys for accelerated development lifecycle | ### Network and Connect Interconnect your application seamlessly with highly performant networking. Railway provides: * Automated service discovery * Blazing fast networking * Support for any protocol | ### Scale and Grow Scale your applications with intuitive vertical and horizontal scaling. Railway dynamically scales: * Servers * Storage * Networking to meet application demands | ### Evolve and Collaborate Accelerate your development lifecycle with: * Environments * Staged changes * Pull request deploys Railway allows teams to make changes in one environment and seamlessly re-apply them in another without risk of breaking production. | ### Deployment Examples | Application | Domain | Deployment Method | | --- | --- | --- | | backend | backend-prod.up.railway.app | CLI | | frontend | frontend-prod.up.railway.app | GitHub | | api gateway | api-prod.up.railway.app | GitHub | | pg-data | postgres | Docker | | umami | umami-prod.up.railway.app | GitHub | | docs | docs-prod.up.railway.app | GitHub | | ### Getting Started Deploy your first project today and experience a better way to deploy applications.

Title: AI Powered Content Creation Platform
Summary: ### Introduction to Dibbly Create Dibbly Create is an all-in-one platform that enables users to create genuine content and speed up their success with easy human-AI collaboration. | ### Key Features * Creates beautiful interiors for both ePub and Paperback, fully customizable and ready to be published * Modify and improve content by highlighting text and using KIP, with 7 options available * Quick access to all KIP tools, including brainstorm, rewrite, and image generation * Chat with ChatKIP interface for requesting freelancers, brainstorming ideas, and inserting text into editor * Generate images right in your document, simply prompt KIP or ChatKIP with what you need | ### Research Tools Dibbly Create offers 5 different types of research, including: * Amazon research: search term analysis, in-depth book analysis, and category analysis * YouTube Research: breaks down video content based on search term, topics discussed, mentions, key takeaways, and more * Google Research: breaks down articles based on search term, overview + key takeaways, competitive analysis, etc. * All tools now accept custom inputs to refine analysis for what users need | ### Content Writer The Content Writer tool, powered by AI, provides: * Human-like writing: KIP writes chapters that are personal and engaging * In-depth research: KIP uses real-time data from diverse sources like Amazon, YouTube, and Google * Customizable tools: refine and tweak your content with our user-friendly interface | ### Collaboration * Create or join team sprints for collaborative writing or research Title: Stella Model Technology Report Released
Summary: ### Introduction The Stella model is trained based on Alibaba-NLP/gte-large-en-v1.5 and Alibaba-NLP/gte-Qwen2-1.5B-instruct. The model simplifies usage of prompts, providing two prompts for most general tasks: * s2p (sentence-to-passage) task: Instruct: Given a web search query, retrieve relevant passages that answer the query.\nQuery: {query} * s2s (sentence-to-sentence) task: Instruct: Retrieve semantically similar text.\nQuery: {query} | ### Model Directory Structure The model directory structure is a standard SentenceTransformer directory with a series of 2_Dense_{dims} folders, where dims represents the final vector dimension. | ### Usage The model can be used with SentenceTransformers or transformers library to encode text. #### SentenceTransformers * Import SentenceTransformer from sentence_transformers * Define query_prompt_name as "s2p_query" or "s2s_query" * Define queries and docs * Initialize model with "dunzhang/stella_en_1.5B_v5" and trust_remote_code=True * Encode queries and docs using model.encode() * Calculate similarities using model.similarity() | #### Transformers * Import AutoModel, AutoTokenizer from transformers * Define query_prompt and queries * Define docs * Initialize model with "dunzhang/stella_en_1.5B_v5" and trust_remote_code=True * Encode queries and docs using model and tokenizer * Calculate similarities using query_vectors @ docs_vectors.T | ### Infinity Usage The model can be used with Infinity, MIT Licensed Inference Server and Docker. | ### Citation @misc{zhang2025jasperstelladistillationsota, title={Jasper and Stella: distillation of SOTA embedding models}, author={Dun Zhang and Jiacheng Li and Ziyang Zeng and Fulong Wang}, year={2025}, eprint={2412.19048}, archivePrefix={arXiv}, primaryClass={cs.IR}} | ### FAQ * Q: The details of training? A: The training method and datasets will be released in the future. * Q: How to choose a suitable prompt for my own task? A: In most cases, please use the s2p and s2s prompts. * Q: How to reproduce MTEB results? A: Please use evaluation scripts in Alibaba-NLP/gte-Qwen2-1.5B-instruct or intfloat/e5-mistral-7b-instruct * Q: Why each dimension has a linear weight? A: MRL has multiple training methods, we choose this method which has the best performance. * Q: What is the sequence length of models? A: 512 is recommended. | ### Model Information * Downloads last month: 83,057 * Model size: 1.54B params * Tensor type: F32 * Inference Providers: Sentence Similarity * Model tree: 88 adapters, 17 finetunes, 2 quantizations * Spaces using: 52 | ### Evaluation Results | Metric | Value | | --- | --- | | accuracy on MTEB AmazonCounterfactualClassification (en) | 92.866 | | ap on MTEB AmazonCounterfactualClassification (en) | 72.135 | | ap_weighted on MTEB AmazonCounterfactualClassification (en) | 72.135 | | f1 on MTEB AmazonCounterfactualClassification (en) | 89.559 | | f1_weighted on MTEB AmazonCounterfactualClassification (en) | 93.136 | | main_score on MTEB AmazonCounterfactualClassification (en) | 92.866 | | accuracy on MTEB AmazonPolarityClassification | 97.165 | | ap on MTEB AmazonPolarityClassification | 96.055 | | ap_weighted on MTEB AmazonPolarityClassification | 96.055 | | f1 on MTEB AmazonPolarityClassification | 97.164 |

Title: Nextron Desktop App Development
Summary: ### Introduction to Nextron Nextron is a framework for building desktop applications using web technologies. It combines the power of Next.js and Electron to provide a seamless development experience. | ### Key Features * Develop desktop apps with only web knowledge * Easy to use and transparent * Open to OSS developers | ### Usage To create a new Nextron application, use the following command: ```bash npx create-nextron-app MY_APP --example with-tailwindcss ``` Replace `MY_APP` with your desired app name and `with-tailwindcss` with your preferred example. | ### Running the Application To run the application in development mode, use the following command: ```bash npm run dev ``` This will launch the Electron app. | ### Production Build To build the application for production, use the following command: ```bash npm run build ``` This will output packaged bundles under the `dist` folder. | ### Basic Directory Structure The basic directory structure for a Nextron application is as follows: ```markdown . ├── main │ ├── background.ts │ └── preload.ts ├── renderer │ ├── next.config.js │ ├── pages │ │ ├── home.tsx │ │ └── next.tsx │ ├── preload.d.ts │ ├── public │ │ └── images │ │ └── logo.png │ └── tsconfig.json ├── resources │ ├── icon.icns │ └── icon.ico ├── nextron.config.js ├── electron-builder.yml ├── package.json ├── tsconfig.json └── README.md ``` ### Configuration #### next.config.js The `next.config.js` file is used to configure the Next.js application. For example: ```javascript module.exports = { output: 'export', distDir: process.env.NODE_ENV === 'production' ? '../app' : '.next', trailingSlash: true, images: { unoptimized: true, }, webpack: (config) => { return config; }, }; ``` #### electron-builder.yml The `electron-builder.yml` file is used to configure the Electron builder. For example: ```yml appId: com.example.nextron productName: My Nextron App copyright: Copyright 2020 Yoshihide Shiono directories: output: dist buildResources: resources files: - from: . filter: - package.json - app publish: null ``` #### nextron.config.js The `nextron.config.js` file is used to configure the Nextron application. For example: ```javascript module.exports = { mainSrcDir: 'main', rendererSrcDir: 'renderer', webpack: (config, env) => { // do some stuff here return config; }, }; ``` ### Examples Nextron provides several examples to get you started. These include: * basic-lang-javascript * basic-lang-javascript-python * basic-lang-typescript * basic-launch-app-from-url * basic-store-data * custom-build-options * custom-renderer-port * with-ant-design * with-chakra-ui * with-emotion * with-material-ui * with-next-i18next * with-tailwindcss | ### Development To develop Nextron, follow these steps: 1. Clone the repository: `git clone https://github.com/saltyshiomix/nextron.git` 2. Install dependencies: `pnpm install` 3. Run the development server: `pnpm dev` | ### Maintainers The maintainers of Nextron are: * saltyshiomix (Shiono Yoshihide) * lacymorrow (Lacy Morrow) * Psycokwet * m5x5 * andirsun (Anderson Laverde) * bm777 (Bayang) * FranciscoJBrito (Francisco Brito) * pixelass (Gregor Adams) | ### Community If you have created an app or service using Nextron, feel free to share it with the community. | ### License Nextron is licensed under the MIT license.

Title: Homebridge Smart Home Automation Server
Summary: ### Introduction to Homebridge Homebridge is a lightweight Node.js server that emulates the iOS HomeKit API, allowing control of non-HomeKit devices through Siri. It supports plugins, which provide a bridge from HomeKit to various 3rd-party APIs. | ### Key Features * Supports community-contributed plugins for various devices * Allows control of devices through Siri * Compatible with multiple platforms, including Raspberry Pi, Linux, macOS, Windows, and Docker | ### Installation | Platform | Installation Method | | --- | --- | | Raspberry Pi | Official Homebridge Raspberry Pi Image | | Linux | Install Homebridge on Debian, Ubuntu, Red Hat, CentOS, or Fedora | | macOS | Install Homebridge on macOS | | Windows 10/11 | Install Homebridge on Windows 10/11 using Hyper-V | | Docker | Install Homebridge on Docker | | Synology | Install Homebridge on Synology DSM 7 | | Unraid | Install Homebridge on Unraid | | QNAP | Install Homebridge on QNAP | | TrueNAS Scale | Install Homebridge on TrueNAS Scale | | ### Adding Homebridge to iOS * Open the Home app on your device * Tap the Home tab, then tap + * Tap Add Accessory, then scan the QR code shown in the Homebridge UI or logs | ### Interacting with Devices * Once added to HomeKit, devices can be controlled through Siri * Note: Siri may prefer default phrase handling over HomeKit devices | ### Plugin Development * Homebridge API reference and plugin examples available at [developers.homebridge.io](https://developers.homebridge.io) * Use the Homebridge Plugin Template project to create your own platform plugin * Study existing plugins, such as Homebridge Example Plugins | ### Common Issues * Home App Says Accessory Already Added: Reset Homebridge * My iOS App Can't Find Homebridge: Try swapping between Bonjour HAP and Ciao mDNS Advertiser options, or flush the DNS cache by turning airplane mode on and off | ### Limitations * One bridge can only expose 150 accessories due to HomeKit limit * Can run plugins as a Child Bridge or run Multiple Homebridge Instances to get around limitation * Changing accessory name via Homebridge won't be automatically reflected in iOS | ### Why Homebridge? Homebridge is a temporary solution until device manufacturers implement the HomeKit API. It allows users to control non-HomeKit devices through Siri and provides a fun way to get a taste of the future. | ### Credit Homebridge was originally created by Nick Farina, and the original HomeKit API work was done by Khaos Tian in his HAP-NodeJS project.

Title: n8n Model Context Protocol Server
Summary: ### Introduction to n8n-MCP n8n-MCP is a Model Context Protocol (MCP) server designed to provide AI assistants with comprehensive access to n8n node documentation, properties, and operations. It enables AI models to understand and work effectively with n8n's workflow automation nodes. | ### Key Features * **n8n Nodes**: 536 nodes from both n8n-nodes-base and @n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain * **Node Properties**: 99% coverage with detailed schemas * **Node Operations**: 63.6% coverage of available actions * **Documentation**: 90% coverage from official n8n docs (including AI nodes) * **AI Tools**: 263 AI-capable nodes detected with full documentation * **Real-world Examples**: 2,646 pre-extracted configurations from popular templates * **Template Library**: 2,500+ workflow templates with smart filtering | ### Important Safety Warning * Never edit production workflows directly with AI * Always make a copy of your workflow before using AI tools * Test in a development environment first * Export backups of important workflows * Validate changes before deploying to production | ### Quick Start To get n8n-MCP running in 5 minutes, follow these options: | #### Option 1: npx 1. Install Node.js on your system 2. Run `npx n8n-mcp` 3. Add to Claude Desktop config: ```json { "mcpServers": { "n8n-mcp": { "command": "npx", "args": ["n8n-mcp"], "env": { "MCP_MODE": "stdio", "LOG_LEVEL": "error", "DISABLE_CONSOLE_OUTPUT": "true" } } } } ``` | #### Option 2: Docker 1. Install Docker on your system 2. Pull the Docker image: `docker pull ghcr.io/czlonkowski/n8n-mcp:latest` 3. Add to Claude Desktop config: ```json { "mcpServers": { "n8n-mcp": { "command": "docker", "args": [ "run", "-i", "--rm", "--init", "-e", "MCP_MODE=stdio", "-e", "LOG_LEVEL=error", "-e", "DISABLE_CONSOLE_OUTPUT=true", "ghcr.io/czlonkowski/n8n-mcp:latest" ] } } } ``` | ### Configuration File Locations * macOS: `~/Library/Application Support/Claude/claude_desktop_config.json` * Windows: `%APPDATA%\Claude\claude_desktop_config.json` * Linux: `~/.config/Claude/claude_desktop_config.json` | ### Local n8n Instance Configuration To allow localhost webhooks, set `WEBHOOK_SECURITY_MODE=moderate` in your Docker configuration. | ### Privacy & Telemetry n8n-mcp collects anonymous usage statistics. To opt out, use the following commands: * For npx users: `npx n8n-mcp telemetry disable` * For Docker users: Add the environment variable `N8N_MCP_TELEMETRY_DISABLED=true` to your Docker configuration. | ### Support This Project n8n-mcp is a personal project that helps tens of thousands of developers automate their workflows efficiently. Your sponsorship helps maintain and develop this project. | ### Important Rules #### Core Behavior 1. **Silent execution**: No commentary between tools 2. **Parallel by default**: Execute independent operations simultaneously 3. **Templates first**: Always check before building (2,500+ available) 4. **Multi-level validation**: Quick check → Full validation → Workflow validation 5. **Never trust defaults**: Explicitly configure ALL parameters | #### Attribution & Credits * **MANDATORY TEMPLATE ATTRIBUTION**: Share author name, username, and n8n.io link * **Template validation**: Always validate before deployment (may need updates) | #### Performance * **Batch operations**: Use diff operations with multiple changes in one call * **Parallel execution**: Search, validate, and configure simultaneously * **Template metadata**: Use smart filtering for faster discovery | #### Code Node Usage * **Avoid when possible**: Prefer standard nodes * **Only when necessary**: Use code node as last resort * **AI tool capability**: ANY node can be an AI tool (not just marked ones) | ### Most Popular n8n Nodes 1. **n8n-nodes-base.code**: JavaScript/Python scripting 2. **n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest**: HTTP API calls 3. **n8n-nodes-base.webhook**: Event-driven triggers 4. **n8n-nodes-base.set**: Data transformation 5. **n8n-nodes-base.if**: Conditional routing 6. **n8n-nodes-base.manualTrigger**: Manual workflow execution 7. **n8n-nodes-base.respondToWebhook**: Webhook responses 8. **n8n-nodes-base.scheduleTrigger**: Time-based triggers 9. **@n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.agent**: AI agents 10. **n8n-nodes-base.googleSheets**: Spreadsheet integration 11. **n8n-nodes-base.merge**: Data merging 12. **n8n-nodes-base.switch**: Multi-branch routing 13. **n8n-nodes-base.telegram**: Telegram bot integration 14. **@n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.lmChatOpenAi**: OpenAI chat models 15. **n8n-nodes-base.splitInBatches**: Batch processing 16. **n8n-nodes-base.openAi**: OpenAI legacy node 17. **n8n-nodes-base.gmail**: Email automation 18. **n8n-nodes-base.function**: Custom functions 19. **n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote**: Workflow documentation 20. **n8n-nodes-base.executeWorkflowTrigger**: Sub-workflow calls

Title: Introduction to Conversational Voice
Summary: # Introduction to Conversational Voice Conversational voice is the most intimate medium for humans, conveying layers of meaning through tone, pitch, rhythm, and emotion. Current digital voice assistants lack essential qualities to make them truly useful, failing to unlock the full power of voice. | ## Achieving Voice Presence The goal is to achieve "voice presence," making spoken interactions feel real, understood, and valued. This involves creating conversational partners that engage in genuine dialogue, building confidence and trust over time. | ### Key Components * Emotional intelligence: reading and responding to emotional contexts * Conversational dynamics: natural timing, pauses, interruptions, and emphasis * Contextual awareness: adjusting tone and style to match the situation * Consistent personality: maintaining a coherent, reliable, and appropriate presence | ## Conversational Speech Model (CSM) CSM is a multimodal, text and speech model that operates directly on RVQ tokens. It uses two autoregressive transformers to model the zeroth codebook and reconstruct speech from the backbone's representations. | ### CSM Model Inference Process 1. Text and audio tokens are interleaved and fed sequentially into the backbone. 2. The backbone predicts the zeroth level of the codebook. 3. The decoder samples levels 1 through N-1 conditioned on the predicted zeroth level. 4. The reconstructed audio token is autoregressively fed back into the backbone for the next step. | ### Technical Details * The model is trained on a large dataset of publicly available audio, transcribed, diarized, and segmented. * Three model sizes are trained: Tiny, Small, and Medium. * Each model is trained with a 2048 sequence length over five epochs. | ## Experiments and Evaluation ### Dataset and Model Sizes | Model Size | Backbone Size | Decoder Size | | --- | --- | --- | | Tiny | 1B | 100M | | Small | 3B | 250M | | Medium | 8B | 300M | | ### Evaluation Metrics * Faithfulness to text * Context utilization * Prosody * Latency | ### Objective Metrics * Word Error Rate (WER) * Speaker Similarity (SIM) * Homograph Disambiguation * Pronunciation Continuation Consistency | ### Objective Metric Results The results show that the metrics are saturated, with modern models achieving near-human performance. The new phonetic transcription-based benchmarks assess pronunciation and contextual understanding. | ### Subjective Metrics A Comparative Mean Opinion Score (CMOS) study was conducted to assess the naturalness and prosodic appropriateness of generated speech. Human evaluators rated the generated sample on a 7-point preference scale relative to the reference. | ## Conclusion The Conversational Speech Model (CSM) is a step towards achieving voice presence, making spoken interactions feel real, understood, and valued. The model's performance improves with larger models, supporting the hypothesis that scaling enhances the synthesis of more realistic speech.

Title: OmniParser: Screen Parsing Tool for Pure Vision Based GUI Agent
Summary: # OmniParser: Screen Parsing Tool for Pure Vision Based GUI Agent ## Overview OmniParser is a comprehensive method for parsing user interface screenshots into structured and easy-to-understand elements, enhancing the ability of GPT-4V to generate actions that can be accurately grounded in the corresponding regions of the interface. | ## News and Updates * 2025/3: Local logging of trajectory is now supported for building training data pipelines. * 2025/3: Multi-agent orchestration and user interface improvements are being added to OmniTool. * 2025/2: OmniParser V2 checkpoints have been released. * 2025/2: OmniTool has been introduced, allowing control of a Windows 11 VM with OmniParser and a vision model of choice. * 2025/1: V2 is coming, with new state-of-the-art results on the Screen Spot Pro grounding benchmark. * 2024/11: OmniParser V1.5 has been released, featuring more fine-grained icon detection and prediction of interactable screen elements. * 2024/10: OmniParser was the #1 trending model on the Hugging Face model hub. * 2024/10: A demo is available on Hugging Face Space. * 2024/09: OmniParser achieved the best performance on the Windows Agent Arena. | ## Installation To install OmniParser, follow these steps: * Clone the repository * Create a conda environment: `conda create -n "omni" python==3.12` * Activate the environment: `conda activate omni` * Install requirements: `pip install -r requirements.txt` * Download V2 weights to the `weights` folder, if not already done | ## Examples and Demos * A few simple examples are available in `demo.ipynb`. * To run the Gradio demo, use: `python gradio_demo.py` | ## Model Weights License The model checkpoints on Hugging Face Model Hub have the following licenses: | Model | License | | --- | --- | | icon_detect | AGPL | | icon_caption_blip2 & icon_caption_florence | MIT | | ## Citation If you find OmniParser useful, please cite the technical report: ``` @misc{lu2024omniparserpurevisionbased, title={OmniParser for Pure Vision Based GUI Agent}, author={Yadong Lu and Jianwei Yang and Yelong Shen and Ahmed Awadallah}, year={2024}, eprint={2408.00203}, archivePrefix={arXiv}, primaryClass={cs.CV}, url={https://arxiv.org/abs/2408.00203}, } ```

Title: surf.new
Summary: # surf.new | A playground for testing web agents powered by Steel.dev. Experience how different AI agents can surf and interact with the web just like humans do. | ## Features | - Test multiple web agents in a controlled environment - Real-time browser interaction visualization - Support for various AI providers (Claude, GPT-4, etc.) - Built with modern web technologies (Next.js 15, FastAPI, Steel SDK) - Beautiful UI powered by Shadcn UI and Tailwind CSS | ## Getting Started | ### Prerequisites | - Node.js 18+ and npm - Python 3.11+ - Steel API Key (sign up at steel.dev) or Local running instance | ### Installation | 1. Clone the repository: ```bash git clone https://github.com/steel-dev/surf.new cd surf.new ``` | 2. Install frontend dependencies: ```bash npm install ``` | 3. Install backend dependencies: ```bash python -m venv venv source venv/bin/activate # On Windows: .\venv\Scripts\activate pip install -r requirements.txt ``` | 4. Set up environment variables: ```bash cp .env.example .env.local ``` Edit `.env.local` and add your Steel API key and other required variables. | ### Running Locally | 1. Run the development server: ```bash npm run dev ``` | 2. **Windows Users**: Use the Windows-specific command: ```bash npm run dev:win ``` | **Technical Reason**: Windows has two different asyncio event loop implementations: - `SelectorEventLoop` (default): Uses select-based I/O and doesn't support subprocesses properly. - `ProactorEventLoop`: Uses I/O completion ports and fully supports subprocesses. | Playwright requires subprocess support to launch browsers. When hot reloading is enabled, the default `SelectorEventLoop` is used, causing a `NotImplementedError` when Playwright tries to create a subprocess. | **Reference Issue**: #32 | This will start both the Next.js frontend (port 3001) and FastAPI backend (port 8000). | 3. Visit `http://localhost:3001` to start using surf.new! | ## Contributing | 1. Fork the repository 2. Create your feature branch: ```bash git checkout -b feature/amazing-feature ``` 3. Commit your changes: ```bash git commit -m 'Add amazing feature' ``` 4. Push to the branch: ```bash git push origin feature/amazing-feature ``` 5. Title: Configuring AI Model Providers
Summary: ### Configuring Model and Provider Midscene uses the OpenAI SDK to call AI services, but it's not limited to OpenAI's services. You can use any model service that supports the same interface. | ### Choosing a Model To learn more about choosing a model, refer to [Choose a model](#choose-a-model). | ### Common Configurations The following are the most common configurations: * **OPENAI_API_KEY**: Required. Your OpenAI API key. * **OPENAI_BASE_URL**: Optional. Custom endpoint URL for API endpoint. * **MIDSCENE_MODEL_NAME**: Optional. Specify a different model name. | ### Extra Configurations for Specific Models The following are extra configurations for specific models: * **Qwen 2.5 VL model**: 	+ **MIDSCENE_USE_QWEN_VL**: Set to "1" to use the adapter of Qwen 2.5 VL model. * **UI-TARS model**: 	+ **MIDSCENE_USE_VLM_UI_TARS**: Version of UI-TARS model, supported values are 1.0 and 1.5. * **DOUBAO (Volcengine version)**: 	+ No extra configuration required. * **Gemini 2.5 Pro model**: 	+ **MIDSCENE_USE_GEMINI**: Set to "1" to use the adapter of Gemini 2.5 Pro model. | ### Advanced Configurations The following are advanced configurations: | Name | Description | | --- | --- | | OPENAI_USE_AZURE | Optional. Set to "true" to use Azure OpenAI Service. | | MIDSCENE_OPENAI_INIT_CONFIG_JSON | Optional. Custom JSON config for OpenAI SDK initialization. | | MIDSCENE_OPENAI_HTTP_PROXY | Optional. HTTP/HTTPS proxy configuration. | | MIDSCENE_OPENAI_SOCKS_PROXY | Optional. SOCKS proxy configuration. | | MIDSCENE_PREFERRED_LANGUAGE | Optional. The preferred language for the model response. | | MIDSCENE_REPLANNING_CYCLE_LIMIT | Optional. The maximum number of replanning cycles. | | OPENAI_MAX_TOKENS | Optional. Maximum tokens for model response. | | ### Debug Configurations The following are debug configurations: * **DEBUG=midscene:ai:profile:stats**: Print AI service cost time, token usage, etc. in comma-separated format. * **DEBUG=midscene:ai:profile:detail**: Print AI token usage details. * **DEBUG=midscene:ai:call**: Print AI response details. * **DEBUG=midscene:android:adb**: Print adb command calling details. | ### Configuring Environment Variables There are two ways to configure environment variables: 1. Set environment variables in your system. 2. Set environment variables using dotenv. | ### Using Azure OpenAI Service To use Azure OpenAI Service, set the following configurations: * **MIDSCENE_USE_AZURE_OPENAI**: Set to "1". * **MIDSCENE_AZURE_OPENAI_SCOPE**: Set to "https://cognitiveservices.azure.com/.default". * **AZURE_OPENAI_ENDPOINT**: Set to the endpoint URL. * **AZURE_OPENAI_API_VERSION**: Set to the API version. | ### Overriding Configurations using JavaScript You can override configurations using JavaScript by calling the `overrideAIConfig` function before running Midscene codes. | ### Examples The following are examples of configuring environment variables for different models: * **Using gpt-4o from OpenAI**: 	+ Set **OPENAI_API_KEY** to your OpenAI API key. 	+ Set **OPENAI_BASE_URL** to the custom endpoint URL. 	+ Set **MIDSCENE_MODEL_NAME** to "gpt-4o-2024-11-20". * **Using qwen-vl-max-latest from Aliyun**: 	+ Set **OPENAI_API_KEY** to your OpenAI API key. 	+ Set **OPENAI_BASE_URL** to the custom endpoint URL. 	+ Set **MIDSCENE_MODEL_NAME** to "qwen-vl-max-latest". 	+ Set **MIDSCENE_USE_QWEN_VL** to "1". * **Using Doubao-1.5-thinking-vision-pro from Volcano Engine**: 	+ Set **OPENAI_BASE_URL** to the custom endpoint URL. 	+ Set **OPENAI_API_KEY** to your OpenAI API key. 	+ Set **MIDSCENE_MODEL_NAME** to "ep-...". 	+ Set **MIDSCENE_USE_DOUBAO_VISION** to "1". * **Using ui-tars-72b-sft hosted by yourself**: 	+ Set **OPENAI_API_KEY** to your OpenAI API key. 	+ Set **OPENAI_BASE_URL** to the custom endpoint URL. 	+ Set **MIDSCENE_MODEL_NAME** to "ui-tars-72b-sft". 	+ Set **MIDSCENE_USE_VLM_UI_TARS** to "1". | ### Troubleshooting Model Service Connectivity Issues To troubleshoot connectivity issues, you can use the 'connectivity-test' folder in the example project. Put your `.env` file in the `connectivity-test` folder and run the test with `npm i && npm run test`.
