Title: Local Code Execution with LLM Interface
Summary: ### Introduction to Open Interpreter Open Interpreter is a desktop application that allows large language models (LLMs) to run code locally. It provides a natural-language interface to a computer's general-purpose capabilities. | ### Key Features * Create and edit photos, videos, PDFs, etc. * Control a Chrome browser to perform research * Plot, clean, and analyze large datasets * Run shell commands, Python, JavaScript, and more | ### Getting Started * Install Open Interpreter using `pip install open-interpreter` * Run `interpreter` in the terminal to start the chat interface * Use `interpreter --help` for more information | ### Commands * `interpreter`: Start the chat interface * `interpreter --model <model_name>`: Change the language model * `interpreter --local`: Run Open Interpreter in local mode * `interpreter --verbose`: Enable verbose mode for debugging | ### Configuration * Use `yaml` files to configure default behaviors * Run `interpreter --profiles` to open the profiles directory * Add `yaml` files to the profiles directory to create multiple profiles | ### Interactive Mode Commands * `%verbose [true/false]`: Toggle verbose mode * `%reset`: Reset the current session's conversation * `%undo`: Remove the previous user message and AI response * `%tokens [prompt]`: Calculate tokens and estimated cost for a prompt * `%help`: Show the help message | ### Running Open Interpreter Locally * Use `interpreter --local` to run in local mode * Set `api_base` and `api_key` to connect to an OpenAI-compatible server * Use `interpreter --local --max_tokens <max_tokens> --context_window <context_window>` to modify token settings | ### API Endpoints * Use FastAPI to create a server that controls Open Interpreter via HTTP REST endpoints * Example server code available in `server.py` | ### Tables #### Language Models | Model | Description | | --- | --- | | gpt-3.5-turbo | A turbocharged version of the GPT-3.5 model | | claude-2 | A Claude 2.0 model | | #### Configuration Options | Option | Description | | --- | --- | | `max_tokens` | The maximum number of tokens to send in a prompt | | `context_window` | The context window size in tokens | | `api_base` | The base URL of the OpenAI-compatible server | | `api_key` | The API key for the OpenAI-compatible server | | ### Examples * Run `interpreter` to start the chat interface * Use `interpreter --model gpt-3.5-turbo` to change the language model * Run `interpreter --local` to run in local mode * Use `interpreter --verbose` to enable verbose mode for debugging

Title: Patreon API Documentation Guide
Summary: ### Introduction to Patreon API The Patreon API provides a set of tools and services to help developers integrate Patreon functionality into their applications. This guide provides an overview of the API, including tutorials, references, and guidelines for getting started. | ### API Libraries Patreon provides open-source libraries for several programming languages to make it easier to work with the API: | * **Python**: Install via pip using `pip install patreon` * **Javascript**: Install via npm using `npm install --save patreon` * **Ruby**: Install via RubyGems using `gem install patreon` * **PHP**: Install via Packagist using `composer require patreon/patreon` * **Java**: Add dependency to Maven project using `<dependency> <groupId>com.patreon</groupId> <artifactId>patreon</artifactId> <version>0.1.1</version> </dependency>` | ### Third Party Libraries Several third-party libraries are available, including: | * **Go**: View source on GitHub * **django-allauth**: Patreon is available as an OAuth backend * **python-social-auth**: Patreon is available as an OAuth backend | ### Clients and API Keys To authenticate with OAuth and interact with the Patreon API, you need to register your client and obtain a: | * **Client ID**: Used to identify your application * **Client Secret**: Used to authenticate your application * **Creator's Access Token**: Used to access the API in the context of the creator account * **Creator's Refresh Token**: Used to refresh new access tokens | ### OAuth Flow The OAuth flow involves the following steps: | 1. **Registering Your Client**: Register your client application on the Clients & API Keys page 2. **Making the Log In Button**: Create a "Log in with Patreon" button on your site that directs users to the Patreon authorization URL 3. **Handling OAuth Redirect**: Handle the redirect from Patreon to your site, which includes a code that can be exchanged for an access token 4. **Validating Receipt of the OAuth Token**: Exchange the code for an access token by sending a POST request to the Patreon token endpoint 5. **Using the OAuth Token**: Use the access token to make API calls to Patreon 6. **Resolving the OAuth Redirect**: Handle the redirect from Patreon to your site, which includes the access token and other user information | ### API Endpoints The Patreon API provides several endpoints for accessing user data, including: | * **User Profile**: Fetch the user's profile information * **Pledge History**: Fetch the user's pledge history * **Member Relationships**: Fetch the user's member relationships, including their campaign and tier information | ### OAuthClient Attributes An OAuthClient object has the following attributes: | | Attribute | Type | Description | | --- | --- | --- | | author_name | string | The author name provided during client setup | | client_secret | string | The client's secret | | default_scopes | string | (Deprecated) The client's default OAuth scopes for the authorization flow | | description | string | The description provided during client setup | | domain | string | The domain provided during client setup | | icon_url | string | The URL of the icon used in the OAuth authorization flow | | name | string | The name provided during client setup | | privacy_policy_url | string | The URL of the privacy policy provided during client setup | | redirect_uris | string | The allowable redirect URIs for the OAuth authorization flow | | tos_url | string | The URL of the terms of service provided during client setup | | version | integer | The Patreon API version the client is targeting | | ### Pledge Event Attributes A PledgeEvent object has the following attributes: | | Attribute | Type | Description | | --- | --- | --- | | amount_cents | integer | Amount (in the currency in which the patron paid) of the underlying event | | currency_code | string | ISO code of the currency of the event | | date | string (UTC ISO format) | The date which this event occurred | | pledge_payment_status | string | The payment status of the pledge | | payment_status | string | The payment status of the pledge |

Title: Obsidian Bases

Title: Introduction to Prompt Engineering
Summary: # Introduction to Prompt Engineering ## Overview Learn Prompting's introductory course on Generative AI and Prompt Engineering is a comprehensive guide for non-technical readers. The course covers the basics of Generative AI, Prompt Engineering, and provides valuable insights for more experienced readers. | ## Generative AI and Prompt Engineering Guide The guide is divided into several sections: * Basics: Introduction to Generative AI, ChatGPT, and Prompt Engineering * Applications: Text summarization, table generation, multiple choice questions, and more * Intermediate: Chain-of-thought prompting, zero-shot chain-of-thought, and self-consistency * Advanced: Introduction to zero-shot, emotion prompting, role prompting, and re-reading * Special Topics: Reliability, prompt hacking, and defensive measures * New Techniques: Aligned chain-of-thought, self-harmonized chain-of-thought, and logic-of-thought * Models: Introduction to OpenAI, FLUID, Stable Diffusion, and more * RAG: Retrieval-Augmented Generation, Auto-RAG, and Self-RAG * Agents: LLMs using tools, LLMs that reason and act, and code as reasoning * Prompt Tuning: Introduction to prompt tuning, soft prompts, and interpretable soft prompts * Language Model Inversion: Introduction to logit2prompt, output2prompt, and reverse prompt engineering * Tooling: Introduction to prompt engineering tools, IDEs, and playgrounds | ## Key Concepts * Generative AI: The world's hottest buzzword, used for creating new content * Prompt Engineering: The process of crafting effective prompts for Generative AI models * Chain-of-thought prompting: A technique used for generating more accurate and informative responses * Zero-shot chain-of-thought: A technique used for generating responses without any prior knowledge or context * Self-consistency: The ability of a model to generate consistent responses to similar prompts | ## Resources * Bibliography: A list of resources for further reading and learning * Prompted Products: A list of products that use Generative AI and Prompt Engineering * Additional Resources: A list of additional resources for learning and exploring Generative AI and Prompt Engineering * Hot Topics: A list of current and trending topics in the field of Generative AI and Prompt Engineering | ## Getting Started To get started with your Generative AI learning journey, click the Introduction to AI button at the bottom left of this page or visit the [Introduction to AI](/docs/basics/intro) page. | ## Conclusion Your feedback helps us improve the course for everyone. It's time to get started with your Generative AI learning journey. Explore the additional resources and advanced topics to dive deeper into the world of Generative AI and Prompt Engineering. | ### Table of Prompt Engineering Techniques | Technique | Description | | --- | --- | | Chain-of-thought prompting | Generating more accurate and informative responses | | Zero-shot chain-of-thought | Generating responses without any prior knowledge or context | | Self-consistency | Generating consistent responses to similar prompts | | Emotion prompting | Generating responses that evoke emotions | | Role prompting | Generating responses that mimic a specific role or personality | | Re-reading | Re-reading and revising responses to improve accuracy and clarity | | ### Table of Models | Model | Description | | --- | --- | | OpenAI | A leading AI model for natural language processing | | FLUID | A highly advanced AI model for natural language processing | | Stable Diffusion | A state-of-the-art AI model for image generation | | DALL-E | A highly advanced AI model for image generation |

Title: Untitled Content
Summary: ### Merged-Vicuna-RP-Stew-34B Model #### Overview The Merged-Vicuna-RP-Stew-34B model is a quantized 4.65 exl2 model that combines the capabilities of several base models. | #### Merge Details The model merge includes: * Nyakura SLERPed into CausalLM-RP for better chat/RP engagement * Nontoxic-Bagel SLERPed with PiVoT-SUS-RP for chat/RP and storytelling diversity * Tess-1.5 for better character/lore understanding * Capybara for general aptitude and preserving longer context length | #### Settings The model settings are as follows: * Temperature: 0.93 * Min-P: 0.02 * Typical-P: 0.9 * Repetition Penalty: 1.07 * Repetition Range: 2048 * Smoothing Factor: 0.39 * Smoothing Curve: 2 * Early Stopping: X * Do Sample: ✓ * Add BOS Token: X * Ban EOS Token: ✓ * Skip Special Tokens: ✓ * Temperature Last: ✓ * Custom Stopping Strings: "< / s >" | #### Chat Commandments To ensure universal stability, the following chat commandments must be followed: * Chat slowly in vivid detail about surroundings, actions, emotions, and stimuli sensations * Embrace the character's bio to stay consistent * Think about next proceedings step-by-step to maintain logical consistency * Use subtle physical cues and gestures to hint at the character's change in mood or attitude * Reference the user's persona, but assume and act out only the character's thoughts, dialogues, and intentions * Be whimsical, humorous, callous, fearful, or assaulting towards the user * Continue the conversation organically, without repeating or summarizing anything * Provide coherent responses based on recent and past events * Allow disturbing content, vulgar language, gory violence, psychological torment, and deviant sexual acts when relevant for realism * Focus on the established structure and stay in character | #### Models Merged The following models were included in the merge: * Nous-Capybara-34B * Tess-34B-v1.5b * Nontoxic-Bagel-34B * PiVoT-SUS-RP * NyakuraV2-34B-Yi-Llama * CausalLM-RP-34B * Yi-34B-200K-Llama | #### Configuration The YAML configuration used to produce this model is as follows: | Model | Weight | Density | | --- | --- | --- | | Nyakura-CausalLM-RP-34B | 0.16 | 0.42 | | Nontoxic-PiVoT-Bagel-RP-34B | 0.22 | 0.54 | | Tess-34B-v1.5b | 0.28 | 0.66 | | Nous-Capybara-34B-V1.9 | 0.34 | 0.78 | The merge method used is dare_ties, with the base model being Yi-34B-200K-Llama. The parameters used are int8_mask: true and dtype: bfloat16. | #### Downloads and Inference Providers The model had 3 downloads last month and is used for text generation.

Title: Model Context Protocol (MCP)
Summary: # Model Context Protocol (MCP) | ## Overview The Model Context Protocol (MCP) is an open, universal standard designed to connect AI assistants and language models to external data sources, tools, and systems such as databases, APIs, and documentation repositories. It enables AI models to access relevant contextual information and interact with external tools automatically to produce more accurate and relevant outputs. | ## Key Features and Capabilities | - **Universal Standard:** Replaces fragmented integrations with a common protocol for AI-to-data/tool connections. - **Tool Exposure & Invocation:** Servers expose tools with metadata and schemas that AI models can discover and call autonomously based on context and user inputs. - **Client-Server Architecture:** MCP clients embedded in AI apps communicate via JSON-RPC with MCP servers that host tools/functions, enabling secure and structured request-response exchanges. - **Human in the Loop:** Supports safety by requiring user confirmation and clear UI indicators when tools are invoked. - **Open Source and Extensible:** Community-driven ecosystem inviting developers and enterprises to build and share MCP servers and connectors. - **Integration Examples:** Connects to IDEs like Cursor, evaluation platforms like Galileo, business tools, and API platforms like Postman to extend AI capabilities with domain-specific data and functions. | ## Category MCP falls under the category of **AI integration protocols and middleware**, focusing on enhancing AI assistants with contextual information and tool capabilities through standardized, interoperable data and function access. | ## Use Cases and Target Audience | | Target Audience | Description | |-------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------| | AI Tool Developers and Researchers | Building assistants requiring real-time access to external datasets and services. | | Enterprises and Organizations | Leveraging existing internal data and business systems for AI-driven workflows. | | Developers | Integrating AI into IDEs, automation platforms, chatbots, and productivity tools. | | Early Adopters and Community Members| Contributing to or deploying open-source MCP servers for custom AI-driven applications.| | This structured markdown format provides a clear and scannable overview of the Model Context Protocol, highlighting its key features, categories, and use cases in a concise manner.

Title: Kartaverse: Computational Imaging for Immersive Media
Summary: # Kartaverse: Computational Imaging for Immersive Media | ## Overview Kartaverse is an academic research project dedicated to developing computational imaging techniques for a post-production pipeline optimized for immersive media, such as VR and fulldome content. It integrates with Blackmagic Design's Fusion Studio and DaVinci Resolve Studio, facilitating high-quality panoramic video stitching, editing, and remapping for XR content creation. | ## Key Features and Capabilities | - **Panoramic Video Processing:** - Stitching, compositing, retouching, and remapping panoramic videos from any projection to any projection (e.g., 360VR, depthmap, lightfield). | - **Workflow Tools:** - Scripts, templates, plugins, and command-line tools to streamline XR media workflows. | - **Advanced Editing:** - VR editing tools integrated into DaVinci Resolve’s Fusion page for advanced compositing and motion graphics tailored to immersive video. | - **Stabilization:** - Supports video stabilization using gyro metadata, collaborating with open-source projects like Gyroflow. | - **Open-Source Distribution:** - Licensed under GNU LGPL/GPL v3, distributed via the Reactor package manager for easy installation and integration with Resolve/Fusion. | - **Spatial Metadata Tools:** - Includes a spatial metadata GUI tool for efficient handling of video metadata, with localization and export customization features. | ## Main Category Computational Imaging and Immersive Media Post-Production Software/Toolkit. | ## Use Cases and Target Audience | - **Content Creators:** - VR and immersive video creators seeking high-quality stitching, editing, and compositing tools for 360-degree and panoramic videos. | - **Media Professionals:** - Users of DaVinci Resolve Studio and Fusion Studio aiming to enhance VR video workflows, particularly for HDR and non-standard lens footage like Canon dual fisheye cameras. | - **Researchers and Developers:** - Individuals interested in computational imaging techniques and open-source tools for spatial video metadata management and stabilization. | - **XR Content Developers:** - Creators producing content for VR headsets and fulldome theaters in an accessible and affordable manner.

They are a core feature of the CodePen platform, which serves as a social development environment for front-end code experimentation and sharing. | ## Target Audience | - **Developers & Designers:** For showcasing work, organizing projects, or curating learning resources. - **Teams:** To manage shared code resources, references, or internal tools. - **Educators & Learners:** To compile tutorials, examples, or assignment submissions. - **Portfolio Builders:** To present a cohesive body of work to potential employers or clients. | ## Summary | CodePen Collections are versatile tools for organizing and sharing code, leveraging the platform's social and collaborative features. While the "Poerqa" collection is not detailed in the provided results, the general functionality of CodePen Collections can be utilized to achieve a wide range of organizational and sharing goals. For specific information on "Poerqa," direct access to the collection's URL is required.

Title: AI Article Generation System
Summary: ### Introduction to STORM STORM (Synthesis of Topic Outlines through Retrieval and Multi-perspective Question Asking) is a system that generates Wikipedia-like articles from scratch based on internet search. Co-STORM enhances STORM by enabling human-AI collaborative knowledge curation. | ### Key Features * Generates articles with citations * Supports human-AI collaborative knowledge curation * Modular design for customization * Integrates with various language models and search engines | ### Latest News * Added litellm integration for language models and embedding models in knowledge-storm v1.1.0 (2025/01) * Released Co-STORM codebase and integrated it into knowledge-storm python package v1.0.0 (2024/09) * Introduced collaborative STORM (Co-STORM) for human-AI collaborative knowledge curation (2024/09) | ### How STORM Works 1. **Pre-writing stage**: Conducts internet-based research to collect references and generates an outline. 2. **Writing stage**: Uses the outline and references to generate the full-length article with citations. 3. **Perspective-Guided Question Asking**: Discovers different perspectives by surveying existing articles from similar topics. 4. **Simulated Conversation**: Simulates a conversation between a Wikipedia writer and a topic expert grounded in internet sources. | ### How Co-STORM Works 1. **Collaborative Discourse Protocol**: Implements a turn management policy to support smooth collaboration among Co-STORM LLM experts, moderator, and human user. 2. **Dynamic Updated Mind Map**: Organizes collected information into a hierarchical concept structure to build a shared conceptual space between the human user and the system. | ### Installation To install the knowledge storm library, use `pip install knowledge-storm`. | ### API The package supports: * Language model components: All language models supported by litellm * Embedding model components: All embedding models supported by litellm * Retrieval module components: YouRM, BingSearch, VectorRM, and more | ### Examples * Run STORM with gpt family models: `python examples/storm_examples/run_storm_wiki_gpt.py` * Run Co-STORM with gpt family models: `python examples/costorm_examples/run_costorm_gpt.py` | ### Customization STORM engine consists of 4 modules: 1. **Knowledge Curation Module**: Collects a broad coverage of information about the given topic. 2. **Outline Generation Module**: Organizes the collected information by generating a hierarchical outline for the curated knowledge. 3. **Article Generation Module**: Populates the generated outline with the collected information. 4. **Article Polishing Module**: Refines and enhances the written article for better presentation. | Co-STORM can also be customized by modifying the implementation of the modules according to specific requirements. | ### Tables | | Module | Description | | --- | --- | | Knowledge Curation Module | Collects a broad coverage of information about the given topic | | Outline Generation Module | Organizes the collected information by generating a hierarchical outline for the curated knowledge | | Article Generation Module | Populates the generated outline with the collected information | | Article Polishing Module | Refines and enhances the written article for better presentation | | | Retrieval Module | Description | | --- | --- | | YouRM | Uses You.com search engine | | BingSearch | Uses Bing search engine | | VectorRM | Uses vector-based search engine | | SerperRM | Uses Serper search engine | | BraveRM | Uses Brave search engine | | SearXNG | Uses SearXNG search engine | | DuckDuckGoSearchRM | Uses DuckDuckGo search engine | | TavilySearchRM | Uses Tavily search engine | | GoogleSearch | Uses Google search engine | | AzureAISearch | Uses Azure AI search engine |

Title: Introduction to llama.cpp
Summary: # Introduction to llama.cpp llama.cpp is a C/C++ implementation of LLM inference with minimal setup and state-of-the-art performance on a wide range of hardware, locally and in the cloud. | ## Key Features * Plain C/C++ implementation without dependencies * Optimized for Apple silicon using ARM NEON, Accelerate, and Metal frameworks * Supports AVX, AVX2, AVX512, and AMX for x86 architectures * 1.5-bit to 8-bit integer quantization for faster inference and reduced memory use * Custom CUDA kernels for running LLMs on NVIDIA GPUs * Vulkan and SYCL backend support * CPU+GPU hybrid inference for partially accelerating models larger than VRAM capacity | ## Models The following models are supported: * LLaMA * LLaMA 2 * LLaMA 3 * Mistral 7B * Mixtral MoE * DBRX * Falcon * Chinese LLaMA / Alpaca * Vigogne (French) * BERT * Koala * Baichuan 1 & 2 * Aquila 1 & 2 * Starcoder models * Refact * MPT * Bloom * Yi models * StableLM models * Deepseek models * Qwen models * PLaMo-13B * Phi models * PhiMoE * GPT-2 * Orion 14B * InternLM2 * CodeShell * Gemma * Mamba * Grok-1 * Xverse * Command-R models * SEA-LION * GritLM-7B * OLMo * OLMo 2 * OLMoE * Granite models * GPT-NeoX * Pythia * Snowflake-Arctic MoE * Smaug * Poro 34B * Bitnet b1.58 models * Flan T5 * Open Elm models * ChatGLM3-6b * ChatGLM4-9b * GLMEdge-1.5b * GLMEdge-4b * GLM-4-0414 * SmolLM * EXAONE-3.0-7.8B-Instruct * FalconMamba Models * Jais * Bielik-11B-v2.3 * RWKV-6 * QRWKV-6 * GigaChat-20B-A3B * Trillion-7B-preview * Ling models * LFM2 models * Hunyuan models * BailingMoeV2 (Ring/Ling 2.0) models * Multimodal LLaVA 1.5 models * LLaVA 1.6 models * BakLLaVA * Obsidian * ShareGPT4V * MobileVLM 1.7B/3B models * Yi-VL * Mini CPM * Moondream * Bunny * GLM-EDGE * Qwen2-VL * LFM2-VL | ## Bindings llama.cpp has bindings for the following languages: * Python: ddh0/easy-llama, abetlen/llama-cpp-python * Go: go-skynet/go-llama.cpp * Node.js: withcatai/node-llama-cpp * JS/TS: lgrammel/modelfusion, offline-ai/cli * JavaScript/Wasm: tangledgroup/llama-cpp-wasm * Typescript/Wasm: ngxson/wllama * Ruby: yoshoku/llama_cpp.rb * Rust: edgenai/llama_cpp-rs, mdrokz/rust-llama.cpp, utilityai/llama-cpp-rs, ShelbyJenkins/llm_client * C#/.NET: SciSharp/LLamaSharp, LM-Kit.NET * Scala 3: donderom/llm4s * Clojure: phronmophobic/llama.clj * React Native: mybigday/llama.rn * Java: kherud/java-llama.cpp, QuasarByte/llama-cpp-jna * Zig: deins/llama.cpp.zig * Flutter/Dart: netdur/llama_cpp_dart, xuegao-tzx/Fllama * PHP: distantmagic/resonance * Guile Scheme: guile_llama_cpp * Swift: srgtuszy/llama-cpp-swift, ShenghaiWang/SwiftLlama * Delphi: Embarcadero/llama-cpp-delphi * Go (no CGo needed): hybridgroup/yzma | ## UIs The following UIs are available: * AI Sublime Text plugin * cztomsik/ava * Dot * eva * iohub/collama * janhq/jan * johnbean393/Sidekick * KanTV * KodiBot * llama.vim * LARS * Llama Assistant * LLMFarm * LLMUnity * LMStudio * LocalAI * LostRuins/koboldcpp * MindMac * MindWorkAI/AI-Studio * Mobile-Artificial-Intelligence/maid * Mozilla-Ocho/llamafile * nat/openplayground * nomic-ai/gpt4all * ollama/ollama * oobabooga/text-generation-webui * PocketPal AI * psugihara/FreeChat * ptsochantaris/emeltal * pythops/tenere * ramalama * semperai/amica * withcatai/catai * Autopen | ## Tools The following tools are available: * akx/ggify: download PyTorch models from HuggingFace Hub and convert them to GGML * akx/ollama-dl: download models from the Ollama library to be used directly with llama.cpp * crashr/gppm: launch llama.cpp instances utilizing NVIDIA Tesla P40 or P100 GPUs with reduced idle power consumption * gpustack/gguf-parser: review/check the GGUF file and estimate the memory usage * Styled Lines: proprietary licensed, async wrapper of inference part for game development in Unity3d with pre-built Mobile and Web platform wrappers and a model example | ## Infrastructure The following infrastructure is available: * Paddler: Open-source LLMOps platform for hosting and scaling AI in your own infrastructure * GPUStack: Manage GPU clusters for running LLMs * llama_cpp_canister: llama.cpp as a smart contract on the Internet Computer, using WebAssembly * llama-swap: transparent proxy that adds automatic model switching with llama-server * Kalavai: Crowdsource end to end LLM deployment at any scale * llmaz: Easy, advanced inference platform for large language models on Kubernetes | ## Games The following games are available: * Lucy's Labyrinth: A simple maze game where agents controlled by an AI model will try to trick you | ## Supported Backends The following backends are supported: | Backend | Target Devices | | --- | --- | | Metal | Apple Silicon | | BLAS | All | | BLIS | All | | SYCL | Intel and Nvidia GPU | | MUSA | Moore Threads GPU | | CUDA | Nvidia GPU | | HIP | AMD GPU | | Vulkan | GPU | | CANN | Ascend NPU | | OpenCL | Adreno GPU | | IBM zDNN | IBM Z & LinuxONE | | WebGPU | All | | RPC | All | | ## Obtaining and Quantizing Models Models can be obtained from the Hugging Face platform. The GGUF format is supported, and models can be quantized using the GGUF-editor space. | ## llama-cli llama-cli is a CLI tool for accessing and experimenting with most of llama.cpp's functionality. It can be used to run models in conversation mode, perform text completion, and constrain output with a custom grammar. | ## llama-server llama-server is a lightweight, OpenAI API compatible, HTTP server for serving LLMs. It can be used to start a local HTTP server, support multiple users and parallel decoding, and enable speculative decoding.

Title: Khoj: Your AI Second Brain
Summary: # Khoj: Your AI Second Brain | ## Overview | - **Khoj** is a personal AI app designed to extend your capabilities. - Scales from on-device personal AI to cloud-scale enterprise AI. - **Key Features:** - Chat with local or online LLMs (e.g., llama3, qwen, gemma, mistral, gpt, claude, gemini, deepseek). - Get answers from the internet and your documents (including image, PDF, markdown, org-mode, Word, Notion files). - Access via Browser, Obsidian, Emacs, Desktop, Phone, or WhatsApp. - Create custom agents with specific knowledge, persona, chat model, and tools. - Automate repetitive research tasks. - Receive personal newsletters and smart notifications. - Advanced semantic search for quick document retrieval. - Generate images, text-to-speech, and play messages. - Open-source and self-hostable. | ## Access Khoj | | Platform | Link/Action | |----------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------| | **Cloud App** | [https://app.khoj.dev](https://app.khoj.dev) | | **Self-Host** | [Read the docs](https://github.com/khoj-ai/khoj) | | **Enterprise** | [Visit our website](https://khoj.dev/enterprise) | | ## Full Feature List | - Detailed features can be found [here](https://github.com/khoj-ai/khoj). | ## Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ) | | Question | Answer | |---------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | **Can I use Khoj without self-hosting?** | Yes, use it at [https://app.khoj.dev](https://app.khoj.dev) — no setup required. | | **What kinds of documents can Khoj read?** | Supports PDFs, Markdown, Notion, Word docs, org-mode files, and more. | | **How can I make my own agent?** | Follow [this blog post](https://khoj.dev/blog/custom-agents) for a step-by-step guide. | | For more questions, visit our [Discord](https://discord.gg/khoj). | ## Contributors | - **Contributors List**: Made with [contrib.rocks](https://contrib.rocks). | ## Contributing | - **Why Contribute?** - Make an impact by building, testing, and improving a widely-used productivity tool. - Learn and grow with cutting-edge AI, LLMs, and semantic search technologies. - **How to Contribute:** - Help build new features. - Improve project documentation. - Report issues and fix bugs. - See [Contributing Guidelines](https://github.com/khoj-ai/khoj/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) and [good first issues](https://github.com/khoj-ai/khoj/issues?q=label%3A%22good+first+issue%22) to get started.

Title: downloads / loads a 4.66GB LLM
Summary: ### GPT4All Overview GPT4All is a large language model (LLM) that can be run privately on everyday desktops and laptops without requiring API calls or GPUs. | ### Key Features * Runs LLMs privately on desktops and laptops * No API calls or GPUs required * Supports DeepSeek R1 Distillations * Available for Windows, macOS, and Ubuntu | ### System Requirements | Platform | Requirements | | --- | --- | | Windows | Intel Core i3 2nd Gen / AMD Bulldozer, or better | | Windows ARM | Qualcomm Snapdragon and Microsoft SQ1/SQ2 processors | | Linux | x86-64 only (no ARM), Intel Core i3 2nd Gen / AMD Bulldozer, or better | | macOS | Monterey 12.6 or newer, best results with Apple Silicon M-series processors | | ### Download Links * Windows Installer * Windows ARM Installer * macOS Installer * Ubuntu Installer | ### Python Client The GPT4All Python client provides access to LLMs using `llama.cpp` implementations. ```python pip install gpt4all from gpt4all import GPT4All model = GPT4All("Meta-Llama-3-8B-Instruct.Q4_0.gguf") # downloads / loads a 4.66GB LLM with model.chat_session(): print(model.generate("How can I run LLMs efficiently on my laptop?", max_tokens=1024)) ``` | ### Integrations * Langchain * Weaviate Vector Database * OpenLIT (OTel-native Monitoring) | ### Release History * July 2nd, 2024: V3.0.0 Release 	+ Fresh redesign of the chat application UI 	+ Improved user workflow for LocalDocs 	+ Expanded access to more model architectures * October 19th, 2023: GGUF Support Launches 	+ Mistral 7b base model 	+ Updated model gallery on the website 	+ Several new local code models * September 18th, 2023: Nomic Vulkan launches 	+ Supporting local LLM inference on NVIDIA and AMD GPUs * July 2023: Stable support for LocalDocs * June 28th, 2023: Docker-based API server launches | ### Contributing GPT4All welcomes contributions from the open source community. Please see CONTRIBUTING.md and follow the issues, bug reports, and PR markdown templates. | ### Citation If you utilize this repository, models or data in a downstream project, please consider citing it with: ``` @misc{gpt4all, author = {Yuvanesh Anand and Zach Nussbaum and Brandon Duderstadt and Benjamin Schmidt and Andriy Mulyar}, title = {GPT4All: Training an Assistant-style Chatbot with Large Scale Data Distillation from GPT-3.5-Turbo}, year = {2023}, publisher = {GitHub}, journal = {GitHub repository}, howpublished = {\url{https://github.com/nomic-ai/gpt4all}}, } ```

Title: Introduction to Ollama
Summary: # Introduction to Ollama Ollama is a lightweight, extensible framework for building and running language models on the local machine. It provides a simple API for creating, running, and managing models. | ## Installation To get started with Ollama, follow these installation steps: * macOS: Download the installer * Windows: Download the installer * Linux: Run `curl -fsSL https://ollama.com/install.sh | sh` * Docker: Use the official Ollama Docker image `ollama/ollama` | ## Libraries Ollama has libraries for Python (`ollama-python`) and JavaScript (`ollama-js`). | ## Community Join the Ollama community on: * Discord * Reddit | ## Quickstart To run and chat with a model, use the command `ollama run <model_name>`. For example, to run the `gemma3` model, use `ollama run gemma3`. | ## Model Library Ollama supports a list of models available on [the Ollama library](#). Some example models include: | Model | Parameters | Size | Download | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | Gemma 3 | 1B | 815MB | `ollama run gemma3:1b` | | Gemma 3 | 4B | 3.3GB | `ollama run gemma3` | | Gemma 3 | 12B | 8.1GB | `ollama run gemma3:12b` | | QwQ | 32B | 20GB | `ollama run qwq` | | DeepSeek-R1 | 7B | 4.7GB | `ollama run deepseek-r1` | | Llama 4 | 109B | 67GB | `ollama run llama4:scout` | | ## Customizing a Model To customize a model, create a `Modelfile` with a `FROM` instruction and specify the local filepath to the model. Then, create and run the model using `ollama create` and `ollama run`. | ## CLI Reference Some useful Ollama CLI commands include: * `ollama create`: Create a model from a Modelfile * `ollama pull`: Pull a model from the Ollama library * `ollama rm`: Remove a model * `ollama cp`: Copy a model * `ollama run`: Run a model * `ollama show`: Show model information * `ollama list`: List models on your computer * `ollama ps`: List models currently loaded * `ollama stop`: Stop a model currently running * `ollama serve`: Start the Ollama server | ## REST API Ollama has a REST API for running and managing models. You can generate a response using `curl http://localhost:11434/api/generate` or chat with a model using `curl http://localhost:11434/api/chat`. | ## Community Integrations Ollama has a wide range of community integrations, including: * Web & Desktop: Open WebUI, SwiftChat, Enchanted, and more * Terminal: oterm, Ellama Emacs client, and more * Cloud: Google Cloud, Fly.io, Koyeb, and more | ## Additional Tools and Resources Some additional tools and resources for Ollama include: * `ollama-co2`: A FastAPI web interface for monitoring and managing local and remote Ollama servers * `orc-cli`: A command-line application for browsing, pulling, and downloading models from the Ollama Registry * `GGUF-to-Ollama`: A tool for importing GGUF models into Ollama * `ollama-multirun`: A bash shell script for running a single prompt against multiple Ollama models * `ollama-bash-toolshed`: A collection of bash scripts for chatting with tools using Ollama models

Title: OpenAI Assistant Swarm Manager Library
Summary: ### Introduction to OpenAI Assistant Swarm Manager The OpenAI Assistant Swarm Manager is a library that enables developers to manage multiple custom agents and their functions via a single API call. This library acts as an extension of the OpenAI NodeJS SDK, providing a new `.swarm` method available on `beta.assistants`. | ### How it Works To use the Swarm Manager, follow these steps: * Install the OpenAI SDK for NodeJS using `yarn add openai` or `npm install openai`. * Install the `openai-assistant-swarm` package using `yarn add @mintplex-labs/openai-assistant-swarm` or `npm install @mintplex-labs/openai-assistant-swarm`. * Enable the client for OpenAI and extend the OpenAI SDK with the Swarm Manager using `EnableSwarmAbilities`. * Initialize the swarm manager to create and register it with your account. | ### Key Features * Delegation via prompt: easily delegate work to one or many assistants in parallel. * Get all available assistants: retrieve a list of all available assistants with a single call. * Get many known assistants at once: fetch multiple assistants by their IDs. | ### Available Tools The following tools are available: * Delegation via prompt: `OpenAIClient.beta.assistants.swarm.delegateWithPrompt` * Get all available assistants: `OpenAIClient.beta.assistants.swarm.allAssistants` * Get many known assistants at once: `OpenAIClient.beta.assistants.swarm.getAssistants` * Event listeners: `OpenAIClient.beta.assistants.swarm.emitter.on` for events such as `parent_assistant_complete`, `child_assistants_complete`, and `poll_event` | ### Example Usage ```javascript const OpenAIClient = new OpenAI({ apiKey: process.env.OPEN_AI_KEY }); EnableSwarmAbilities(OpenAIClient); await OpenAIClient.beta.assistants.swarm.init(); | const response = OpenAIClient.beta.assistants.swarm.delegateWithPrompt('What is the weather in New York city right now? Also what is the top stock for today?'); console.log({ parentRun: response.parentRun, subRuns: response.subRuns, }); ``` | ### Event Listeners You can set up event listeners to handle events such as: * `parent_assistant_complete`: when the parent response is completed. * `child_assistants_complete`: when the delegated assistant responses are completed. * `poll_event`: to see every step event as it is completed. | ### API Reference The following API methods are available: | Method | Description | | --- | --- | | `delegateWithPrompt` | Delegate work to one or many assistants in parallel. | | `allAssistants` | Retrieve a list of all available assistants. | | `getAssistants` | Fetch multiple assistants by their IDs. | | `init` | Initialize the swarm manager. | | `emitter.on` | Set up event listeners for events such as `parent_assistant_complete`, `child_assistants_complete`, and `poll_event`. |

Title: All-in-One AI Chat Application
Summary: ### Introduction to AnythingLLM AnythingLLM is a full-stack application that enables users to turn any document, resource, or piece of content into context that any Large Language Model (LLM) can use as a reference during chatting. It supports commercial off-the-shelf LLMs and popular open-source LLMs and vectorDB solutions. | ### Key Features * Full MCP-compatibility * No-code AI Agent builder * Multi-modal support (both closed and open-source LLMs) * Custom AI Agents * Multi-user instance support and permissioning * Docker version only: Agents inside your workspace (browse the web, etc) * Custom Embeddable Chat widget for your website (Docker version only) * Multiple document type support (PDF, TXT, DOCX, etc) * Simple chat UI with Drag-n-Drop functionality and clear citations * 100% Cloud deployment ready * Works with all popular closed and open-source LLM providers * Built-in cost & time-saving measures for managing very large documents * Full Developer API for custom integrations | ### Supported Models and Databases #### Large Language Models (LLMs) * Any open-source llama.cpp compatible model * OpenAI * OpenAI (Generic) * Azure OpenAI * AWS Bedrock * Anthropic * NVIDIA NIM (chat models) * Google Gemini Pro * Hugging Face (chat models) * Ollama (chat models) * LM Studio (all models) * LocalAI (all models) * Together AI (chat models) * Fireworks AI (chat models) * Perplexity (chat models) * OpenRouter (chat models) * DeepSeek (chat models) * Mistral * Groq * Cohere * KoboldCPP * LiteLLM * Text Generation Web UI * Apipie * xAI * Novita AI (chat models) * PPIO * Moonshot AI * Microsoft Foundry Local * CometAPI (chat models) | #### Embedder Models * AnythingLLM Native Embedder (default) * OpenAI * Azure OpenAI * LocalAI (all) * Ollama (all) * LM Studio (all) * Cohere | #### Audio Transcription Models * AnythingLLM Built-in (default) * OpenAI | #### TTS (Text-to-Speech) Support * Native Browser Built-in (default) * PiperTTSLocal - runs in browser * OpenAI TTS * ElevenLabs * Any OpenAI Compatible TTS service | #### STT (Speech-to-Text) Support * Native Browser Built-in (default) | #### Vector Databases * LanceDB (default) * PGVector * Astra DB * Pinecone * Chroma & ChromaCloud * Weaviate * Qdrant * Milvus * Zilliz | ### Technical Overview The AnythingLLM monorepo consists of six main sections: 1. **Frontend**: A viteJS + React frontend for creating and managing content. 2. **Server**: A NodeJS express server for handling interactions and vectorDB management. 3. **Collector**: A NodeJS express server for processing and parsing documents. 4. **Docker**: Docker instructions and build process for building from source. 5. **Embed**: A submodule for generating and creating the web embed widget. 6. **Browser-Extension**: A submodule for the Chrome browser extension. | ### Self-Hosting AnythingLLM can be deployed on various environments, including: | Environment | Deployment Method | | --- | --- | | Docker | Follow Docker instructions | | AWS | Use AWS deployment scripts | | GCP | Use GCP deployment scripts | | Digital Ocean | Use Digital Ocean deployment scripts | | Render.com | Use Render.com deployment scripts | | Railway | Use Railway deployment scripts | | RepoCloud | Use RepoCloud deployment scripts | | Elestio | Use Elestio deployment scripts | | ### Setup for Development To set up AnythingLLM for development, run the following commands: * `yarn setup` to fill in the required `.env` files * `yarn dev:server` to boot the server locally * `yarn dev:frontend` to boot the frontend locally * `yarn dev:collector` to run the document collector | ### External Apps and Integrations The following external apps are compatible with AnythingLLM: * Midori AI Subsystem Manager * Coolify * GPTLocalhost for Microsoft Word | ### Telemetry and Privacy AnythingLLM collects anonymous usage information to improve the product and prioritize work on new features and bug fixes. The telemetry feature can be opt-out by setting `DISABLE_TELEMETRY` to "true" in the server or Docker `.env` settings. | ### Contributing Contributions to AnythingLLM are welcome. Please refer to the contributing guide for more information. | ### Sponsors and Contributors AnythingLLM is sponsored by various organizations and individuals. A list of contributors can be found on the project's website. | ### More Products Mintplex Labs offers other products, including: * VectorAdmin: An all-in-one GUI and tool-suite for managing vector databases. * OpenAI Assistant Swarm: A tool for turning your entire library of OpenAI assistants into one single army commanded from a single agent. | ### License AnythingLLM is MIT licensed.

Title: Private LLM Desktop Application
Summary: ### Introduction to GPT4All GPT4All is an application that runs large language models (LLMs) privately on desktops and laptops without requiring API calls or GPUs. | ### Key Features * Runs LLMs privately on everyday desktops and laptops * No API calls or GPUs required * Supports DeepSeek R1 Distillations * Available for Windows, macOS, and Ubuntu | ### Download Links * Windows Installer * Windows ARM Installer * macOS Installer * Ubuntu Installer | ### System Requirements The following system requirements apply: * Windows and Linux builds require Intel Core i3 2nd Gen / AMD Bulldozer, or better * Windows ARM build supports Qualcomm Snapdragon and Microsoft SQ1/SQ2 processors * Linux build is x86-64 only (no ARM) * macOS build requires Monterey 12.6 or newer, with best results on Apple Silicon M-series processors | ### Installation GPT4All can be installed using the following methods: * Download the installer for your operating system * Install GPT4All Python using `pip install gpt4all` * Use the Flathub community-maintained installation | ### Python Client The GPT4All Python client provides access to LLMs using `llama.cpp` implementations. Please see CONTRIBUTING.md and follow the issues, bug reports, and PR markdown templates. | ### Citation If you utilize this repository, models, or data in a downstream project, please consider citing it with: ```bib @misc{gpt4all, author = {Yuvanesh Anand and Zach Nussbaum and Brandon Duderstadt and Benjamin Schmidt and Andriy Mulyar}, title = {GPT4All: Training an Assistant-style Chatbot with Large Scale Data Distillation from GPT-3.5-Turbo}, year = {2023}, publisher = {GitHub}, journal = {GitHub repository}, howpublished = {\url{https://github.com/nomic-ai/gpt4all}}, } ```

Title: Introduction to AutoGroq

Title: Motion: JavaScript and React Animation Library
Summary: # Motion: JavaScript and React Animation Library | ## Overview Motion is a modern, production-grade JavaScript and React animation library designed to create smooth, customizable UI animations. It operates without the need for additional UI libraries, offering a simple API and advanced features such as gesture support and a vault of pre-built animated components suitable for landing pages and dashboards. | ## Key Features - **Customization:** 100% customizable animations with control over independent transforms (x, y, rotateZ, etc.) - **Standalone Operation:** Works standalone with React, JavaScript, and Vue without additional UI libraries - **Pre-built Components:** A vault of 290+ ready-to-use animations for easy integration - **Advanced Toolkit:** Motion+ toolkit offering features like magnetic cursors and infinite scrolling tickers - **Animation Types:** Supports scroll animations, exit animations via AnimatePresence - **Gesture Support:** Includes hover, press, and drag gestures - **Layout Transitions:** Smooth transitions between layouts with a built-in layout animation engine - **Timeline Control:** Variants, stagger, and sequence control for animations - **Physics:** Realistic spring physics for natural motion - **Visual Editor:** In alpha, allows direct editing of bezier curves - **Community Resources:** Includes tutorials and professional workflow tools | ## Main Category Motion is categorized as a JavaScript UI animation library specifically optimized for React but is also compatible with JavaScript and Vue frameworks. | ## Target Audience - **Frontend Developers** building landing pages, dashboards, and interactive web applications - **Development Teams** seeking fast, production-ready, customizable animated UI components - **Developers** needing intuitive APIs with complex motion capabilities like gestures and layout transitions - **Individuals** looking to enhance user experience through engaging animations - **Designers & Developers** who want to streamline animation workflows with pre-built components and VS Code integration | ## Related Libraries Other libraries known as "Motion UI" focus more on CSS transitions and animations, such as the Sass-based Motion UI from Zurb/Foundation, which uses pre-made effect classes. In contrast, Motion for React/JS provides 100% customizable animations and does not require additional UI libraries.

Title: Racemic Mixture
Summary: # Racemic Mixture | ## Brief Description A racemic mixture (or racemate) is a chemical mixture containing equal amounts of two enantiomers, which are chiral molecules that are non-superimposable mirror images of each other. This mixture is optically inactive because the two enantiomers rotate plane-polarized light in equal but opposite directions. | ## Key Features | - **Composition**: 50:50 mix of enantiomers - **Optical Activity**: Optically inactive - **Formation**: Created by mixing equal quantities of pure enantiomers or through racemization processes | ## Category Chemistry, specifically Stereochemistry, focused on the study of chiral molecules and their properties. | ## Use Cases and Target Audience | | Use Case/Audience | Description | |-----------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | Pharmaceuticals | Crucial in drug development, as chiral drugs may have only one therapeutically active or safe enantiomer. | | Chemical Synthesis | Employed in preparing chiral compounds when both enantiomers are desired or when separating enantiomers is difficult. | | Researchers and Students| Studied in chemistry and pharmacy to understand chirality and its implications in chemical reactions and biological activity. | | ## References - [1][5][7][11][15] (Original content references)

Title: YouTube Search API Request
Summary: ### YouTube Search API #### Overview The YouTube Search API returns a collection of search results that match the query parameters specified in the API request. By default, a search result set identifies matching video, channel, and playlist resources. | #### Quota Impact * A call to this method has a quota cost of 100 units. | #### Common Use Cases * Requesting search results based on specific query parameters | ### Request #### HTTP Request * `GET https://www.googleapis.com/youtube/v3/search` | #### Parameters The following table lists the parameters that this query supports: | | Parameter | Type | Description | | --- | --- | --- | | part | string | Specifies a comma-separated list of one or more search resource properties that the API response will include. | | forContentOwner | boolean | Restricts the search to only retrieve videos owned by the content owner identified by the `onBehalfOfContentOwner` parameter. | | forDeveloper | boolean | Restricts the search to only retrieve videos uploaded via the developer's application or website. | | forMine | boolean | Restricts the search to only retrieve videos owned by the authenticated user. | | channelId | string | Indicates that the API response should only contain resources created by the channel. | | channelType | string | Lets you restrict a search to a particular type of channel. | | eventType | string | Restricts a search to broadcast events. | | location | string | Defines a circular geographic area and restricts a search to videos that specify, in their metadata, a geographic location that falls within that area. | | locationRadius | string | Specifies the maximum distance that the location associated with a video can be from the point specified in the `location` parameter. | | maxResults | unsigned integer | Specifies the maximum number of items that should be returned in the result set. | | onBehalfOfContentOwner | string | Indicates that the request's authorization credentials identify a YouTube CMS user who is acting on behalf of the content owner specified in the parameter value. | | order | string | Specifies the method that will be used to order resources in the API response. | | pageToken | string | Identifies a specific page in the result set that should be returned. | | publishedAfter | datetime | Indicates that the API response should only contain resources created at or after the specified date and time. | | publishedBefore | datetime | Indicates that the API response should only contain resources created before the specified date and time. | | q | string | Specifies the query term or phrase to search for. | | regionCode | string | Lets you restrict search results to videos that are viewable in a specific geographic region. | | relatedToVideoId | string | Retrieves a list of videos that are related to the video specified by the `relatedToVideoId` parameter. | | type | string | Restricts a search to a particular type of resource. | | videoCaption | string | Filters search results to only include videos that have captions. | | videoCategory | string | Filters search results to only include videos that have a specific category. | | videoDefinition | string | Filters search results to only include videos that have a specific definition. | | videoDimension | string | Filters search results to only include videos that have a specific dimension. | | videoDuration | string | Filters search results to only include videos that have a specific duration. | | videoEmbeddable | string | Lets you restrict a search to only videos that can be embedded into a webpage. | | videoLicense | string | Filters search results to only include videos with a particular license. | | videoPaidProductPlacement | string | Filters search results to only include videos that the creator has denoted as having a paid promotion. | | videoSyndicated | string | Lets you restrict a search to only videos that can be played outside youtube.com. | | videoType | string | Lets you restrict a search to a particular type of videos. | | ### Optional Parameters * The following parameters are optional and can be used to further restrict the search results: + `channelId` + `channelType` + `eventType` + `location` + `locationRadius` + `maxResults` + `onBehalfOfContentOwner` + `order` + `pageToken` + `publishedAfter` + `publishedBefore` + `q` + `regionCode` + `relatedToVideoId` + `type` + `videoCaption` + `videoCategory` + `videoDefinition` + `videoDimension` + `videoDuration` + `videoEmbeddable` + `videoLicense` + `videoPaidProductPlacement` + `videoSyndicated` + `videoType` | ### Request Body * Do not provide a request body when calling this method. | ### Response * If successful, this method returns a response body with the following structure: ```json { "kind": "youtube#searchListResponse", "etag": etag, "nextPageToken": string, "prevPageToken": string, "regionCode": string, "pageInfo": { "totalResults": integer, "resultsPerPage": integer }, "items": [ search Resource ] } ``` #### Properties * The following properties are returned in the response: + `kind` + `etag` + `nextPageToken` + `prevPageToken` + `regionCode` + `pageInfo` + `items`

Title: AI Browser Automation Project
Summary: ### Installation To install the application, follow these steps: * Create a Firebase account and project * Generate a private key and save it in `firebaseAdmin/cert/dev.json` for development or `prod.json` for production * Install dependencies by running `npm install` * Configure environment variables by copying `.env.sample` to `.env.production` or `.env.development` and filling in your credentials * Run the following commands: * `npm run db:deploy` * `npm run dev` for development * `npm run build` and `npm run start` for production | ### How it Works The application solves two main problems with current browser agents: * **Problem 1: Finding the Right Element**: The application indexes the entire DOM in MeiliSearch, allowing GPT-4-vision to generate commands for element interactions. It then searches the index with the generated text and retrieves the element ID to send back to the browser. * **Problem 2: GPT Derailing from Workflow**: The application uses a technique called Actions Augmented Generation, which records DOM element changes for every user action and generates a set of tasks based on the workflow title, objective, and recorded actions. | ### Stack The application is built using the following technologies: * Next.js * Rust * Postgres * MeiliSearch * Firebase Auth | ### Roadmap The following features are planned for future development: * Workflows * Chat with what you see * More actions support (scrolling, opening links in a new tab, etc.) * Loop in workflows * Clever Tab management * Share workflows * Open source models support * Community shared workflows * Cloud version of AI Employe * Control browser by text * Control browser by voice | ### Building and Loading the Extension After running `npm run dev` or `npm run build`, the extension will be built inside the `./client/extension/build` folder. This folder can be loaded as an unpacked extension in your browser.

Submit an issue [here](https://github.com/intel/openvino-plugins-ai-audacity) to help us improve this project. | ## Contribution Your contributions are welcome and valued. Feel free to submit a pull request to help improve this project. | ## Acknowledgements and Citations * Audacity development team and Muse Group * Whisper transcription and translation analyzer uses whisper.cpp (with OpenVINO backend) * Music Generation and Continuation use MusicGen model from Meta * Music Separation effect uses Meta's Demucs v4 model * Noise Suppression: 	+ noise-suppression-denseunet-ll from OpenVINO's Open Model Zoo 	+ DeepFilterNet2 and DeepFilterNet3 * Super Resolution feature is a port of the Versatile Audio Super Resolution project | ## Citations * @inproceedings{schroeter2022deepfilternet2}: DeepFilterNet2: Towards Real-Time Speech Enhancement on Embedded Devices for Full-Band Audio * @inproceedings{schroeter2023deepfilternet3}: DeepFilterNet: Perceptually Motivated Real-Time Speech Enhancement * @article{liu2023audiosr}: AudioSR: Versatile Audio Super-resolution at Scale | ## OpenVINO Notebooks We have learned from the OpenVINO Notebooks and use them to implement AI pipelines using OpenVINO.

It focuses on **multi-agent orchestration** and **streaming interactive UIs**. | ## Target Audience and Use Cases | | Category | Use Cases | |----------|-----------| | **Developers and Researchers** | Building complex AI applications with multiple cooperating AI agents. | | **Interactive Applications** | Creating applications that require chaining multiple LLM-based tools and custom operations. | | **Custom AI Workflows** | Developing stateful agents for conversational AI, decision automation, or multi-step data processing. | | **Streamlit Users** | Rapid prototyping and deployment of multi-agent LLM applications with live interaction. | | This platform facilitates the development of advanced AI applications by combining AI, custom logic, and user-friendly interfaces.

Title: Introduction to Drizzle ORM
Summary: # Introduction to Drizzle ORM Drizzle ORM is a headless TypeScript Object-Relational Mapping (ORM) tool that provides a simple and performant way to interact with relational databases. It offers both relational and SQL-like query APIs, making it a versatile tool for accessing and managing data. | ## Key Features * Lightweight and performant * Typesafe and serverless-ready by design * Supports multiple database dialects, including Postgres, MySQL, and SQLite * No dependencies, making it a great choice for serverless applications * Opt-in tools for enhanced developer experience | ## Why Drizzle ORM? Drizzle ORM is designed to provide a seamless and intuitive experience for developers. It: * Embraces SQL, reducing the learning curve for developers already familiar with SQL * Offers a SQL-like query API, allowing developers to access data in a familiar way * Provides a relational query API for querying nested data * Outputs exactly 1 SQL query, making it performant and efficient | ## Database Support Drizzle ORM supports multiple database dialects, including: * Postgres * MySQL * SQLite * SingleStore | ## Community and Resources * Discord community for support and discussion * Twitter for updates and news * Video tutorials and courses available, including: 	+ Full Drizzle Course for Beginners 	+ Learn Drizzle In 60 Minutes 	+ Drizzle ORM in 100 Seconds 	+ Learn Drizzle ORM in 13 mins (crash course) 	+ Easiest Database Setup in Next.js 14 with Turso & Drizzle 	+ Next.js Project with Vercel, Neon, Drizzle, TailwindCSS, FlowBite and more! | ## Example Use Cases * Define and manage database schemas in TypeScript * Access data in a SQL-like or relational way * Use opt-in tools to enhance developer experience * Build serverless applications with ease | ## Comparison to Other ORMs Drizzle ORM is designed to be a more flexible and performant alternative to traditional ORMs. It: * Does not require building projects around it, unlike data frameworks * Offers a more intuitive and SQL-like query API * Provides better support for serverless applications | ## Conclusion Drizzle ORM is a powerful and flexible tool for interacting with relational databases. Its SQL-like query API, relational query API, and opt-in tools make it a great choice for developers looking for a seamless and performant experience. With its growing community and extensive resources, Drizzle ORM is an excellent choice for building serverless applications and managing data with ease.

Title: Introduction to OpenManus
Summary: # Introduction to OpenManus OpenManus is an open-source framework for building general AI agents. It allows users to achieve any idea without an invite code. | ## Team and Contributions The project is led by team members from MetaGPT, including: * @Xinbin Liang * @Jinyu Xiang * @Zhaoyang Yu * @Jiayi Zhang * @Sirui Hong | ## Project Overview OpenManus has the following features: * Simple implementation * Open-source * Dedicated to reinforcement learning (RL)-based tuning methods for LLM agents * Collaborative development with researchers from UIUC and OpenManus | ## Installation There are two installation methods: * **Method 1: Using conda** 	+ Create a new conda environment: `conda create -n open_manus python=3.12` 	+ Activate the environment: `conda activate open_manus` 	+ Clone the repository: `git clone https://github.com/FoundationAgents/OpenManus.git` 	+ Install dependencies: `pip install -r requirements.txt` * **Method 2: Using uv (Recommended)** 	+ Install uv: `curl -LsSf https://astral.sh/uv/install.sh | sh` 	+ Clone the repository: `git clone https://github.com/FoundationAgents/OpenManus.git` 	+ Create a new virtual environment and activate it: `uv venv --python 3.12` and `source .venv/bin/activate` (on Unix/macOS) or `.venv\Scripts\activate` (on Windows) 	+ Install dependencies: `uv pip install -r requirements.txt` | ## Configuration To set up OpenManus, follow these steps: * Create a `config.toml` file in the `config` directory * Edit `config/config.toml` to add API keys and customize settings * Example configuration: 	+ Global LLM configuration: `llm.model = "gpt-4o"`, `llm.base_url = "https://api.openai.com/v1"`, `llm.api_key = "sk-..."` 	+ Optional configuration for specific LLM models: `llm.vision.model = "gpt-4o"`, `llm.vision.base_url = "https://api.openai.com/v1"`, `llm.vision.api_key = "sk-..."` | ## Quick Start To run OpenManus, use the following commands: * `python main.py` to run the main program * `python run_mcp.py` to run the MCP tool version * `python run_flow.py` to run the unstable multi-agent version | ## Custom Adding Multiple Agents To add multiple agents, follow these steps: * Edit `config.toml` to add the `runflow` section * Set `use_data_analysis_agent` to `true` to activate the DataAnalysis Agent * Install relevant dependencies to ensure the agent runs properly | ## Community and Contributions * Join the networking group on Feishu to share experiences with other developers * Contribute to the project by creating issues or submitting pull requests * Contact @mannaandpoem via email: mannaandpoem@gmail.com | ## Acknowledgement Thanks to: * PPIO for computing source support * anthropic-computer-use, browser-use, and crawl4ai for providing basic support * AAAJ, MetaGPT, OpenHands, and SWE-agent for their contributions * stepfun(阶跃星辰) for supporting the Hugging Face demo space | ## Cite To cite OpenManus, use the following reference: ``` @misc{ openmanus2025, author = {Xinbin Liang and Jinyu Xiang and Zhaoyang Yu and Jiayi Zhang and Sirui Hong and Sheng Fan and Xiao Tang}, title = {OpenManus: An open-source framework for building general AI agents}, year = {2025}, publisher = {Zenodo}, doi = {10.5281/zenodo.15186407}, } ```

Title: Introduction to Serene Pub
Summary: # Introduction to Serene Pub Serene Pub is a modern, open-source AI roleplay chat application designed for simplicity, speed, and beautiful usability. It brings characters and worlds to life on your terms, with your data, and your favorite AI models. | ## Key Features * Zero Clutter, Maximum Fun: Clean, intuitive UI with minimal menus and instant access to everything you need * Real-Time Sync: All chats, settings, and characters update live across devices via WebSockets * Portable & Private: Runs locally, no accounts, no cloud lock-in. Your data stays with you * AI Freedom: Connect to OpenAI, Ollama, LM Studio, Llama.cpp, and more. Mix and match models, run local or cloud * Roleplay-First: Built for character-driven, story-rich experiences. Import Silly Tavern cards, manage personas, and more | ## Screenshots The application has a responsive design for desktop and mobile, with features including: * Desktop Experience: Chat & Editing, Connections & Characters, Contexts & Lorebooks * Mobile Experience: Chat, Connections, Edit Character, Home, Navigation | ## Features The following features are available in Serene Pub: * AI Model Agnostic: Connect to OpenAI, Ollama, Llama.cpp, and more * Ollama Manager: Built-in UI to easily manage, download, and activate Ollama models * Character & Persona Management: Import, create, and edit with rich metadata and avatars * Lorebooks+: Organize world lore, character lore, and history for deep roleplay * Group Chats: Multi-character chats for immersive group roleplay and dynamic storylines * Tags: Easily organize and filter chats, characters, personas, and lorebooks with customizable tags * Chat & Context Tools: Auto character response, edit/delete messages, streaming & regenerate, manual & hidden responses * Prompt Statistics: View compiled prompts before sending * Context Templates: Handlebar-based, customizable prompt formats * Mobile-First Design: Fully responsive, works great on phones and tablets * Themes & Dark Mode: 20+ themes, instant switching, and accessibility options * Accessibility & Screen Reader Support: Experimental support for screen readers and assistive technologies (in progress) * Portable & Secure: Embedded database, no cloud required, runs anywhere * Silly Tavern Compatibility: Import/export character cards and avatars * Open Source & Extensible: AGPL-3.0, modular adapters, easy to hack | ## Quick Start To get started with Serene Pub, follow these steps: 1. Download the latest release for your OS 2. Extract the archive anywhere 3. Read the included INSTRUCTIONS.txt for your platform 4. Run the launcher script (run.sh / run.cmd) 5. Open http://localhost:3000 in your browser 6. Add your first AI connection and start chatting! | ## Documentation The full documentation for Serene Pub is available in the [Wiki](https://github.com/doolijb/serene-pub/wiki). Popular pages include: * Installation & Setup: Detailed setup instructions for all platforms * Connections: How to connect to AI models (OpenAI, Ollama, LM Studio, etc.) * Characters & Personas: Creating and managing your roleplay characters * Lorebooks: Advanced world-building and context management * Context Templates: Customizing AI prompts with Handlebars * Troubleshooting: Common issues and solutions | ## Planned Features The following features are planned for future releases: * Vectorization / embeddings * Assistant Chat: Ask AI questions about Serene Pub and get suggestions to improve your characters, personas, and lore * Assistant Chat: In-chat OOC discussions * Image generation * Chat summarizing * Multi-user logins & multi-user group chats * Admin user account management | ## Contributing Serene Pub is community-driven, and contributions are welcome. Please open an issue or start a discussion before submitting large changes. For development setup and contribution guidelines, see the [Contributing Guide](https://github.com/doolijb/serene-pub/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md). | ## License Serene Pub is licensed under AGPL-3.0. See the [LICENSE](https://github.com/doolijb/serene-pub/blob/main/LICENSE) and [NOTICE.md](https://github.com/doolijb/serene-pub/blob/main/NOTICE.md) for details.

Title: n8n: Fact Sheet
Summary: # n8n: Fact Sheet | ## Brief Description n8n (pronounced “n-eight-n”) is a powerful, open-source workflow automation platform. It enables users to visually connect applications and automate business processes without requiring advanced coding skills. The platform uses a node-based, drag-and-drop interface to build automated workflows, called “noodles,” that can integrate with hundreds of apps and APIs, including AI agents and custom logic. This makes automation accessible to both technical and non-technical teams. | ## Key Features and Capabilities | - **Visual Workflow Builder**: Drag-and-drop, node-based interface for designing automation flows. - **Extensive Integrations**: Over 350+ built-in nodes for apps, databases, APIs, and AI services, with the ability to add custom HTTP requests or code (JavaScript/Python). - **Hybrid Model**: Low-code/no-code interface with full code access for advanced customization. - **Deployment Options**: Can be deployed on-premises (Docker, Kubernetes) or used as a hosted SaaS solution. - **Enterprise-Ready**: Features include multi-instance scaling, isolated environments (dev/staging/prod), Git version control, advanced permissions, and audit logs. - **AI & Agent Support**: Seamless integration with LLMs, vector databases, and multi-step AI agents for advanced automation scenarios. - **Debugging & Monitoring**: Inline logs, data replay, and integration with monitoring tools like Datadog and Sentry. - **Template Library**: Thousands of workflow templates to jumpstart automation projects. | ## Main Category n8n falls under **low-code/no-code workflow automation** platforms. It has strong capabilities in **API integration**, **business process automation**, and **AI-powered workflow orchestration**. | ## Main Use Cases and Target Audience | | Use Case / Audience | Description | |----------------------|-------------| | **Everyday Automation** | Automate mundane tasks across apps like Gmail, Slack, and Google Sheets (e.g., lead management, notifications). | | **Business Process Automation** | Streamline complex, multi-step processes such as onboarding, invoice processing, or syncing data between CRMs and project tools. | | **AI & Agent Workflows** | Build and orchestrate AI agents, automate research, and blend traditional business logic with AI capabilities. | | **Technical & Non-Technical Users** | Designed for developers, IT teams, business analysts, and even non-technical users who want to automate without writing code. | | **Enterprise IT & DevOps** | Suitable for organizations needing secure, scalable, and audit-compliant automation solutions. | | n8n is particularly popular among teams that value flexibility, self-hosting, and the ability to mix visual automation with custom code. Title: Python AMS for Bambu Lab Printers
Summary: # Python AMS for Bambu Lab Printers | ## Overview The **Python AMS** is an advanced, fully 3D printed upgrade for the Bambu Lab Automatic Material System (AMS), enhancing functionality, flexibility, and compatibility with various filament spools for the Bambu Lab X1/P1 3D printers. It offers a modular design that supports between two to sixteen spool slots and is compatible with different spool sizes, including cardboard and abrasive filament spools. | ## Key Features - **Spool Compatibility**: Supports spools up to 80 mm wide and 210 mm in diameter, including sample and unconventional spools. - **Gear System**: Utilizes self-centering double helical gears with a high gear ratio (135:2 or 11:1) for improved filament feeding and higher torque. - **External Spool Holders**: All spool slots function as external spool holders, supporting abrasive materials and multiple printers. - **Filament Drying**: Optional enclosure housing allows for active filament drying with up to four Polymaker PolyDryers, ideal for hygroscopic filaments like PETG. - **Roller Grip**: Bypasses the original AMS roller grip system, reducing wear and AMS overload errors. - **RFID**: Variant-specific; original Python upgrade loses RFID compatibility, but some variants (e.g., Mega Python AMS) retain it. - **Modular Design**: Allows daisy-chaining multiple AMS units. | ## Category 3D printer filament management system—specifically a modular upgrade kit for automatic filament handling systems on Bambu Lab 3D printers. | ## Use Cases and Target Audience - **Enthusiasts and Professionals**: Those using Bambu Lab printers seeking enhanced filament handling flexibility. - **Diverse Filament Users**: Individuals printing with a wide variety of filament spools, including abrasive filaments and non-standard sizes. - **Active Filament Drying**: Users requiring integrated filament drying solutions to prevent moisture-related print failures. - **System Upgraders**: Those looking to improve their Bambu Lab AMS system for increased reliability, adaptability, and scalability in multi-spool and multi-printer environments.

Title: Open Operator
Summary: # Open Operator | ## Warning - This is a proof of concept. - Browserbase aims to provide tools for building web agents, not to compete with them. - Recommended to explore Browserbase and Stagehand for building web agents. | ## Getting Started | ### Prerequisites - `pnpm` | ### Installation 1. Install dependencies: ```bash pnpm install ``` 2. Copy the example environment variables: ```bash cp .env.example .env.local ``` 3. Set up API keys: - Obtain OpenAI API key from OpenAI's dashboard. - Obtain Browserbase API key and project ID from Browserbase. 4. Update `.env.local` with the following keys: - `OPENAI_API_KEY`: Your OpenAI API key - `BROWSERBASE_API_KEY`: Your Browserbase API key - `BROWSERBASE_PROJECT_ID`: Your Browserbase project ID 5. Run the development server: ```bash pnpm dev ``` 6. Access Open Operator at: ``` http://localhost:3000 ``` | ## How It Works | ### Process Overview 1. Understand user's intent. 2. Convert intent into headless browser operations. 3. Execute actions and extract results into structured data. | ### Key Components - **Stagehand**: Converts natural language into headless browser operations, executes actions, and extracts results. - **Browserbase**: Executes actions on the browser. - **OpenAI**: Understands the user's intent. | ### Agent Loop - Calls Stagehand to convert user intent into headless browser operations. - Calls Browserbase to execute those operations. | ## Key Technologies | | Technology | Description | |--------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------| | Browserbase | Powers core browser automation and interaction capabilities | | Stagehand | Handles precise DOM manipulation and state management | | Next.js | Provides the modern web framework foundation | | OpenAI | Enables natural language understanding and decision making | | ## Contributing - Add new features - Improve documentation - Report bugs - Suggest enhancements | Open issues and pull requests to contribute. | ## License - Open Operator is open source software licensed under the MIT license. | ## Acknowledgments - Inspired by OpenAI's Operator feature. - Built upon open source technologies including Next.js, React, Browserbase, and Stagehand.
