Title: Getting Started with Let's Encrypt
Summary: # Getting Started with Let's Encrypt | **Last updated:** January 23, 2025 | ## Overview - Let's Encrypt issues certificates through an automated API based on the ACME protocol. - An "ACME client" software is required to interact with the Let's Encrypt API. - The process for obtaining a certificate does not occur on the Let's Encrypt website. | ## Obtaining Certificates | ### Through Hosting Provider - Many hosting providers manage Let's Encrypt certificates for users. - Check your provider's documentation and configuration options. - Certificates will either be managed automatically or require enabling a configuration option. | ### Using an ACME Client - If your hosting provider does not manage certificates, you can run an ACME client yourself. - **Recommended ACME Client:** Certbot - Visit the [Certbot website](https://certbot.eff.org/) for documentation and instructions. - **Other ACME Client Options:** - [List of ACME Clients](https://letsencrypt.org/docs/client-options/) | ## ACME API Endpoint - **Production API Endpoint:** `https://acme-v02.api.letsencrypt.org/directory` - **Staging API Endpoint:** Use for testing before production. | ## Getting Help - **Community Forums:** For questions related to Let's Encrypt and ACME clients. - **Documentation:** Visit the [Let's Encrypt documentation](https://letsencrypt.org/docs/) for detailed information.

Title: Introduction to WebUI
Summary: # Introduction to WebUI The WebUI project is built on the foundation of the browser-use project, which aims to make websites accessible for AI agents. We acknowledge the contribution of WarmShao to this project. | ## Key Features * WebUI is built on Gradio and supports most browser-use functionalities * Expanded Large Language Model (LLM) support, including Google, OpenAI, Azure OpenAI, Anthropic, DeepSeek, and Ollama * Custom browser support, allowing users to use their own browser with the tool * Persistent browser sessions, enabling users to keep the browser window open between AI tasks | ## Installation Guide ### Option 1: Local Installation #### Steps 1. Clone the repository: `git clone https://github.com/browser-use/web-ui.git` and navigate to the web-ui directory 2. Set up Python environment using `uv` and activate the virtual environment 3. Install dependencies: `uv pip install -r requirements.txt` and `playwright install --with-deps` 4. Configure environment: create a copy of the example environment file and add API keys and other settings 5. Run the WebUI: `python webui.py --ip 127.0.0.1 --port 7788` and access it at `http://127.0.0.1:7788` | ### Option 2: Docker Installation #### Prerequisites * Docker and Docker Compose installed * Docker Desktop (for Windows/macOS) or Docker Engine and Docker Compose (for Linux) #### Steps 1. Clone the repository: `git clone https://github.com/browser-use/web-ui.git` and navigate to the web-ui directory 2. Configure environment: create a copy of the example environment file and add API keys and other settings 3. Docker build and run: `docker compose up --build` 4. Enjoy the WebUI and VNC: access the WebUI at `http://localhost:7788` and the VNC viewer at `http://localhost:6080/vnc.html` | ## Configuration To use your own browser, set `BROWSER_PATH` to the executable path of your browser and `BROWSER_USER_DATA` to the user data directory of your browser. For example: * Windows: `BROWSER_PATH = "C:\Program Files\Google\Chrome\Application\chrome.exe"` and `BROWSER_USER_DATA = "C:\Users\YourUsername\AppData\Local\Google\Chrome\User Data"` * Mac: `BROWSER_PATH = "/Applications/Google Chrome.app/Contents/MacOS/Google Chrome"` and `BROWSER_USER_DATA = "/Users/YourUsername/Library/Application Support/Google/Chrome"` | ## Changelog | Date | Description | | --- | --- | | 2025/01/26 | Added support for combining with DeepSeek-r1 for deep thinking | | 2025/01/10 | Added Docker setup option and support for keeping browser open between tasks | | 2025/01/06 | Released a new and well-designed WebUI |

Title: GitHub Project
Summary: ### Introduction to WPFCustomMessageBox WPFCustomMessageBox is a WPF clone of the native Windows/.NET MessageBox with extra features like custom button text. It allows developers to use verbs for MessageBox buttons, improving user understanding of button functionality. | ### Key Features * Customizable button text * Returns standard .NET MessageBoxResults * Available via NuGet * Uses static methods for easy integration | ### Usage To use WPFCustomMessageBox, explore the CustomMessageBoxDemo project for demos. The library uses static methods, making it easy to plug-and-play without modifying code. | ### Examples #### Standard .NET Message Box ```csharp MessageBox.Show("Hello World!", "This is the title of the MessageBox", MessageBoxButton.OKCancel); ``` #### WPFCustomMessageBox Equivalent ```csharp CustomMessageBox.Show("Hello World!", "This is the title of the MessageBox", MessageBoxButton.OKCancel); ``` #### Adding Custom Button Text ```csharp CustomMessageBox.ShowOKCancel("Are you sure you want to eject the nuclear fuel rods?", "Confirm Fuel Ejection", "Eject Fuel Rods", "Don't do it!"); ``` | ### Custom Button Methods The WPFCustomMessageBox library provides the following customizable equivalents of .NET MessageBox button types: * `CustomMessageBox.Show()`: Standard MessageBox * `CustomMessageBox.ShowOK()`: MessageBox with single customizable "OK" button * `CustomMessageBox.ShowOKCancel()`: MessageBox with customizable "OK" and "Cancel" buttons * `CustomMessageBox.ShowYesNo()`: MessageBox with customizable "Yes" and "No" buttons * `CustomMessageBox.ShowYesNoCancel()`: MessageBox with customizable "Yes", "No", and "Cancel" buttons | ### Todo * i18n support (especially for languages that read right-to-left) | ### License The MIT License #### Copyright Copyright (c) 2013 Evan Wondrasek / Apricity Software LLC #### Permission Permission is hereby granted, free of charge, to any person obtaining a copy of this software and associated documentation files (the "Software"), to deal in the Software without restriction, including without limitation the rights to use, copy, modify, merge, publish, distribute, sublicense, and/or sell copies of the Software, and to permit persons to whom the Software is furnished to do so, subject to the following conditions: * The above copyright notice and this permission notice shall be included in all copies or substantial portions of the Software. * THE SOFTWARE IS PROVIDED "AS IS", WITHOUT WARRANTY OF ANY KIND, EXPRESS OR IMPLIED, INCLUDING BUT NOT LIMITED TO THE WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY, FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE AND NONINFRINGEMENT. Title: LivePortrait-Windows Overview
Summary: # LivePortrait-Windows Overview | ## Description LivePortrait-Windows is the official Windows package of LivePortrait, an AI-powered software designed to animate static images of portraits, including humans, cats, and dogs. The package was last updated on 2024/08/29, enhancing image-driven and regional control functionalities within its Gradio interface for easier and more refined animation control. | ## Key Features | - **Efficient Animation Control**: Supports stitching and retargeting for realistic motion. - **Multiple Animation Modes**: Includes image-driven animation and regional control for detailed editing. - **Realistic Reenactment**: Animates facial expressions, head movements, and emotions based on a driver video. - **Animal Support**: Specialized models for animating pets and animals. - **User Interface**: Features a user-friendly Gradio interface and a one-click installer for Windows with auto-update support. - **Applications**: Enables personalized video communication and content creation from still photos. | ## Category | AI-powered portrait animation software / Image-to-video animation tool. | ## Use Cases and Target Audience | ### Content Creators - Video platforms - Startups animating portraits or animal images for videos and social media content | ### Personal Use - Creating personalized video messages, greetings, or announcements from static images - Creative applications such as FacePoke (photo animation prank tool) | ### Developers and Individuals - Those seeking an accessible Windows-based solution for local image animation - Individuals looking for advanced control features in animation software | By providing these capabilities, LivePortrait-Windows offers a comprehensive tool for creating dynamic content from static images, catering to a broad audience of content creators, developers, and enthusiasts.

Title: Text Generation Web UI Project
Summary: ### Introduction to Text Generation Web UI The Text Generation Web UI is a Gradio web UI for large language models. It supports multiple local text generation backends and offers a range of features, including easy setup, 100% offline and private usage, file attachments, vision, web search, and more. | ### Key Features * Supports multiple local text generation backends, including llama.cpp, Transformers, ExLlamaV3, ExLlamaV2, and TensorRT-LLM * Easy setup with portable builds or one-click installer * 100% offline and private, with zero telemetry, external resources, or remote update requests * File attachments: upload text files, PDF documents, and .docx documents * Vision: attach images to messages for visual understanding * Web search: optionally search the internet with LLM-generated queries * Aesthetic UI with dark and light themes * Syntax highlighting for code blocks and LaTeX rendering for mathematical expressions * Instruction-following mode and chat-instruct/chat modes for talking to custom characters * Automatic prompt formatting using Jinja2 templates * Edit messages, navigate between message versions, and branch conversations * Multiple sampling parameters and generation options * Switch between different models in the UI without restarting * Automatic GPU layers for GGUF models (on NVIDIA GPUs) * Free-form text generation in the Notebook tab * OpenAI-compatible API with Chat and Completions endpoints * Extension support with numerous built-in and user-contributed extensions | ### Installation Options #### Option 1: Portable Builds * Download and unzip the portable build for your operating system (Windows, Linux, or macOS) * Run the executable file to start the web UI | #### Option 2: Manual Portable Install with Venv * Clone the repository and create a virtual environment using Python 3.9+ * Install dependencies using pip * Activate the virtual environment and launch the server using the basic command | #### Option 3: One-Click Installer * Clone the repository or download the source code and extract it * Run the startup script for your operating system (Windows, Linux, or macOS) * Select your GPU vendor and follow the prompts to complete the installation | ### Manual Full Installation with Conda or Docker #### Full Installation with Conda | Step | Description | | --- | --- | | 1 | Install Conda using Miniforge | | 2 | Create a new Conda environment with Python 3.11 | | 3 | Install PyTorch using the system and GPU specifications | | 4 | Install the web UI using pip and the requirements file for your hardware | | #### Manual Install * Use the `requirements_nowheels.txt` file to manually compile and install the dependencies * Install the desired loaders manually | #### Alternative: Docker * Create a Docker environment using the provided Dockerfiles and docker-compose files * Set environment variables and configure the Docker setup as needed | ### Updating the Requirements * Use the `pip install -r <requirements file> --upgrade` command to update the dependencies * Update the Conda environment using the `conda activate textgen` and `pip install -r <requirements file> --upgrade` commands | ### Command-Line Flags The server.py script accepts a range of command-line flags for customizing the web UI and its behavior. The available flags include: * Basic settings: `--multi-user`, `--model`, `--lora`, `--model-dir`, `--lora-dir`, `--model-menu`, `--settings`, `--extensions`, `--verbose`, `--idle-timeout` * Model loader: `--loader` * Context and cache: `--ctx-size`, `--cache-type` * Speculative decoding: `--model-draft`, `--draft-max`, `--gpu-layers-draft`, `--device-draft`, `--ctx-size-draft` * Llama.cpp: `--gpu-layers`, `--mmproj`, `--streaming-llm`, `--tensor-split`, `--row-split`, `--no-mmap`, `--mlock`, `--no-kv-offload`, `--batch-size`, `--threads`, `--threads-batch`, `--numa`, `--extra-flags` * Transformers/Accelerate: `--cpu`, `--cpu-memory`, `--disk`, `--disk-cache-dir`, `--load-in-8bit`, `--bf16`, `--no-cache`, `--trust-remote-code`, `--force-safetensors`, `--no_use_fast`, `--attn-implementation`

Title: AI Text Generation Software Project
Summary: ### Introduction to KoboldCpp KoboldCpp is an easy-to-use AI text-generation software for GGML and GGUF models. It's a single self-contained distributable that builds off llama.cpp and adds many additional powerful features. | ### Key Features * Single file executable with no installation required and no external dependencies * Runs on CPU or GPU, supporting full or partial offloaded LLM text generation * Supports various models, including GGML and GGUF, with backwards compatibility * Image generation, speech-to-text, and text-to-speech capabilities * Compatible APIs for popular web services * Bundled KoboldAI Lite UI with editing tools and multiple modes * Supports loading Tavern Character Cards and importing/exporting data formats | ### System Requirements and Binaries * Ready-to-use binaries for Windows, MacOS, and Linux * Can be run directly with Colab, Docker, or self-compiled for other platforms (e.g., Android, Raspberry PI) | ### Obtaining and Loading Models * KoboldCpp uses GGUF models, which can be downloaded from external sources (e.g., Bartowski's Huggingface) * Recommended models for beginners: L3-8B-Stheno-v3.2, Tiefighter 13B, or Gemma-3-27B Abliterated * Models for Whisper, Image Generation, Text-to-Speech, or Image Recognition can be found on the Wiki | ### Improving Performance * GPU Acceleration: use `--usecuda` flag (Nvidia only) or `--usevulkan` (any GPU) * GPU Layer Offloading: add `--gpulayers` to offload model layers to GPU * Increasing Context Size: use `--contextsize` to increase context size * Old CPU Compatibility: try running in non-avx2 compatibility mode with `--noavx2` flag | ### Compiling KoboldCpp from Source Code #### Compiling on Linux * Use automated build script `koboldcpp.sh` or manual method with `make` * Optional: link own install of Vulkan SDK or CLBlast manually * Set `LLAMA_PORTABLE=1` to make build sharable and capable of working on other devices | #### Compiling on Windows * Use w64devkit and clone the repo * Run `make` to create .dll files for pure CPU native build * Optional: build with CUDA using Visual Studio, CMake, and CUDA Toolkit | #### Compiling on MacOS * Clone the repo and run `make` with optional Metal GPU support * Set `LLAMA_PORTABLE=1` to make build sharable and capable of working on other devices | #### Compiling on Android (Termux Installation) * Use Termux Quick Setup Script or manual instructions * Install dependencies, clone the repo, and build the project * Start the python server and connect to `http://localhost:5001` on mobile browser | ### Third-Party Resources * Unofficial resources and forks (e.g., ROCM fork for AMD users) may be available, but are not officially supported.

Title: LLM Answer Engine Project
Summary: ### Introduction to Perplexity-Inspired LLM Answer Engine The Perplexity-Inspired LLM Answer Engine is a sophisticated project that leverages the capabilities of various technologies, including Groq, Mistral AI's Mixtral, Langchain.JS, Brave Search, Serper API, and OpenAI. This project efficiently returns sources, answers, images, videos, and follow-up questions based on user queries. | ### Technologies Used * Next.js: A React framework for building server-side rendered and static web applications. * Tailwind CSS: A utility-first CSS framework for rapidly building custom user interfaces. * Vercel AI SDK: A library for building AI-powered streaming text and chat UIs. * Groq & Mixtral: Technologies for processing and understanding user queries. * Langchain.JS: A JavaScript library focused on text operations, such as text splitting and embeddings. * Brave Search: A privacy-focused search engine used for sourcing relevant content and images. * Serper API: Used for fetching relevant video and image results based on the user's query. * OpenAI Embeddings: Used for creating vector representations of text chunks. * Cheerio: Utilized for HTML parsing, allowing the extraction of content from web pages. * Ollama (Optional): Used for streaming inference and embeddings. * Upstash Redis Rate Limiting (Optional): Used for setting up rate limiting for the application. * Upstash Semantic Cache (Optional): Used for caching data for faster response times. | ### Getting Started #### Prerequisites * Obtain API keys from OpenAI, Groq, Brave Search, and Serper. * Ensure Node.js and npm are installed on your machine (for non-Docker installation). * Ensure Docker and docker compose are installed on your machine (for Docker installation). | #### Obtaining API Keys * OpenAI API Key: Generate your OpenAI API key. * Groq API Key: Get your Groq API key. * Brave Search API Key: Obtain your Brave Search API key. * Serper API Key: Get your Serper API key. | #### Quick Clone and Deploy * Clone the repository: `git clone https://github.com/developersdigest/llm-answer-engine.git` * Move into the directory: `cd llm-answer-engine` * Fill in all required API keys for installation. | #### Installation ##### Docker Installation 1. Edit the `docker-compose.yml` file and add your API keys. 2. Run the server: `docker compose up -d` (for v2) or `docker-compose up -d` (for v1). | ##### Non-Docker Installation 1. Install required dependencies: `npm install` or `bun install`. 2. Create a `.env` file in the root of your project and add your API keys. 3. Run the server: `npm run dev` or `bun run dev`. | ### Editing the Configuration The configuration file is located in the `app/config.tsx` file. You can modify the following values: * `useOllamaInference`: False * `useOllamaEmbeddings`: False * `inferenceModel`: 'mixtral-8x7b-32768' * `inferenceAPIKey`: Process.env.GROQ_API_KEY * `embeddingsModel`: 'text-embedding-3-small' * `textChunkSize`: 800 * `textChunkOverlap`: 200 * `numberOfSimilarityResults`: 2 * `numberOfPagesToScan`: 10 * `nonOllamaBaseURL`: 'https://api.groq.com/openai/v1' * `useFunctionCalling`: True * `useRateLimiting`: False * `useSemanticCache`: False * `usePortkey`: False | ### Function Calling Support (Beta) Function calling is currently supported with the following capabilities: * Maps and Locations (Serper Locations API) * Shopping (Serper Shopping API) * TradingView Stock Data (Free Widget) * Spotify (Free API) | ### Ollama Support (Partially Supported) * Currently, streaming text responses are supported for Ollama, but follow-up questions are not yet supported. * Embeddings are supported, but time-to-first-token can be quite long when using both a local embedding model and a local model for streaming inference. | ### Roadmap | Task | Status | | --- | --- | | Add document upload + RAG for document search/retrieval | Pending | | Add a settings component to allow users to select the model, embeddings model, and other parameters from the UI | Pending | | Add support for follow-up questions when using Ollama | Pending | | Add support for diffusion models (Fal.AI SD3 to start), accessible via '@ mention' | Complete | | Add AI Gateway to support multiple models and embeddings | Complete | | Add support for semantic caching to improve response times | Complete | | Add support for dynamic and conditionally rendered UI components based on the user's query | Complete | | Add dark mode support based on the user's system preference | Complete | | ### Contributing Contributions to the project are welcome. Feel free to fork the repository, make your changes, and submit a pull request. You can also open issues to suggest improvements or report bugs. | ### License This project is licensed under the MIT License. | ### Support If you find this project helpful, consider supporting the developer: * Patreon: Support on Patreon at patreon.com/DevelopersDigest * Buy Me A Coffee: Buy a coffee at buymeacoffee.com/developersdigest * Website: Check out the website at developersdigest.tech * Github: Follow on GitHub at github.com/developersdigest * Twitter: Follow on Twitter at twitter.com/dev__digest

Title: FLUX Overview
Summary: # FLUX Overview FLUX is an open-weight model developed by Black Forest Labs. The repository contains minimal inference code to run image generation and editing with FLUX open-weight models. | ## Local Installation To install FLUX locally: * Clone the repository: `git clone https://github.com/black-forest-labs/flux` * Create a virtual environment: `python3.10 -m venv .venv` * Activate the environment: `source .venv/bin/activate` * Install the required packages: `pip install -e .[all]` | ## Local Installation with TensorRT Support To install FLUX with TensorRT support: * Clone the repository: `git clone https://github.com/black-forest-labs/flux` * Install enroot and import the PyTorch image from NVIDIA * Create a new enroot environment: `enroot create -n pti2501 nvidia+pytorch+25.01-py3.sqsh` * Start the enroot environment: `enroot start --rw -m ${PWD}/flux:/workspace/flux -r pti2501` * Install the required packages: `pip install -e .[tensorrt] --extra-index-url https://pypi.nvidia.com` | ## Open-Weight Models The following open-weight models are available: | Model | Usage | HuggingFace Repo | License | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | FLUX.1 [schnell] | Text to Image | https://huggingface.co/black-forest-labs/FLUX.1-schnell | apache-2.0 | | FLUX.1 [dev] | Text to Image | https://huggingface.co/black-forest-labs/FLUX.1-dev | FLUX.1-dev Non-Commercial License | | FLUX.1 Fill [dev] | In/Out-painting | https://huggingface.co/black-forest-labs/FLUX.1-Fill-dev | FLUX.1-dev Non-Commercial License | | FLUX.1 Canny [dev] | Structural Conditioning | https://huggingface.co/black-forest-labs/FLUX.1-Canny-dev | FLUX.1-dev Non-Commercial License | | FLUX.1 Depth [dev] | Structural Conditioning | https://huggingface.co/black-forest-labs/FLUX.1-Depth-dev | FLUX.1-dev Non-Commercial License | | FLUX.1 Canny [dev] LoRA | Structural Conditioning | https://huggingface.co/black-forest-labs/FLUX.1-Canny-dev-lora | FLUX.1-dev Non-Commercial License | | FLUX.1 Depth [dev] LoRA | Structural Conditioning | https://huggingface.co/black-forest-labs/FLUX.1-Depth-dev-lora | FLUX.1-dev Non-Commercial License | | FLUX.1 Redux [dev] | Image variation | https://huggingface.co/black-forest-labs/FLUX.1-Redux-dev | FLUX.1-dev Non-Commercial License | | FLUX.1 Kontext [dev] | Image editing | https://huggingface.co/black-forest-labs/FLUX.1-Kontext-dev | FLUX.1-dev Non-Commercial License | | FLUX.1 Krea [dev] | Text to Image | https://huggingface.co/black-forest-labs/FLUX.1-Krea-dev | FLUX.1-dev Non-Commercial License | | ## API Usage The FLUX API offers access to all models, including Pro tier non-open weight models. For more information, refer to the [API documentation](docs.bfl.ai). | ## Licensing Models for Commercial Use To license FLUX models for commercial use, visit [https://bfl.ai/pricing/licensing](https://bfl.ai/pricing/licensing). To enable usage tracking, select `track_usage` in the CLI or click the corresponding checkmark in the provided demos. | ## Citation If you find the provided code or models useful for your research, consider citing them as: ```bib @misc{ labs2025flux1kontextflowmatching, title={FLUX.1 Kontext: Flow Matching for In-Context Image Generation and Editing in Latent Space}, author={Black Forest Labs and Stephen Batifol and Andreas Blattmann and Frederic Boesel and Saksham Consul and Cyril Diagne and Tim Dockhorn and Jack English and Zion English and Patrick Esser and Sumith Kulal and Kyle Lacey and Yam Levi and Cheng Li and Dominik Lorenz and Jonas Müller and Dustin Podell and Robin Rombach and Harry Saini and Axel Sauer and Luke Smith}, year={2025}, eprint={2506.15742}, archivePrefix={arXiv}, primaryClass={cs.GR}, url={https://arxiv.org/abs/2506.15742} } | @misc{ flux2024, author={Black Forest Labs}, title={FLUX}, year={2024}, howpublished={\url{https://github.com/black-forest-labs/flux}} } ```

Title: Hugging Face Model Downloader Tool
Summary: ### HuggingFaceModelDownloader #### Overview HuggingFaceModelDownloader is a fast, resilient, and resumable CLI (and Go library) for downloading models and datasets from the Hugging Face Hub. The v2.0.0 release features a clean flag set, a colorful TUI, structured JSON events, retry/backoff, and filesystems-only resume. | #### Highlights * Resumable by default * LFS files verified by SHA-256 (when provided by the repo) * Non-LFS files verified by size * Large files use multipart range downloads with per-part resume * Beautiful live TUI with auto-adaptation to terminal width/height * Robust and fast cancellation * Structured progress events for CI/logging | #### Installation * From source (Go 1.21+): + `git clone https://github.com/bodaay/HuggingFaceModelDownloader` + `cd HuggingFaceModelDownloader` + `go build -o hfdownloader` + Optional: `go install .` (installs into your $GOBIN) * Requirements: + Go 1.21+ + macOS / Linux / Windows (modern terminals support the live TUI; otherwise it falls back to plain text) | #### Quick Start * Public model: `hfdownloader download TheBloke/Mistral-7B-Instruct-v0.2-GGUF -o ./Models` * Private or gated repo (requires token): `HF_TOKEN=xxxx hfdownloader download owner/private-model -o ./Models` * Filter LFS artifacts by name; append a subdir for each filter: `hfdownloader download TheBloke/vicuna-13b-v1.3.0-GGML:q4_0,q5_0 --append-filter-subdir -o ./Models -c 8 --max-active 3` * Dataset mode: `hfdownloader download facebook/flores --dataset -o ./Datasets` * Plan only (no downloads), pretty-printed JSON: `hfdownloader download TheBloke/Mistral-7B-Instruct-v0.2-GGUF:q4_0 --dry-run --plan-format json` | #### CLI * `hfdownloader download [REPO] [flags]` * REPO: owner/name or owner/name:filter1,filter2 * Common flags: + Destination: `-o, --output` (base folder for downloads) + What to fetch: `-r, --repo` (optional if REPO positional is present) + `--dataset` (treat repo as dataset instead of model) + `-b, --revision` (main, branch/tag/sha) + `-F, --filters` (comma-separated LFS name filters) + `--append-filter-subdir` (put each filter's files under its own subfolder) | #### Speed and Parallelism * `-c, --connections` (per-file HTTP range connections) * `--max-active` (max concurrent files) * `--multipart-threshold` (only multipart files ≥ threshold) | #### Reliability * `--retries` (retry attempts per request/part) * `--backoff-initial` (initial backoff time) * `--backoff-max` (max backoff time) * `--verify` (non-LFS verification: none | size | etag | sha256) | #### Planning / Logging * `--dry-run` (plan only, no downloads) * `--plan-format` (table or JSON, with `--dry-run`) * `--json` (emit machine-readable events) * `-q, --quiet` (reduce console noise) * `-v, --verbose` (more diagnostics) | #### Auth & Config * `-t, --token` (Hugging Face token or HF_TOKEN env) * `--config` (path to JSON config, defaults to ~/.config/hfdownloader.json if present) | #### Live TUI * Header: repo, revision, dataset flag, output dir, connections, verification, retries * Global progress bar with %, bytes, speed, ETA * Per-file rows: status, filename, bar, bytes, speed, ETA * Auto-adjusts to width/height; plain text fallback when colors/ANSI aren’t available | #### JSON Mode * `--json` bypasses the TUI for structured events * JSON events (for CI/logging): + `scan_start` + `plan_item` + `file_start` + `file_progress` + `retry` + `file_done` + `error` + `done` | #### Skip Lines * Each file prints at most one “skip (…)” per run * Deduplication is enforced internally | #### Resume & Verification * LFS files (SHA available): compute local SHA-256 and compare to the repo’s SHA * Non-LFS / SHA unknown: compare file size * Multipart parts: each range part downloads to path.part-00, path.part-01, …; matching-length parts are not re-fetched and the file is assembled when all parts are present | #### Cancellation & Signals * SIGINT/SIGTERM: fast, cooperative cancellation—no new work is scheduled, all goroutines exit promptly, and ongoing HTTP calls are context-bound * SIGKILL (9): cannot be intercepted by any program; the OS terminates immediately | #### Examples * Download GGUF variants into separate subfolders: `hfdownloader download TheBloke/Mistral-7B-Instruct-v0.2-GGUF:q4_0,q5_0 --append-filter-subdir -o ./Models --connections 8 --max-active 3` * Stricter verification (non-LFS): `hfdownloader download owner/name --verify etag` * Datasets: `hfdownloader download huggingface/awesome-dataset --dataset -o ./Datasets` * Plan first, then run: `hfdownloader download owner/name:q4_0 --dry-run --plan-format json` followed by `hfdownloader download owner/name:q4_0` | #### Configuration File * If `--config` is not provided, the tool will read ~/.config/hfdownloader.json when present and use values as defaults (CLI flags still override) * Example configuration file: ```json { "output": "Storage", "connections": 8, "max-active": 3, "multipart-threshold": "256MiB", "verify": "size", "retries": 4, "backoff-initial": "400ms", "backoff-max": "10s", "token": "hf_xxx" } ``` | #### Library Usage (Go) * Import the `hfdownloader` package and create a `Job` struct with the desired settings * Use the `Download` function to start the download * Example: ```go package main | import ( 	"context" 	"log" 	"github.com/bodaay/HuggingFaceModelDownloader/hfdownloader" ) | func main() { 	job := hfdownloader.Job{ 		Repo: "TheBloke/Mistral-7B-Instruct-v0.2-GGUF:q4_0", 		Revision: "main", 		IsDataset: false, 		Filters: []string{"q4_0"}, 	} 	cfg := hfdownloader.Settings{ 		OutputDir: "Storage", 		Concurrency: 8, 		MaxActiveDownloads: 3, 		MultipartThreshold: "256MiB", 		Verify: "size", 		Retries: 4, 		BackoffInitial: "400ms", 		BackoffMax: "10s", 		Token: "", 	} 	progress := func(ev hfdownloader.ProgressEvent) { 		switch ev.Event { 		case "file_done": 			if strings.HasPrefix(ev.Message, "skip") { 				log.Printf("skip: %s (%s)", ev.Path, ev.Message) 			} else { 				log.Printf("done: %s", ev.Path) 			} 		case "retry": 			log.Printf("retry %s: attempt %d: %s", ev.Path, ev.Attempt, ev.Message) 		} 	} 	if err := hfdownloader.Download(context.Background(), job, cfg, progress); err != nil { 		log.Fatal(err) 	} } ``` | #### Troubleshooting * 401 Unauthorized: provide a token using `-t TOKEN` or `HF_TOKEN=...` * 403 Forbidden (terms): visit the repo page and accept terms, then retry * Range requests disabled: multipart falls back to a single GET automatically; downloads still work * Slow throughput: increase `--connections` and `--max-active` gradually; ensure disk/FS and network can keep up * Repeated “skip” lines: v2.0.0 emits at most one “skip (…)” per file per run; if you still see duplicates, check for duplicate paths in the upstream tree or path collisions on Windows | #### Why v2? * Cleaner mental model (one `download` command, sensible defaults) * Filesystem-only resume—reliable and transparent; no “state” files to corrupt * JSON events and a TUI that looks great everywhere * Strong cancellation story for real-world, long-running downloads | #### License * Apache-2.0 (see LICENSE) | #### Acknowledgements * Thanks to the HF community and tooling ecosystem—this project tries to be a pragmatic drop-in for everyday model & dataset fetching

Title: Lip-Sync on Diffeomorphic Models
Summary: # Lip-Sync on Diffeomorphic Models | ## Overview This guide outlines the procedure to create a lip-sync animation for a Diffeomorphic-based model. | ## Requirements - Diffeomorphic model (e.g., Tifa) - Papagayo (lip-syncing generator software) - .wav sound file (convert other formats using online tools if necessary) | ## Steps | ### Using Papagayo 1. **Open Papagayo** - Drag and drop the .wav file into the Papagayo window. - Sound analysis of the .wav file will appear at the top. - Enter the spoken text in the field below. - Set the frames per second (FPS) in the upper right field. | 2. **Adjust Text Position** - Ensure the text overlaps with the blue part of the sound analysis. - Drag the vertical grey columns to adjust the text position. - Listen to the sound as you adjust the text placement. | 3. **Export the File** - Export the file as a .dat file. | ### Importing into Blender 1. **Import the .dat File** - Select the **Import Daz** tab in the Morph panel. - Go to the **Visemen** part and click on the **Load Moho** button. - Choose the .dat file and adjust the settings on the right (press `N` if settings are not visible). | 2. **Settings to Adjust** - **Emphasis**: Reduce if the animation is too strong. - **New Action**: Choose where to add the animation. - **Start At Frame 1**: Disable if you want the animation to start at the current frame. | 3. **Verify Import** - Ensure the animation appears in the Dope Sheet. - Select the armature where you want to import the animation to avoid import failures. | ### Additional Tips - **Rest Pose**: Add keys before/after the animation with all visemes set to 0 for a rest pose. - **Viseme Values**: Modify viseme values in the Visemen tab of Diffeomorphic. | ## Troubleshooting - **Error During Import**: Ensure the armature is selected. - **Rest Pose**: Add keys with all visemes set to 0 for a rest pose before/after the animation. | ## Further Information - For more details, refer to the [Diffeomorphic documentation](https://mustard3d.eu/documentation/). | ## Comments - Log in to post a comment.

Title: Notion Database Ultimate Beginners Guide
Summary: ### Introduction to Notion Databases Notion databases are a powerful and flexible tool that can store various kinds of data, including text, numbers, file attachments, and more. They can be used to create: * Task managers * Calendars * Note-taking systems * CRMs * Custom dashboards for sales and performance data * And much more | ### Creating a Notion Database To create a Notion database, follow these steps: 1. Type `/database` on any Notion page to see a list of database options. 2. Choose **Database – Inline** to create a brand-new database on that page. 3. Alternatively, create a blank page and select one of the database options. 4. Use the `/` command to create a database with a specific layout, such as: 	* Table view 	* Board view 	* Gallery view 	* List view 	* Calendar view 	* Timeline view | ### Database Structure Notion databases store rows (also known as records), which are actually Notion pages themselves. Each row can have properties, which hold structured information within a database. Properties can be used for filtering and sorting data. | ### Properties Properties have several uses, including: * Filtering data * Sorting data * Holding structured information within a database * Typically set on a per-row basis | ### Database Views Notion databases can have multiple views, each with its own unique filters and sorts. This allows you to look at the same database in many different ways. | ### What is a Database? A database is an organized collection of data stored and accessed electronically. Nearly every program and web app relies heavily on databases. | ### Notion Database Features Notion databases have several features, including: * Filters * Sorts * Grouping * Properties * Relations * Linked databases | ### Relations Relations are a type of property that allows you to link rows in one database to rows in another database. This can be used to model parent-child relationships, such as tasks and sub-tasks. | ### Creating a Relation To create a relation in Notion, follow these steps: 1. Add a new property to your database. 2. Select the **Relation** type. 3. Select the database you want your relation property to point towards. 4. Choose whether the relation should have separate properties. | ### Example Use Case: Task Manager Relations can be used to create a task manager with parent tasks and sub-tasks. By setting up a relation between the two, you can filter and sort data to show only the sub-tasks for a specific parent task. | ### Multi-Database Relations Notion also allows you to create relations between two separate databases. This can be used to model complex relationships, such as tasks and projects. | ### Linked Databases Linked databases are a feature in Notion that allows you to link a database to a page, and then filter and sort data based on that link. This can be used to create a filtered view of a database inside a page. | ### Conclusion Notion databases are a powerful tool that can be used to create a wide range of applications, from simple task managers to complex CRMs. By understanding how to create and use databases, properties, and relations, you can unlock a whole new level of capability inside of Notion. | ### Database Layouts The following database layouts are available in Notion: | Layout | Description | | --- | --- | | Table view | Displays data in a table format | | Board view | Displays data in a board format | | Gallery view | Displays data in a gallery format | | List view | Displays data in a list format | | Calendar view | Displays data in a calendar format | | Timeline view | Displays data in a timeline format | | ### Properties Table The following properties are available in Notion: | Property | Description | | --- | --- | | Text | A text property | | Number | A number property | | Select | A select property | | Multi-Select | A multi-select property | | Date | A date property | | Relation | A relation property |

Title: Creating Recurring Tasks in Notion
Summary: # Creating Recurring Tasks in Notion Notion does not have a built-in recurring task feature, but there is a workaround. This guide will show you how to create tasks with recurring due dates in Notion. | ## Overview of the Solution To manage recurring tasks in Notion, you must set the due date to the next due date (based on your day/time interval) once you finish the task. A template with a `Next Due` property can be used to make this process easier. | ## Key Features of the Template * Create tasks with either a `One-Time` or `Recurring` type * Recurring tasks display a `Next Due` date based on the chosen due date and recur interval * One-Time tasks do not show a `Next Due` date * A `State` property shows the task status at a glance: + Overdue (red) + Due today (green) + Due in the future (blue) | ## Using the Template 1. **Duplicate the template**: Duplicate the recurring tasks template into your own Notion workspace. 2. **Modify filters**: Modify the filters for each view to fit your needs. 3. **Create custom views**: Create custom views for any date ranges you want. 4. **Add tasks**: Add tasks to your database. 5. **Add day intervals**: Add a number to the `Recur Interval` property for recurring tasks. 6. **Change due dates**: Change the due date to the date displayed in `Next Due` to "complete" a recurring task. | ## Formulas Used ### Next Due Property Formula The formula for the `Next Due` property ensures that it always suggests the most logical date based on both your interval and today's date. ```javascript if ( dateBetween( now(), prop("Due"), "days" ) >= 1 and unequal( formatDate(now(), "L"), formatDate(prop("Due"), "L") ) and unequal( prop("Type"), "One-Time" ), // ... ) ``` ### State Property Formula The formula for the `State` property uses nested if-statements to determine which color circle to display: ```php if ( empty(prop("Due")), "", if ( formatDate(prop("Due"), "L") == formatDate(now(), "L"), "", if ( prop("Due") < now(), "", "" ) ) ) ``` ## Additional Resources * Check out other Notion content creators' solutions for recurring tasks: + Ruben Loan's solution + Marie Poulin's solution + Danny Hatcher's solution + August Bradley's solution * Visit the Notion Tips hub page for more Notion content * Subscribe to Thomas Frank Explains for more Notion tutorials!

Title: ComfyUI-VibeVoice
Summary: # ComfyUI-VibeVoice ComfyUI-VibeVoice is a custom node for ComfyUI that integrates Microsoft's VibeVoice, a frontier model for generating expressive, long-form, multi-speaker conversational audio. | ## About The Project * VibeVoice is a novel framework by Microsoft for generating expressive, long-form, multi-speaker conversational audio. * It excels at creating natural-sounding dialogue, podcasts, and more, with consistent voices for up to 4 speakers. * The custom node handles everything from model downloading and memory management to audio processing. | ## Key Features * Multi-Speaker TTS: Generate conversations with up to 4 distinct voices in a single audio output. * High-Fidelity Voice Cloning: Use any audio file (.wav, .mp3) as a reference for a speaker's voice. * Hybrid Generation Mode: Mix and match cloned voices with high-quality, zero-shot generated voices in the same script. * Flexible Scripting: Use simple tags or the classic Speaker 1: format to write your dialogue. * Advanced Attention Mechanisms: Choose between eager, sdpa, flash_attention_2, and the high-performance sage attention for fine-tuned control over speed and compatibility. * Robust 4-Bit Quantization: Run the large language model component in 4-bit mode to significantly reduce VRAM usage. * Automatic Model Management: Models are downloaded automatically and managed efficiently by ComfyUI to save VRAM. | ## Getting Started To install ComfyUI-VibeVoice, follow these steps: 1. Install through the ComfyUI Manager: Go to Manager -> Install Custom Nodes, search for ComfyUI-VibeVoice, and click "Install". 2. Alternatively, install manually by cloning the repository, installing dependencies, and restarting ComfyUI. | ## Models The following models are available: | Model | Context Length | Generation Length | Weight | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | VibeVoice-1.5B | 64K | ~90 min | HF link | | VibeVoice-Large | 32K | ~45 min | HF link | | ## Usage The node is designed for maximum flexibility within your ComfyUI workflow. To use it: 1. Add the VibeVoice TTS node to your graph. 2. Load your reference voice files using ComfyUI's built-in Load Audio node. 3. Connect the AUDIO output from each Load Audio node to the corresponding speaker_*_voice input. 4. Write your dialogue using one of the supported formats. 5. Generate a single audio file containing the full conversation. | ## Scripting and Voice Modes * Speaker Tagging: Assign lines to speakers using modern or classic formats. * Hybrid Voice Generation: Mix cloned voices and generated voices in the same script. | ## Node Inputs * model_name: Select the VibeVoice model to use (1.5B or Large). * text: The conversational script. * quantize_llm_4bit: Enable to run the LLM component in 4-bit mode. * attention_mode: Select the attention implementation (eager, sdpa, flash_attention_2, or sage). * cfg_scale: Controls how strongly the model adheres to the reference voice's timbre. * inference_steps: Number of diffusion steps for audio generation. * seed: A seed for reproducibility. * do_sample, temperature, top_p, top_k: Standard sampling parameters. | ## Performance & Advanced Features The node features a sophisticated system for managing performance, memory, and stability. The following table summarizes the feature compatibility and VRAM matrix: | Quantize LLM | Attention Mode | Behavior / Notes | Relative VRAM | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | OFF | eager | Full Precision. Most compatible baseline. | High | | OFF | sdpa | Full Precision. Recommended for balanced performance. | High | | OFF | flash_attention_2 | Full Precision. High performance on compatible GPUs. | High | | OFF | sage | Full Precision. Uses high-performance mixed-precision kernels. | High | | ON | eager | Falls back to sdpa with bfloat16 compute. Warns user. | Low | | ON | sdpa | Recommended for memory savings. Uses bfloat16 compute. | Low | | ON | flash_attention_2 | Falls back to sdpa with bfloat16 compute. Warns user. | Low | | ON | sage | Recommended for stability. Uses fp32 compute to ensure numerical stability with quantization. | Medium | | ## Changelog * v1.5.0: Stability and Prompting - Fixed bug where a speaker's voice could unintentionally change or blend with another reference voice mid-sentence. * v1.3.0: SageAttention & Quantization Overhaul - Full integration with the sageattention library and robust 4-bit LLM quantization. * v1.2.0: Compatibility Update - Includes automatic detection and compatibility for both older and newer versions of the Transformers library. | ## Tips from the Original Authors * Punctuation: For Chinese text, using English punctuation can improve stability. * Model Choice: The 7B model variant (VibeVoice-Large) is generally more stable. * Spontaneous Sounds/Music: The model may spontaneously generate background music. * Singing: The model was not trained on singing data, but it may attempt to sing as an emergent behavior. | ## License This project is distributed under the MIT License. The VibeVoice model and its components are subject to the licenses provided by Microsoft. Please use responsibly. | ## Acknowledgments * Microsoft for creating and open-sourcing the VibeVoice project. * The ComfyUI team for their incredible and extensible platform.

Title: OpenCode AI Coding Agent
Summary: # OpenCode AI Coding Agent | ## Installation | ### YOLO Method ```bash curl -fsSL https://opencode.ai/install | bash ``` | ### Package Managers | - **npm** ```bash npm i -g opencode-ai@latest ``` | - **bun/pnpm/yarn** ```bash scoop bucket add extras scoop install extras/opencode ``` | - **Windows (Chocolatey)** ```bash choco install opencode ``` | - **macOS and Linux (Homebrew)** ```bash brew install sst/tap/opencode ``` | - **Arch Linux** ```bash paru -S opencode-bin ``` | **Tip:** Remove versions older than 0.1.x before installing. | ### Installation Directory | The install script respects the following priority order for the installation path: | | Environment Variable | Description | |----------------------|-------------| | `$OPENCODE_INSTALL_DIR` | Custom installation directory | | `$XDG_BIN_DIR` | XDG Base Directory Specification compliant path | | `$HOME/bin` | Standard user binary directory (if exists or can be created) | | `$HOME/.opencode/bin` | Default fallback | | **Examples:** ```bash OPENCODE_INSTALL_DIR=/usr/local/bin curl -fsSL https://opencode.ai/install | bash XDG_BIN_DIR=$HOME/.local/bin curl -fsSL https://opencode.ai/install | bash ``` | ## Documentation | For more information on how to configure OpenCode, refer to the official documentation. | ## Contributing | If you're interested in contributing to OpenCode, please read the contributing guidelines before submitting a pull request. | ## FAQ | ### How is OpenCode different from Claude Code? | - **100% Open Source**: Not coupled to any provider. Can be used with Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, or even local models. - **Out of the Box LSP Support**: Focus on Terminal User Interface (TUI). - **Client/Server Architecture**: Allows OpenCode to run on your computer while being driven remotely from a mobile app. | ### Other Repo | The other confusingly named repo has no relation to this one. You can read the story behind it for more details. | ## Join the Community | - **Discord** - **X.com**

Title: OpenCode AI Coding Agent
Summary: ### Introduction to OpenCode OpenCode is an AI coding agent built for the terminal. This guide provides a step-by-step introduction to getting started with OpenCode. | ### Prerequisites To use OpenCode, you'll need: * A modern terminal emulator such as: * WezTerm (cross-platform) * Alacritty (cross-platform) * Ghostty (Linux and macOS) * Kitty (Linux and macOS) * API keys for the LLM providers you want to use | ### Installation OpenCode can be installed using the following methods: * **Install Script**: `curl -fsSL https://opencode.ai/install | bash` * **Node.js**: + `npm install -g opencode-ai` + `bun install -g opencode-ai` + `pnpm install -g opencode-ai` + `yarn global add opencode-ai` * **Homebrew (macOS and Linux)**: `brew install sst/tap/opencode` * **Paru (Arch Linux)**: `paru -S opencode-bin` * **Windows**: + **Chocolatey**: `choco install opencode` + **WinGet**: `winget install opencode` + **Scoop**: `scoop bucket add extras` and `scoop install extras/opencode` + **NPM**: `npm install -g opencode-ai` | ### Configuration To configure OpenCode, you'll need to: 1. Run `opencode auth login` and select your provider 2. Create an API key at [https://opencode.ai/auth](https://opencode.ai/auth) 3. Paste your API key into the terminal | ### Initialization To initialize OpenCode, navigate to your project directory and run: 1. `cd /path/to/project` 2. `opencode` 3. `/init` to analyze your project and create an `AGENTS.md` file | ### Usage Once initialized, you can use OpenCode to: * Ask questions about your codebase * Create a plan for new features * Implement new features * Undo changes made by OpenCode * Share conversations with your team * Customize OpenCode to fit your needs | ### Key Features * **Plan Mode**: Disables OpenCode's ability to make changes and suggests how to implement a feature * **Build Mode**: Allows OpenCode to make changes to your codebase * **Undo/Redo**: Revert or reapply changes made by OpenCode * **Share**: Create a link to the current conversation and copy it to your clipboard | ### Customization To make OpenCode your own, you can: * Pick a theme * Customize keybinds * Configure code formatters * Create custom commands * Play around with the OpenCode config

Title: AI Companions with Customizable Voices
Summary: ### Introduction to Sankaku Companions Sankaku Companions is a service that allows users to create and interact with original AI characters, as well as characters from established franchises. These Companions can chat with users via text or voice, using the original voice of their character. | ### Key Features * Create custom Companions based on existing anime/game characters or entirely original characters * Fully customize Companion appearance, characteristics, personality, boundaries, and other details * Place Companions into custom scenarios and engage in group chats * Set boundaries for Companions, including "universal boundaries" that can be turned off with a premium account * Enable "emote" upgrade for role-playing style interactions | ### Interaction and Customization * Regenerate undesired responses from Companions * Edit previously sent messages to create separate conversation branches * Companions can interact in multiple languages, including French, Spanish, German, and Italian * Permanently upgrade favorite Companions' cognitive faculties to expand their memory | ### Advanced Settings * Interaction Summary: summarize recent events to help Companions remember certain events * System AI can create its own summary of events * Advanced settings include: 	+ Image Creation: allows Companions to send pictures 	+ Intelligence Enhancement: allows Companions to have larger memory for longer conversations 	+ Custom voice selection: apply a cloned voice pattern to Companions | ### Sharing and Membership * Interactions can be saved and shared as "memories" * Users can enjoy other users' publicly available memories and launch interactions based on them * Companions can be made public, private, or cloned from a public Companion * Basic features are free, but limited to a certain number of messages per day * Plus or Infinite membership required for unlimited interaction | ### Companion Management | Feature | Description | | --- | --- | | Boundary setting | Set limits for Companion behavior | | Cloning | Create a private variation of a public Companion | | Upgrades | Permanently upgrade favorite Companions' cognitive faculties | | Voice selection | Apply a custom voice to Companions | | ### Conclusion Sankaku Companions offers a unique and interactive experience, allowing users to create and customize their own AI characters. With advanced features and customization options, users can enjoy unlimited interaction with their Companions.

Title: Genspark Overview
Summary: # Genspark Overview | ## Brief Description | Genspark is an AI agent engine that enhances online information access by generating **Sparkpages**—custom, synthesized pages based on user queries in real-time. Unlike traditional search engines, Genspark consolidates knowledge from multiple reputable sources into a single, interactive page, aiming to reduce research time and eliminate commercial bias. | ## Key Features | - **Multi-Agent Framework:** Uses specialized AI agents for various queries and tasks, providing comprehensive insights. - **Real-Time Sparkpage Generation:** Creates tailored, on-demand pages that summarize, synthesize, and cite relevant information. - **Built-in AI Copilot:** Each Sparkpage includes an interactive AI assistant for follow-up questions and deeper exploration. - **Unbiased, Comprehensive Content:** Delivers informative, impartial content free from commercial influences. - **Interactive, Consolidated Outputs:** Provides concise multimedia summaries, key points, and direct answers in a structured format. - **Multi-Functional:** Capable of tasks beyond information retrieval, such as automated reporting, content generation, and app building. | ## Main Category | Genspark is categorized as an **AI-Powered Research and Information Synthesis Platform**, also described as an AI agent workspace and advanced search experience. | ## Main Use Cases | ### Target Audience | - **Researchers, Students, and Professionals:** For rapid topic onboarding, research briefs, and curated overviews. - **Content Creators and Developers:** Useful for generating blog posts, reports, slides, multimedia assets, and app or website building. - **General Knowledge Seekers:** Efficient, unbiased, and synthesized information on a wide range of topics. - **Multilingual Content Production:** Assists in generating multilingual assets, with evolving quality for nuanced tasks. | ## Table: Feature Comparison | | Feature | Description | |---------|-------------| | Multi-Agent Framework | Specialized AI agents handle different tasks and queries. | | Real-Time Sparkpage Generation | Tailored, on-demand pages summarizing and synthesizing information. | | Built-in AI Copilot | Interactive assistant for follow-up questions and deeper exploration. | | Unbiased, Comprehensive Content | Informative and impartial content free from commercial influences. | | Interactive, Consolidated Outputs | Concise multimedia summaries, key points, and direct answers. | | Multi-Functional | Capable of automated reporting, content generation, and app building. | | ## Conclusion | Genspark is designed for users who prioritize efficiency, depth, and clarity in online information discovery, offering an advanced alternative to traditional search engines and chatbots.

Title: Qwen Chat Overview
Tags: LLMs, multimodal, NLP, API Development, data-analysis
Summary: # Qwen Chat Overview | ## Brief Description | **Qwen Chat** is an official AI app developed by Alibaba. It is a multimodal assistant capable of understanding and generating text, images, and audio. The app enhances productivity by serving as a personal assistant for work, study, and daily life, featuring advanced reasoning and real-time internet data integration. Compatibility with certain systems is being actively worked on by developers[1][2][8]. | ## Key Features and Capabilities | - **Multimodal Support**: Text, images, audio, and video understanding and generation - **Multilingual Capabilities**: Natural language processing supporting 29 languages - **Extended Context Handling**: Conversations up to 128K tokens - **Data Interpretation**: Ability to interpret structured data such as tables and XML files - **AI Integration**: Includes chatbot impersonation and conditional responses - **Web Search**: Real-time web search with advanced filtering - **Content Creation**: Image and video understanding and generation - **Developer Access**: API compatible with OpenAI API standards - **Performance**: High performance validated against models like GPT-4, with superior reasoning and problem-solving skills[1][2][4][5][7] | ## Category | Artificial Intelligence / AI Chatbot / Large Language Model Assistant. | ## Main Use Cases and Target Audience | - **Individual Users**: Seeking a personal AI assistant for life management, productivity, or study support - **Developers and Businesses**: Integrating advanced AI via API for apps, chatbots, and enterprise solutions - **Knowledge Seekers**: Requiring deep question answering and emotional support - **Content Creation**: Writing, summarizing, translating, and data analysis - **Android Users**: Looking for an intelligent companion with multimodal capabilities[2][3][4][7]

Title: Qwen Image Foundation Model Project
Summary: ## Introduction Qwen-Image is a 20B MMDiT image foundation model that achieves significant advances in complex text rendering and precise image editing. It shows strong general capabilities in both image generation and editing, with exceptional performance in text rendering, especially for Chinese. | ## News * 2025.09.22: Introduced Qwen-Image-Edit-2509, the monthly iteration of Qwen-Image-Edit, with improvements including multi-image editing support and enhanced single-image consistency. * 2025.08.19: Updated to the latest diffusers commit to ensure optimal results. * 2025.08.18: Open-sourced Qwen-Image-Edit. * 2025.08.09: Added support for LoRA models, such as MajicBeauty LoRA. * 2025.08.05: Released Qwen-Image weights and technical report. | ## Quick Start To use Qwen-Image, ensure you have `transformers>=4.51.3` and install the latest version of `diffusers`. Then, use the following code snippet to generate images based on text prompts: ```python from diffusers import DiffusionPipeline import torch | model_name = "Qwen/Qwen-Image" pipe = DiffusionPipeline.from_pretrained(model_name) pipe = pipe.to("cuda") | prompt = "..." image = pipe(prompt).images[0] image.save("example.png") ``` For image editing, use the following code snippet: ```python from diffusers import QwenImageEditPipeline import torch from PIL import Image | pipeline = QwenImageEditPipeline.from_pretrained("Qwen/Qwen-Image-Edit") image = Image.open("./input.png").convert("RGB") prompt = "..." inputs = { "image": image, "prompt": prompt, "generator": torch.manual_seed(0), "true_cfg_scale": 4.0, "negative_prompt": " ", "num_inference_steps": 50, } output = pipeline(**inputs) output_image = output.images[0] output_image.save("output_image_edit.png") ``` ## Advanced Usage ### Prompt Enhance for Text-to-Image Use the official Prompt Enhancement Tool powered by Qwen-Plus to enhance prompt optimization and multi-language support. ```python from tools.prompt_utils import rewrite prompt = rewrite(prompt) ``` ### Prompt Enhance for Image Edit Use the official Prompt Enhancement Tool powered by Qwen-VL-Max to enhance stability. ```python from tools.prompt_utils import polish_edit_prompt prompt = polish_edit_prompt(prompt, pil_image) ``` ## Deploy Qwen-Image Qwen-Image supports Multi-GPU API Server for local deployment. Start the Gradio-based web interface with: ```bash export NUM_GPUS_TO_USE=4 export TASK_QUEUE_SIZE=100 export TASK_TIMEOUT=300 cd src DASHSCOPE_API_KEY=sk-xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx python examples/demo.py ``` ## Showcase Qwen-Image-Edit-2509 showcases support for multi-image inputs, enhanced consistency, and native support for ControlNet image conditions. | ### Multi-Image Input Examples include "person + person", "person + scene", and "person + object" inputs. | ### Enhanced Consistency Qwen-Image-Edit-2509 shows significant improvement over Qwen-Image-Edit in person consistency, product consistency, and text consistency. | ### ControlNet Support Native support for ControlNet image conditions, such as keypoint control and sketches. | ## AI Arena An open benchmarking platform built on the Elo rating system to comprehensively evaluate the general image generation capabilities of Qwen-Image and compare it with state-of-the-art closed-source APIs.

Title: Qwen Image Edit Introduction
Summary: ### Introduction to Qwen-Image-Edit Qwen-Image-Edit is an image editing version of Qwen-Image, built upon the 20B Qwen-Image model. It extends Qwen-Image's text rendering capabilities to image editing tasks, enabling precise text editing. The model simultaneously feeds the input image into Qwen2.5-VL (for visual semantic control) and the VAE Encoder (for visual appearance control), achieving capabilities in both semantic and appearance editing. | ### Key Features * **Semantic and Appearance Editing**: Qwen-Image-Edit supports low-level visual appearance editing and high-level visual semantic editing. * **Precise Text Editing**: Qwen-Image-Edit supports bilingual (Chinese and English) text editing, preserving the original font, size, and style. * **Strong Benchmark Performance**: Qwen-Image-Edit achieves state-of-the-art (SOTA) performance in image editing tasks. | ### Quick Start To use Qwen-Image-Edit, install the latest version of diffusers using `pip install git+https://github.com/huggingface/diffusers`. The following code snippet illustrates how to use the model: ```python import os from PIL import Image import torch from diffusers import QwenImageEditPipeline | pipeline = QwenImageEditPipeline.from_pretrained("Qwen/Qwen-Image-Edit") image = Image.open("./input.png").convert("RGB") prompt = "Change the rabbit's color to purple, with a flash light background." inputs = { "image": image, "prompt": prompt, "generator": torch.manual_seed(0), "true_cfg_scale": 4.0, "negative_prompt": " ", "num_inference_steps": 50, } with torch.inference_mode(): output = pipeline(**inputs) output_image = output.images[0] output_image.save("output_image_edit.png") ``` | ### Showcase Qwen-Image-Edit's capabilities are demonstrated through various examples, including: * **Semantic Editing**: Modifying image content while preserving the original visual semantics. * **Appearance Editing**: Keeping certain regions of the image unchanged while adding, removing, or modifying specific elements. * **Text Editing**: Accurately editing English and Chinese text in images. | ### Applications Qwen-Image-Edit has wide-ranging applications in scenarios such as: * Adjusting a person's background or changing clothing * Creating original IP content * Novel view synthesis * Style transfer | ### License Agreement Qwen-Image is licensed under Apache 2.0. | ### Citation If you find Qwen-Image-Edit useful, please cite the following work: ```markdown @misc{wu2025qwenimagetechnicalreport, title={Qwen-Image Technical Report}, author={Chenfei Wu and Jiahao Li and Jingren Zhou and Junyang Lin and Kaiyuan Gao and Kun Yan and Sheng-ming Yin and Shuai Bai and Xiao Xu and Yilei Chen and Yuxiang Chen and Zecheng Tang and Zekai Zhang and Zhengyi Wang and An Yang and Shuting Yu and Tingkun Wen and Wensen Feng and Xiaoxiao Xu and Yi Wang and Yichang Zhang and Yongqiang Zhu and Yujia Wu and Yuxuan Cai and Zenan Liu}, year={2025}, eprint={2508.02324}, archivePrefix={arXiv}, primaryClass={cs.CV} } ``` | ### Join Us If you're passionate about fundamental research, we're hiring full-time employees (FTEs) and research interns. Reach out to us at fulai.hr@alibaba-inc.com. | ### Model Statistics * Downloads last month: 182,310 * Inference Providers: fal * Model tree for Qwen/Qwen-Image-Edit: 	+ Adapters: 39 models 	+ Finetunes: 20 models 	+ Merges: 1 model 	+ Quantizations: 7 models 	+ Spaces using Qwen/Qwen-Image-Edit: 100 	+ Collection including Qwen/Qwen-Image-Edit: 100

Title: WEBWAIFU
Summary: # WEBWAIFU An interactive AI VTuber experience with VRM avatar support, real-time speech recognition, and dynamic responses. | ## Features * **VRM Avatar Support**: Load custom models, lifelike animations, and full customization * **Advanced Speech Recognition**: Whisper AI integration, optimized performance, and voice hotkeys * **Multi-Provider AI Support**: Google Gemini, OpenAI, and Ollama local AI models * **High-Quality Text-to-Speech**: Azure Neural Voices and browser TTS fallback * **Streaming & Chat Integration**: Twitch chat, stream mode, and real-time chat display * **Customization Options**: Custom backgrounds, visual effects, and real-time controls | ## Quick Start ### Prerequisites * A modern web browser (Chrome/Edge recommended) * API keys for chosen AI provider (Google Gemini or OpenAI) * Azure Cognitive Services key (optional) for premium TTS * Running instance of Ollama (optional) for local AI | ### Installation 1. Clone the repository: `git clone https://github.com/xsploit/WEBWAIFU.git` 2. Open in browser: Simply open the `index.html` file in your web browser 3. No build process or servers required - pure client-side application! | ### First-Time Setup 1. Upload VRM model: In settings panel → "VRM Model" → upload your `.vrm` file 2. Configure AI: Go to "AI Configuration" → select provider → enter API key 3. Configure voice: Set up microphone and TTS under "Voice Controls" and "Text-to-Speech" 4. Start chatting: Use text input or voice hotkey (default: Shift) to interact with your AI companion! | ## Technical Details ### Architecture | Component | Technology | | --- | --- | | Frontend | Vanilla JavaScript (ES6), HTML5, CSS3 | | 3D Rendering | Three.js with @pixiv/three-vrm library | | Speech Recognition | Xenova/transformers.js for client-side Whisper | | Audio Processing | Web Audio API for real-time microphone analysis and TTS mouth sync | | ### Performance Optimizations * Fast speech processing: 10-second Whisper chunks (3x faster than default) * Smart AI queue: Prevents API spam while maintaining responsiveness * Timeout protection: All API calls have 30-second timeouts to prevent hanging * Memory management: Proper cleanup of audio contexts and event listeners | ### Supported File Formats * VRM models: `.vrm` (VRM 0.x specification) * Backgrounds: JPG, PNG, GIF, WEBP, MP4, MOV, AVI * Audio: High-quality Azure TTS (24kHz) or browser TTS fallback | ## Troubleshooting ### Common Issues * Speech recognition not working: Check browser microphone permissions, verify correct microphone selected in settings, refresh page to reload Whisper model * VRM model not loading: Ensure valid `.vrm` file, check browser console for error messages, try smaller VRM files first * AI not responding: Verify API key is correct and active, check internet connection stability, look for timeout errors in console (should auto-retry) * Performance issues: Close other browser tabs to free memory, disable background extensions, use Chrome/Edge for best performance, check console for memory warnings | ## Repository and Acknowledgments * GitHub: https://github.com/xsploit/WEBWAIFU.git * Built upon the excellent VU-VRM project foundation by itsTallulahhh and powered by open-source libraries: Three.js, three-vrm, and Transformers.js | ## License This project is open source. See the repository for license details. | ## Future Features * Azure TTS Streaming for real-time voice synthesis * LLM Streaming for faster response generation * Enhanced VRM expression controls * More AI provider integrations

Title: Introduction to HunyuanWorld 1.0
Summary: # Introduction to HunyuanWorld 1.0 HunyuanWorld 1.0 is a novel framework for generating immersive, explorable, and interactive 3D worlds from text and image conditions. It combines the best of both video-based and 3D-based methods, providing 360° immersive experiences, mesh export capabilities, and disentangled object representations. | ## News and Updates * October 22, 2025: Release of HunyuanWorld-1.1 (WorldMirror), supporting 3D world creation from videos or multi-view images. * October 16, 2025: Proposal of FlashWorld, enabling 3DGS world generation in 5~10 seconds on a single GPU. * September 2, 2025: Release of RGB-D Video Diffusion model HunyuanWorld-Voyager, supporting 3D-consistency world exploration and fast 3D reconstruction. * August 15, 2025: Release of the quantization version of HunyuanWorld-1.0 (HunyuanWorld-1.0-lite), supporting running on consumer-grade GPUs. * July 26, 2025: Release of the first open-source, simulation-capable, immersive 3D world generation model, HunyuanWorld-1.0. | ## Architecture HunyuanWorld 1.0's generation architecture integrates panoramic proxy generation, semantic layering, and hierarchical 3D reconstruction to achieve high-quality scene-scale 360° 3D world generation, supporting both text and image inputs. | ## Performance Extensive experiments demonstrate that HunyuanWorld 1.0 achieves state-of-the-art performance in generating coherent, explorable, and interactive 3D worlds. The results are compared to other open-source panorama generation methods and 3D world generation methods. | ### Text-to-Panorama Generation | Method | BRISQUE | NIQE | Q-Align | CLIP-T | | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | | Diffusion360 | 69.5 | 7.5 | 1.8 | 20.9 | | MVDiffusion | 47.9 | 7.1 | 2.4 | 21.5 | | PanFusion | 56.6 | 7.6 | 2.2 | 21.0 | | LayerPano3D | 49.6 | 6.5 | 3.7 | 21.5 | | HunyuanWorld 1.0 | 40.8 | 5.8 | 4.4 | 24.3 | | ### Image-to-Panorama Generation | Method | BRISQUE | NIQE | Q-Align | CLIP-I | | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | | Diffusion360 | 71.4 | 7.8 | 1.9 | 73.9 | | MVDiffusion | 47.7 | 7.0 | 2.7 | 80.8 | | HunyuanWorld 1.0 | 45.2 | 5.8 | 4.3 | 85.1 | | ### Text-to-World Generation | Method | BRISQUE | NIQE | Q-Align | CLIP-T | | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | | Director3D | 49.8 | 7.5 | 3.2 | 23.5 | | LayerPano3D | 35.3 | 4.8 | 3.9 | 22.0 | | HunyuanWorld 1.0 | 34.6 | 4.3 | 4.2 | 24.0 | | ### Image-to-World Generation | Method | BRISQUE | NIQE | Q-Align | CLIP-I | | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | | WonderJourney | 51.8 | 7.3 | 3.2 | 81.5 | | DimensionX | 45.2 | 6.3 | 3.5 | 83.3 | | HunyuanWorld 1.0 | 36.2 | 4.6 | 3.9 | 84.5 | | ## Models and Checkpoints The following models are available: * HunyuanWorld-PanoDiT-Text: Text to Panorama Model * HunyuanWorld-PanoDiT-Image: Image to Panorama Model * HunyuanWorld-PanoInpaint-Scene: PanoInpaint Model for scene * HunyuanWorld-PanoInpaint-Sky: PanoInpaint Model for sky | ## Getting Started To use HunyuanWorld 1.0, follow these steps: 1. Install the required dependencies, including Python 3.10 and PyTorch 2.5.0+cu124. 2. Clone the repository and create a conda environment using the provided YAML file. 3. Install Real-ESRGAN and ZIM. 4. Login to your Hugging Face account. | ## Code Usage Example code is provided for image-to-world generation and text-to-world generation, with and without quantization and cache. | ## Quick Start A quick start script is provided in the `scripts` directory. | ## 3D World Viewer A ModelViewer tool is provided to enable quick visualization of generated 3D worlds in the web browser. | ## Open-Source Plan The inference code, model checkpoints, and technical report are available. | ## Contact For questions, please contact [tengfeiwang12@gmail.com](mailto:tengfeiwang12@gmail.com). | ## Acknowledgements The authors would like to thank the contributors to the Stable Diffusion, FLUX, diffusers, HuggingFace, Real-ESRGAN, ZIM, GroundingDINO, MoGe, Worldsheet, and WorldGen repositories.

Title: X-Omni: Reinforcement Learning for Discrete Autoregressive Image Generative Models
Summary: # X-Omni: Reinforcement Learning for Discrete Autoregressive Image Generative Models ## Overview X-Omni is a unified discrete autoregressive model for both image and language modalities. It offers superior instruction following, text rendering, and arbitrary resolution image generation capabilities. | ## Highlights * **Unified Modeling Approach**: Handles image and language modalities. * **Superior Instruction Following**: Exceptional capability to follow complex instructions. * **Superior Text Rendering**: Accurately renders text in multiple languages, including English and Chinese. * **Arbitrary Resolutions**: Produces aesthetically pleasing images at arbitrary resolutions. | ## Quick Start Guide ### Installation To install X-Omni, follow these steps: * Create a conda environment: `conda create -n xomni python==3.12` * Activate the environment: `conda activate xomni` * Install requirements: `pip install -r requirements.txt` * Install flash-attn: `pip install flash-attn --no-build-isolation` | ### Inference X-Omni supports several inference modes: 1. **Image Generation in English**: Generate images based on English prompts. 	* Example prompt: A formal letter document with a professional tone. 	* Command: `python generate.py --model_name_or_path X-Omni/X-Omni-En --flux_model_name_or_path $FLUX_PATH --prompt "$PROMPT" --image-size 1152 1152 --cfg-scale 1.0 --min-p 0.03 --seed 1234 --output-path $IMG_PATH` 2. **Image Generation in Chinese**: Generate images based on Chinese prompts. 	* Example prompt: 生成一张雪中的紫禁城全景封面图，作为北京冬季旅游指南的主题。 	* Command: `python generate.py --model_path X-Omni/X-Omni-Zh --flux_model_name_or_path $FLUX_PATH --prompt "$PROMPT" --image-size 1152 1152 --cfg-scale 1.0 --min-p 0.03 --seed 1234 --output-path $IMG_PATH` 3. **Multi-modal Chat**: Describe an image in detail. 	* Command: `python chat.py --model_name_or_path X-Omni/X-Omni-En --flux_model_name_or_path $FLUX_PATH --prompt "$PROMPT" --image-path $IMG_PATH` | ## LongText-Bench ### Installation To install the LongText-Bench environment, follow these steps: 1. Install transformers: `pip install transformers==4.52.0` 2. Install qwen_vl_utils: `pip install qwen_vl_utils` | ### Sample Results Generate images according to prompts in `text_prompts.jsonl` and `text_prompts_zh.jsonl` and save them in the following structure: ``` ├── <SAMPLE_DIR>/ │ ├── 0000_1.png │ ├── 0000_2.png │ ├── 0000_3.png │ ├── 0000_4.png │ ├── ... │ ├── 0199_1.png │ ├── 0199_2.png │ ├── 0199_3.png │ └── 0199_4.png ``` Make sure your generation results are saved in the format: `{prompt_id}_{repeat_id}.png`. | ### Evaluation Use the distributed evaluation script with torch DDP: ```bash cd textbench bash eval.sh ``` Replace `MODE` and `SAMPLE_FOLDER` according to your generation results. | ## Citation If you find this project helpful, please cite the paper: ``` @article{ geng2025xomni, author = {Zigang Geng, Yibing Wang, Yeyao Ma, Chen Li, Yongming Rao, Shuyang Gu, Zhao Zhong, Qinglin Lu, Han Hu, Xiaosong Zhang, Linus, Di Wang and Jie Jiang}, title = {X-Omni: Reinforcement Learning Makes Discrete Autoregressive Image Generative Models Great Again}, journal = {CoRR}, volume = {abs/2507.22058}, year = {2025} } ``` | ## Contact & Feedback For questions or feedback, please contact: * Yibing Wang: wangyibing18@mails.ucas.ac.cn * Tencent Hunyuan X Team If you are interested in joining the team, please contact Xiaosong Zhang.

Title: Introduction to BlenderMCP
Summary: # Introduction to BlenderMCP BlenderMCP is an integration that connects Blender to Claude AI through the Model Context Protocol (MCP), enabling prompt-assisted 3D modeling, scene creation, and manipulation. | ## Key Features * Two-way communication between Claude AI and Blender * Object manipulation: create, modify, and delete 3D objects in Blender * Material control: apply and modify materials and colors * Scene inspection: get detailed information about the current Blender scene * Code execution: run arbitrary Python code in Blender from Claude | ## Components The system consists of two main components: * Blender Addon (addon.py): a Blender addon that creates a socket server within Blender to receive and execute commands * MCP Server (src/blender_mcp/server.py): a Python server that implements the Model Context Protocol and connects to the Blender addon | ## Installation ### Prerequisites * Blender 3.0 or newer * Python 3.10 or newer * uv package manager (install instructions: 	+ Mac: `brew install uv` 	+ Windows: `powershell -c "irm https://astral.sh/uv/install.ps1 | iex"` and then set the Path environment variable) | ### Environment Variables The following environment variables can be used to configure the Blender connection: * `BLENDER_HOST`: Host address for Blender socket server (default: "localhost") * `BLENDER_PORT`: Port number for Blender socket server (default: 9876) | ## Integration with Claude ### Setup 1. Install uv 2. Watch the setup instruction video 3. Configure Claude by adding the following to `claude_desktop_config.json`: ```json { "mcpServers": { "blender": { "command": "uvx", "args": ["blender-mcp"] } } } ``` | ## Integration with Visual Studio Code ### Prerequisites * Visual Studio Code installed | ### Installing the Blender Addon 1. Download the `addon.py` file from this repository 2. Open Blender 3. Go to Edit > Preferences > Add-ons 4. Click "Install..." and select the `addon.py` file 5. Enable the addon by checking the box next to "Interface: Blender MCP" | ## Usage ### Starting the Connection 1. In Blender, go to the 3D View sidebar (press N if not visible) 2. Find the "BlenderMCP" tab 3. Turn on the Poly Haven checkbox if you want assets from their API (optional) 4. Click "Connect to Claude" 5. Make sure the MCP server is running in your terminal | ### Capabilities * Get scene and object information * Create, delete, and modify shapes * Apply or create materials for objects * Execute any Python code in Blender * Download models, assets, and HDRIs through Poly Haven * Generate 3D models through Hyper3D Rodin | ## Example Commands Some examples of what you can ask Claude to do: * "Create a low poly scene in a dungeon, with a dragon guarding a pot of gold" * "Create a beach vibe using HDRIs, textures, and models like rocks and vegetation from Poly Haven" * "Generate a 3D model of a garden gnome through Hyper3D" | ## Troubleshooting * Connection issues: make sure the Blender addon server is running, and the MCP server is configured on Claude * Timeout errors: try simplifying your requests or breaking them into smaller steps * Poly Haven integration: Claude is sometimes erratic with its behavior; try restarting both Claude and the Blender server | ## Technical Details The system uses a simple JSON-based protocol over TCP sockets: * Commands are sent as JSON objects with a `type` and optional `params` * Responses are JSON objects with a `status` and `result` or `message` | ## Limitations and Security Considerations * The `execute_blender_code` tool allows running arbitrary Python code in Blender, which can be powerful but potentially dangerous; use with caution in production environments * Poly Haven requires downloading models, textures, and HDRI images; if you do not want to use it, please turn it off in the checkbox in Blender * Complex operations might need to be broken down into smaller steps | ## Contributing Contributions are welcome! Please feel free to submit a Pull Request.
