Title: Overview
Summary: # Overview opcode is a powerful desktop application for interacting with Claude Code, providing a GUI for managing sessions, creating custom agents, and more. | ## Features * Project & Session Management: Visual project browser, session history, smart search, and session insights * CC Agents: Custom AI agents, agent library, background execution, and execution history * Usage Analytics Dashboard: Cost tracking, token analytics, visual charts, and export data * MCP Server Management: Server registry, easy configuration, connection testing, and Claude Desktop import * Timeline & Checkpoints: Session versioning, visual timeline, instant restore, fork sessions, and diff viewer * CLAUDE.md Management: Built-in editor, live preview, project scanner, and syntax highlighting | ## Usage ### Getting Started 1. Launch opcode and choose between CC Agents or Projects 2. First-time setup: opcode detects your `~/.claude` directory ### Managing Projects 1. Select a project to view its sessions 2. Resume sessions directly or start new ones ### Creating Agents 1. Design your agent: set name, icon, and system prompt 2. Configure model: choose between available Claude models 3. Set permissions: configure file read/write and network access 4. Execute tasks: run your agent on any project ### Tracking Usage 1. View analytics: monitor costs by model, project, and date 2. Export data for reports 3. Set up usage alerts (coming soon) ### Working with MCP Servers 1. Add servers manually or via JSON 2. Import from Claude Desktop configuration 3. Test connections before using | ## Installation ### Prerequisites * Claude Code CLI: install from Claude's official site * Release executables will be published soon ### Build from Source #### System Requirements | Requirement | Minimum | | --- | --- | | Operating System | Windows 10/11, macOS 11+, or Linux (Ubuntu 20.04+) | | RAM | 4GB (8GB recommended) | | Storage | 1GB free space | #### Required Tools * Rust (1.70.0 or later) * Bun (latest version) * Git * Claude Code CLI #### Platform-Specific Dependencies * Linux (Ubuntu/Debian): install system dependencies * macOS: install Xcode Command Line Tools * Windows: install Microsoft C++ Build Tools and WebView2 #### Build Steps 1. Clone the repository: `git clone https://github.com/getAsterisk/opcode.git` 2. Install frontend dependencies: `bun install` 3. Build the application: `bun run tauri build` #### Troubleshooting * "cargo not found" error: ensure Rust is installed and `~/.cargo/bin` is in your PATH * Linux: "webkit2gtk not found" error: install the webkit2gtk development packages * Windows: "MSVC not found" error: install Visual Studio Build Tools with C++ support | ## Development ### Tech Stack * Frontend: React 18 + TypeScript + Vite 6 * Backend: Rust with Tauri 2 * UI Framework: Tailwind CSS v4 + shadcn/ui * Database: SQLite (via rusqlite) * Package Manager: Bun ### Project Structure * `opcode/`: React frontend 	+ `components/`: UI components 	+ `lib/`: API client & utilities 	+ `assets/`: Static assets * `src-tauri/`: Rust backend 	+ `src/`: Tauri command handlers, timeline management, and process management 	+ `tests/`: Rust test suite * `public/`: Public assets ### Development Commands * Start development server: `bun run tauri dev` * Run frontend only: `bun run dev` * Type checking: `bunx tsc --noEmit` * Run Rust tests: `cd src-tauri && cargo test` * Format code: `cd src-tauri && cargo fmt` | ## Security * Process Isolation: agents run in separate processes * Permission Control: configure file and network access per agent * Local Storage: all data stays on your machine * No Telemetry: no data collection or tracking * Open Source: full transparency through open source code | ## Contributing We welcome contributions! Please see our [Contributing Guide](#) for details. ### Areas for Contribution * Bug fixes and improvements * New features and enhancements * Documentation improvements * UI/UX enhancements * Test coverage * Internationalization | ## License This project is licensed under the AGPL License - see the [LICENSE](#) file for details. | ## Acknowledgments Built with [Tauri](#) - The secure framework for building desktop apps Claude by Anthropic Made with ❤️ by the Asterisk

Title: Introduction to Claude Manager
Summary: # Introduction to Claude Manager Claude Manager is a real-time web dashboard for managing Claude Code across multiple projects. It provides centralized control over MCP servers, automated hooks, session tracking, and configuration management with live WebSocket updates. | ## What is Claude Manager? Claude Manager is a dual-service web application that provides unified management for Claude Code environments. It offers a centralized dashboard with the following features: * Real-time Project Registry * AI-Powered Agent & Slash Command Generation * MCP Server Management * Automated Hook System * Session Tracking & Usage Monitoring * Dual-Scope Environment Management * Live CLAUDE.md Editor * WebSocket Real-time Updates | ## Quick Start ### Prerequisites & Installation To install Claude Manager, ensure you have the following system requirements: * Bun (JavaScript runtime and package manager) * Node.js 20+ (required for the application runtime) * Claude Code CLI (required for MCP server management) | ### Installation Steps 1. Clone the repository and install dependencies: `git clone https://github.com/callmebeachy/claude-manager.git`, `cd claude-manager`, `bun install` 2. Install global project registration commands: `./install.sh && source ~/.zshrc` 3. Configure OpenRouter API key for AI features (optional): `mkdir -p ~/.claude-manager`, `echo "OPENROUTER_API_KEY=your_key_here" >> ~/.claude-manager/user.env` | ### Development Startup To start the application, use the following commands: * One-Command Start: `bun run dev` * Manual Service Control: 	+ Backend only: `cd backend && bun --watch index.js` 	+ Frontend only: `cd frontend && PORT=3456 BROWSER=none bun start` | ## Access Points The following access points are available: * Development Dashboard: `http://localhost:3456` * Backend API: `http://localhost:3455/api/` * Production Dashboard: `http://localhost:3455` (after running `bun run build`) | ## Essential Commands The following essential commands are available: * `bun run build`: Build production frontend bundle * `bun run test`: Run TypeScript/React tests * `bun run typecheck`: TypeScript error checking * `bun run claude:register`: Register current directory as project * `./install.sh`: Install cm-reg/cm-unreg global commands | ## Core Features ### Project Management * Register projects from anywhere and manage all Claude configurations centrally * Features: 	+ Live monitoring of `.claude/settings.json` changes via WebSocket 	+ Real-time CLAUDE.md file editing with markdown preview 	+ Project-specific environment variable management 	+ Dedicated MCP server configurations per project | ### AI-Powered Agent & Slash Command Creation * Generate professional Claude Code configurations using natural language * Features: 	+ Agent Creator: Describe your agent's purpose and behavior in plain English 	+ Slash Command Builder: Natural language description converts to professional slash commands | ### MCP Server Management * Manage Model Context Protocol servers with templates and AI discovery * Built-in Templates: 	+ Supabase 	+ Neo4j 	+ Playwright/Puppeteer 	+ GitHub 	+ PostgreSQL 	+ Notion 	+ Figma * AI-Powered Discovery: Describe what you need and AI finds relevant MCP servers | ### Automated Hook System * Set up workflows that trigger automatically on Claude actions * Features: 	+ Safety & Security: Block dangerous commands, prevent API key exposure, validate file permissions and paths 	+ Code Quality & Formatting: Auto-format with Black, Prettier, Go fmt, Ruff, run linters, execute tests 	+ Git Integration: Auto-stage modified files, run pre-commit hooks and validation, push changes to remote repositories 	+ Custom Integrations: TTS announcements, Slack/Discord notifications, file backup and synchronization | ### Session Tracking & Usage Monitoring * Monitor your Claude Code usage in real-time * Features: 	+ Live Session Countdown: See exact time remaining in your current 5-hour session 	+ Plan Limit Tracking: Automatic detection of Pro (45), Max-5x (225), Max-20x (900) plans 	+ Monthly Usage Analytics: Historical usage with configurable billing periods | ### Environment & Configuration Management * Centralized API key and configuration management across all projects * Two-Tier Variable System: 	+ User-Level: Global API keys shared across all projects 	+ Project-Level: Project-specific configuration and overrides * Dashboard Features: 	+ Masked Display: Sensitive values shown as `abc...xyz` for security 	+ Copy Between Scopes: Move variables from user to project level and vice versa 	+ Real-time Validation: Immediate feedback on variable format and requirements | ## Troubleshooting * Dashboard not loading: Ensure port 3455 is available, check `server.log` for errors * Projects not registering: Verify `.claude/` directory exists in project, check file permissions on config directories * MCP servers not working: Ensure Claude Code is installed and in PATH, check environment variables are correctly set, verify MCP server packages are available * Session tracking not updating: Ensure JSONL files are in the default location | ## Context-Aware Status Line The context-aware status line script provides real-time context usage information using the same calculation method as Claude Code's `/context` command. * Features: 	+ Accurate Token Calculation: Uses the same methodology as `/context` by analyzing JSONL session files 	+ Smart Emoji Indicators: Shows what's consuming the most context space 	+ Color-Coded Usage: Green (0-25%), Yellow (26-75%), Red (76-99%) 	+ Dashboard Integration: Shows Claude Manager URL for quick access | ### Installation To install the context-aware status line script, follow these steps: 1. Copy the script to your Claude config: `cp statusline-context-aware.sh ~/.claude/statusline.sh` 2. Make it executable: `chmod +x ~/.claude/statusline.sh` 3. Add to your `~/.claude/settings.json`: `"statusLine": { "type": "command", "command": "~/.claude/statusline.sh" }` | ### Output Format The status line updates automatically as your context usage changes and will accurately reflect token consumption patterns shown in `/context`. Title: Building Fast AI Voice Agents
Summary: ### Introduction to Building Lightning-Fast AI Voice Agents Building effective AI voice agents requires achieving response times that feel natural to the user. A response time of 1200ms marks the threshold between a natural conversation and one that feels painfully slow. | ### Components That Affect Speed The following components can be controlled by developers to optimize the speed of AI voice agents: * **Speech to Text (STT)**: Choose a model with the quickest and most accurate transcription speed that supports real-time conversation with streaming. * **Turn Taking**: Decide between punctuation/VAD-based approaches and trained models, considering trade-offs between speed and accuracy. * **Large Language Model (LLM)**: Optimize for time to first token, as this is the most critical metric for conversational flow. * **Text to Speech (TTS)**: Select a model with quick generation speed while maintaining quality and realism, built for real-time conversation. * **Telephony**: Choose providers with the lowest latency to minimize added latency compared to web-based solutions. | ### Speech to Text (STT) Models The following table compares popular STT models: | Provider | Model | Speed | Accuracy | Notes | | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | | Deepgram | Nova 3 | Very Fast | High | Excellent balance, good pricing | | Cartesia | Sonic Whisper | Very Fast | High | OpenAI Whisper optimized for real-time | | Assembly AI | Universal Streaming | Fast | High | Built specifically for AI voice agents | | ### Turn Taking Approaches The following table compares turn taking approaches: | Approach | Speed | Accuracy | Best For | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | Punctuation/VAD-based | Fastest | Good | Maximum speed priority | | LiveKit (Aggressive) | Fast | Very Good | Balanced approach | | Pipecat Model | Medium | Very Good | Balanced approach | | ### Large Language Models (LLMs) The following table compares popular LLMs: | Provider | Model | Time to First Token | Availability | Notes | | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | | Cerebras | Qwen3 235B 2507 | ~250ms | Limited quota | Crazy fast tokens/second | | Groq | Kimi K2 | ~210ms | Good limits | Excellent balance | | Groq | Llama 3.3 70B | ~150ms | Wide availability | Reliable fallback | | Vapi/OpenAI | GPT-4.0/4.1 | ~400ms | Vapi only | 1000ms latency reduction | | ### Text to Speech (TTS) Models The following table compares popular TTS models: | Provider | Model | Speed | Quality | Pricing | | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | | Rime AI | Arcana/Mist V2 | Very Fast | Excellent | Mid-range | | ElevenLabs | Flash/Turbo | Fast | Very Good | Higher | | Cartesia | Sonic 2.0 | Very Fast | Good | Mid-range | | MiniMax | Speech 02 Turbo | Fast | Excellent | Mid-range | | Vapi | Native Model | Very Fast | Good | Good value | | ### Telephony Providers The following table compares telephony providers: | Provider | Added Latency | Quality | Notes | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | Telnyx | ~300ms | Excellent | Taking AI voice seriously | | Vonage | ~400ms | Good | Better routing than Twilio | | Twilio | ~600ms | Good | Slowest due to infrastructure load | | ### Real-World Performance Comparison A comparison of two configurations demonstrates the impact of component choice on response latency: * **Slow Configuration**: LLM (Claude Sonnet 4), STT (Elevenlabs Scribe), TTS (Vapi's own), Endpointing (LiveKit default) - Result: 3-5 second response latency * **Fast Configuration**: LLM (Llama 3.3 70B), STT (Deepgram Nova 3), TTS (Vapi's own), Endpointing (VAD-based) - Result: Sub-1200ms response latency consistently | ### Key Takeaways * Most platforms have optimized the voice AI pipeline well; the differentiator is component choice. * Open-source models with specialized inference providers often beat closed-source models for speed. * Sub-1200ms latency is achievable with proper component selection. * Speed is as important as intelligence; a 2-3 second delay makes even the smartest agent unusable.

Title: Char Card Reader
Summary: # Char Card Reader A lightweight library for reading SillyTavern character card metadata from image files (PNG, JPEG, WEBP) and JSON without external dependencies. | ## Features * Supports character card specifications v1, v2, and v3 * Extracts metadata from PNG, JPEG, WEBP images and JSON data * Provides conversion between different spec versions * Zero external dependencies * Works in both Node.js and browser environments | ## Installation To install the library, use one of the following commands: * `npm install @lenml/char-card-reader` * `yarn add @lenml/char-card-reader` * `pnpm install @lenml/char-card-reader` | ## Usage ### Basic Usage (Node.js) ```javascript import { CharacterCard } from '@lenml/char-card-reader'; import fs from 'fs'; | (async () => { // Load from image file const file = fs.readFileSync('./path/to/character.png'); const card = await CharacterCard.from_file(file); | // Or load from JSON data const jsonData = { /* character data, support v1/v2/v3 format */ }; const jsonCard = CharacterCard.from_json(jsonData); | // Access card properties console.log("Character Name:", card.name); console.log("Description:", card.description); console.log("First Message:", card.first_message); | // Convert between specs const v1Data = card.toSpecV1(); const v2Data = card.toSpecV2(); const v3Data = card.toSpecV3(); })(); ``` | ### Browser Usage ```javascript <script type="module"> import { CharacterCard } from 'https://esm.run/@lenml/char-card-reader'; | const fileInput = document.getElementById('character-file'); fileInput.addEventListener('change', async (e) => { const file = e.target.files[0]; if (!file) return; | // Load from image file if (file.type.startsWith('image/')) { const arrayBuffer = await file.arrayBuffer(); const card = await CharacterCard.from_file(arrayBuffer); console.log("Character Info:", card.toSpecV3()); } // Or load from JSON file else if (file.type === 'application/json') { const jsonText = await file.text(); const jsonData = JSON.parse(jsonText); const card = CharacterCard.from_json(jsonData); console.log("Character Info:", card.toSpecV3()); } }); </script> ``` | ## API ### CharacterCard Class #### Static Methods | Method | Description | | --- | --- | | `from_file(file: ArrayBuffer | Uint8Array): Promise<CharacterCard>` | Creates a CharacterCard instance from a file | | `from_json(raw_data: CharRawData, fallback_avatar = ""): CharacterCard` | Creates a CharacterCard instance from JSON data | | #### Instance Properties * `avatar`: Character avatar URL * `name`: Character name * `description`: Character description * `first_message`: First message/opening line * `personality`: Personality description * `scenario`: Scenario context * `alternate_greetings`: Array of alternate greetings * `tags`: Array of character tags * And more... | #### Conversion Methods | Method | Description | | --- | --- | | `toSpecV1()` | Converts to v1 spec format | | `toSpecV2()` | Converts to v2 spec format | | `toSpecV3()` | Converts to v3 spec format | | ## CharacterBook (Lorebook) ### From Character Card ```javascript import { CharacterCard } from '@lenml/char-card-reader'; const file = fs.readFileSync('./path/to/character.png'); const card = await CharacterCard.from_file(file); const book = card.get_book(); const entries = book.scan(card.first_message); ``` | ### From JSON Data ```javascript import { CharacterBook } from '@lenml/char-card-reader'; const json_data = { entries: [/*...*/] }; const book = CharacterBook.from_json(json_data); const entries = book.scan("this xxoo world."); ``` | ## Document [Read generated document](#) | ## Specifications References * [Spec v1](#) * [Spec v2](#) * [Spec v3](#) | ## License AGPL-3.0

Title: Postgres MCP Pro
Summary: # Postgres MCP Pro Postgres MCP Pro is an open-source Model Context Protocol (MCP) server built to support developers and AI agents throughout the entire development process. | ## Overview Postgres MCP Pro offers the following features: * Database health analysis (index health, connection utilization, buffer cache, vacuum health, sequence limits, replication lag, etc.) * Index tuning to find the best solution for your workload * Query plan validation and optimization * Schema intelligence for context-aware SQL generation * Safe SQL execution with configurable access control | ## Demo The demo showcases how Postgres MCP Pro can improve the performance of a movie app generated by an AI assistant. With Postgres MCP Pro, performance issues were fixed in minutes, including: * Fixing ORM queries, indexing, and caching * Fixing a broken page by exploring the data and adding related content * Improving the top movies by exploring the data and fixing the ORM query | ## Quick Start ### Prerequisites Before getting started, ensure you have: * Access credentials for your database * Docker or Python 3.12 or higher | ### Installation Choose one of the following methods to install Postgres MCP Pro: * Using Docker: `docker pull crystaldba/postgres-mcp` * Using Python: `pipx install postgres-mcp` or `uv pip install postgres-mcp` | ### Configure Your AI Assistant Edit the configuration file to add Postgres MCP Pro. For Claude Desktop, update the `mcpServers` section: ```json { "mcpServers": { "postgres": { "command": "docker", "args": [ "run", "-i", "--rm", "-e", "DATABASE_URI", "crystaldba/postgres-mcp", "--access-mode=unrestricted" ], "env": { "DATABASE_URI": "postgresql://username:password@localhost:5432/dbname" } } } } ``` Replace `postgresql://...` with your Postgres database connection URI. | ## Access Modes Postgres MCP Pro supports multiple access modes: * Unrestricted Mode: allows full read/write access * Restricted Mode: limits operations to read-only transactions | ## SSE Transport Postgres MCP Pro supports the SSE transport, which allows multiple MCP clients to share one server. To use the SSE transport, start the server with the `--transport=sse` option. | ## Postgres Extension Installation (Optional) To enable index tuning and comprehensive performance analysis, load the `pg_statements` and `hypopg` extensions on your database. | ## Usage Examples * Get database health overview: Ask "Check the health of my database and identify any issues." * Analyze slow queries: Ask "What are the slowest queries in my database? And how can I speed them up?" * Get recommendations on how to speed things up: Ask "My app is slow. How can I make it faster?" * Generate index recommendations: Ask "Analyze my database workload and suggest indexes to improve performance." | ## MCP Server API The MCP standard defines various types of endpoints. Postgres MCP Pro provides functionality via MCP tools alone. | ### Postgres MCP Pro Tools | Tool Name | Description | | --- | --- | | list_schemas | Lists all database schemas available in the PostgreSQL instance. | | list_objects | Lists database objects (tables, views, sequences, extensions) within a specified schema. | | get_object_details | Provides information about a specific database object. | | execute_sql | Executes SQL statements on the database, with read-only limitations when connected in restricted mode. | | explain_query | Gets the execution plan for a SQL query. | | get_top_queries | Reports the slowest SQL queries based on total execution time. | | analyze_workload_indexes | Analyzes the database workload to identify resource-intensive queries and recommends optimal indexes. | | analyze_query_indexes | Analyzes a list of specific SQL queries and recommends optimal indexes. | | analyze_db_health | Performs comprehensive health checks. | | ## Related Projects Postgres MCP Servers, Que, and other related projects are available. | ## Index Tuning Postgres MCP Pro uses a greedy search strategy to find the optimal index configuration. The search strategy is critical because evaluating all possible index combinations is feasible only in simple situations. | ### Cost-Benefit Analysis When posed with two indexing alternatives, one which produces better performance and one which requires more space, Postgres MCP Pro performs a cost-benefit analysis to decide which to choose. The analysis is based on a configurable parameter that sets the threshold for the change in relative terms.

Title: Guitars4Vets Find-A-Chapter Program
Summary: # Guitars4Vets Find-A-Chapter Program | ## Program Overview Guitars4Vets Find-A-Chapter is a specialized tool designed to help military veterans locate nearby chapters offering free, personalized guitar lessons. These lessons are crafted to support healing from physical injuries, PTSD, and emotional trauma through the therapeutic power of music. | ## Key Features | - **Local Chapter Location:** Utilizes a map and directory to assist veterans in finding nearby Guitars 4 Vets chapters. - **Instruction Formats:** * In-person lessons at local chapters * Online lessons via the Cyber Chapter - **Support Services:** * Customized lesson plans for veterans with physical and emotional challenges * Opportunities for peer connection through communal jam sessions - **Graduation Benefits:** * Veterans receive a new Yamaha guitar and starter kit upon completion of the program | ## Eligibility and Benefits | | Category | Details | |----------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------| | **In-Person Lessons** | Available within a 40-mile radius of a chapter | | **Online Lessons** | Offered through the Cyber Chapter for veterans living beyond 40 miles from a local chapter | | **Lesson Duration** | 10 weeks of one-on-one instruction | | **Equipment** | A free guitar and gear kit upon completing the program | | ## Main Category | The Guitars4Vets program falls under the category of veteran support and rehabilitation through music therapy and education, operating as a nonprofit social service program. | ## Target Audience | - **Veterans with Health Challenges:** Those dealing with PTSD, physical injuries, and emotional difficulties. - **Aspiring Musicians:** Veterans interested in learning the guitar, whether they are near a physical chapter or require virtual lessons. - **Community Seekers:** Veterans looking to connect with peers and gain support through music and group activities.

Title: Introduction to Live API
Summary: # Introduction to Live API The Live API enables low-latency, real-time voice and video interactions with Gemini. It processes continuous streams of audio, video, or text to deliver immediate, human-like spoken responses. | ## Key Features * Voice Activity Detection * Tool use and function calling * Session management for managing long running conversations * Ephemeral tokens for secure client-sided authentication | ## Getting Started To get started with the Live API, you need to make two important decisions: 1. **Choose a model**: Select a model that determines the audio generation architecture used to create the audio response. 2. **Choose an implementation approach**: Decide on either a server-to-server or client-to-server implementation approach. | ### Choosing a Model The following models are available: | Model | Description | Supported Audio Generation Architecture | | --- | --- | --- | | gemini-2.5-flash-native-audio-preview-09-2025 | Native audio model | Native audio | | gemini-live-2.5-flash-preview | Half-cascade audio model | Half-cascade audio | | gemini-2.0-flash-live-001 | Half-cascade audio model | Half-cascade audio | | ### Choosing an Implementation Approach The following implementation approaches are available: * **Server-to-server**: Your backend connects to the Live API using WebSockets. * **Client-to-server**: Your frontend code connects directly to the Live API using WebSockets. | ## Example Applications The following example applications demonstrate how to use the Live API: * Live audio starter app on AI Studio * Live API Python cookbook using Pyaudio | ## Partner Integrations The following partner platforms have integrated the Gemini Live API: * Daily * LiveKit * Voximplant | ## Code Samples ### Python ```python import asyncio import io from pathlib import Path import wave from google import genai from google.genai import types import soundfile as sf import librosa | # New native audio model model = "gemini-2.5-flash-native-audio-preview-09-2025" config = { "response_modalities": ["AUDIO"], "system_instruction": "You are a helpful assistant and answer in a friendly tone." } | async def main(): async with genai.Client().aio.live.connect(model=model, config=config) as session: # Send audio chunk buffer = io.BytesIO() y, sr = librosa.load("sample.wav", sr=16000) sf.write(buffer, y, sr, format='RAW', subtype='PCM_16') buffer.seek(0) audio_bytes = buffer.read() await session.send_realtime_input(audio=types.Blob(data=audio_bytes, mime_type="audio/pcm;rate=16000")) | # Receive audio response wf = wave.open("audio.wav", "wb") wf.setnchannels(1) wf.setsampwidth(2) wf.setframerate(24000) async for response in session.receive(): if response.data is not None: wf.writeframes(response.data) wf.close() | if __name__ == "__main__": asyncio.run(main()) ``` | ### JavaScript ```javascript import { GoogleGenAI, Modality } from '@google/genai'; import * as fs from 'node:fs'; import pkg from 'wavefile'; | const { WaveFile } = pkg; | const ai = new GoogleGenAI({}); const model = "gemini-2.5-flash-native-audio-preview-09-2025"; const config = { responseModalities: [Modality.AUDIO], systemInstruction: "You are a helpful assistant and answer in a friendly tone." }; | async function live() { const responseQueue = []; async function waitMessage() { let done = false; let message = undefined; while (!done) { message = responseQueue.shift(); if (message) { done = true; } else { await new Promise((resolve) => setTimeout(resolve, 100)); } } return message; } | async function handleTurn() { const turns = []; let done = false; while (!done) { const message = await waitMessage(); turns.push(message); if (message.serverContent && message.serverContent.turnComplete) { done = true; } } return turns; } | const session = await ai.live.connect({ model: model, callbacks: { onopen: function () { console.debug('Opened'); }, onmessage: function (message) { responseQueue.push(message); }, onerror: function (e) { console.debug('Error:', e.message); }, onclose: function (e) { console.debug('Close:', e.reason); }, }, config: config, }); | // Send audio chunk const fileBuffer = fs.readFileSync("sample.wav"); const wav = new WaveFile(); wav.fromBuffer(fileBuffer); wav.toSampleRate(16000); wav.toBitDepth("16"); const base64Audio = wav.toBase64(); session.sendRealtimeInput({ audio: { data: base64Audio, mimeType: "audio/pcm;rate=16000" } }); | const turns = await handleTurn(); // Combine audio data strings and save as wave file const combinedAudio = turns.reduce((acc, turn) => { if (turn.data) { const buffer = Buffer.from(turn.data, 'base64'); const intArray = new Int16Array(buffer.buffer, buffer.byteOffset, buffer.byteLength / Int16Array.BYTES_PER_ELEMENT); return acc.concat(Array.from(intArray)); } return acc; }, []); const audioBuffer = new Int16Array(combinedAudio); const wf = new WaveFile(); wf.fromScratch(1, 24000, '16', audioBuffer); fs.writeFileSync('audio.wav', wf.toBuffer()); | session.close(); } | async function main() { await live().catch((e) => console.error('got error', e)); } main(); ``` | ## What's Next * Read the full [Live API Capabilities](https://ai.google.dev/gemini-api/docs/live/capabilities) guide for key capabilities and configurations. * Read the [Tool use](https://ai.google.dev/gemini-api/docs/live/tool-use) guide to learn how to integrate Live API with tools and function calling. * Read the [Session management](https://ai.google.dev/gemini-api/docs/live/session-management) guide for managing long running conversations. * Read the [Ephemeral tokens](https://ai.google.dev/gemini-api/docs/live/ephemeral-tokens) guide for secure authentication in client-to-server applications. * For more information about the underlying WebSockets API, see the [WebSockets API reference](https://ai.google.dev/gemini-api/docs/live/websockets-api).

Title: Introduction to Mesh2Motion
Summary: # Introduction to Mesh2Motion Mesh2Motion is an open-source application that allows users to import 3D models and automatically assign and export animations. | ## Key Features * Import 3D models in GLB/GLTF format * Assign and export animations * Support for various skeleton types * Open-source, allowing for expansion and evolution | ## Usage To use Mesh2Motion, follow these steps: * Import a 3D model * Pick a skeleton type * Modify the skeleton to fit the model (optional) * Test various animations * Select and export desired animations (currently only GLB/GLTF format) | ## Building and Running Locally To run Mesh2Motion locally, ensure you have Node.js installed (version 22.18.0 or later). Then, follow these steps: * Open a command line tool in the project directory * Run `npm install` * Run `npm run dev` to start the web server | ## Creating a Production Build To create a production build, use the following command: * `npm run build` (uses Vite for the web server and builder) | ## Running in Docker To run Mesh2Motion in Docker, ensure you have Docker and Docker Compose installed. Then, follow these steps: * Navigate to the project directory * Run `docker-compose up -d` * Visit `http://localhost:3000` to try it out | ## Video Previews A separate tool is available for generating video previews for each animation. See the [Preview Generator Documentation](#preview-generator-documentation) for more information. | ## Contributing ### Contributor Guide Contributions are welcome, especially from animators. The current animation library is limited, and additional animations are needed. ### Contribute to the Animation Fund Donations are accepted to fund the creation of new animations. This will allow the hiring of professional animators to expand the animation library. | ## Technical Details The following table summarizes the technical dependencies: | Dependency | Version | | --- | --- | | Node.js | 22.18.0 or later | | Vite | (used for web server and builder) | | Note: For more information, visit the [Mesh2Motion website](https://mesh2motion.org/) or try it live at [https://app.mesh2motion.org/](https://app.mesh2motion.org/).

Title: Texas State Benefits for 100% Disabled Veterans
Summary: # Texas State Benefits for 100% Disabled Veterans | ## Overview | The vadisabilitygroup provides a resource outlining state-level benefits available to Texas veterans with a 100% service-connected VA disability rating, including those deemed unemployable due to their disability. | ## Key Benefits | ### Property Tax Exemptions - **Total Exemption:** Available for veterans who own their homestead. - **Eligibility:** Must have a 100% service-connected disability or be totally disabled. | ### Driver’s Licenses - **Free Licenses:** Issued to qualifying veterans. - **Renewal:** Typically, there is no fee for renewal. | ### Disability-Based Exemptions - **Percentage-Based:** Varies according to the degree of disability. - **Application:** Requires a VA disability letter as proof. | ## Application Process | ### Documentations Required - VA disability letter - Discharge papers (DD-214) - Proof of residency - Property tax statement (if applicable) | ### Steps to Apply 1. **Contact Local Appraisal District:** Inquire about specific benefits and eligibility. 2. **Submit Required Forms:** Complete application along with necessary documentation. 3. **Confirmation:** Wait for confirmation from the local office. | ## Additional Considerations | ### County Variability - Some benefits and application processes may differ by county. - Veterans should verify details with their local appraisal district. | ### Surviving Spouses - **Eligibility:** May qualify if they remain unmarried and reside in the homestead. - **Benefits:** Similar to those available to the veteran. | ## Table: Key Benefits and Requirements | | Benefit | Eligibility | Documentation Required | |------------------------------------------|---------------------------------|--------------------------------------| | Property Tax Exemption | 100% VA Disability | VA disability letter, DD-214 | | Free Driver’s License | 100% VA Disability | VA disability letter, ID proof | | Disability-Based Exemptions | Varies by disability percentage | VA disability letter, residence proof| | Survivors’ Benefits | Remaining spouse | Death certificate, marriage certificate| | ## Target Audience | - **100% Disabled Veterans:** Those with a 100% service-connected disability or deemed unemployable. - **Surviving Spouses:** Those who meet state residency and marital status requirements. - **Information Seekers:** Veterans or families looking for clear guidance on Texas state benefits.

Title: NEO Home Automation Robot

It offers a range of features, including assistance with everyday tasks, automation of household chores, and helpful intelligence. | ### Key Features * Provides assistance with everyday tasks * and safe interactions | ### Design and Safety NEO is designed with safety and comfort in mind, featuring: * A lightweight and quiet operation * A deformable 3D lattice that wraps internal components
